text: dr. goldberg offers everything i look for in a general practitioner.  he's nice and easy to talk to without being patronizing; he's always on time in seeing his patients; he's affiliated with a top-notch hospital (nyu) which my parents have explained to me is very important in case something happens and you need surgery; and you can get referrals to see specialists without having to see him first.  really, what more do you need?  i'm sitting here trying to think of any complaints i have about him, but i'm really drawing a blank.
paraphrase: Dr. Goldberg possesses all the qualities I seek in a general practitioner. He's friendly and approachable without being condescending, consistently punctual with his appointments, and connected to a prestigious hospital (NYU), which my parents have emphasized as crucial if surgery is ever needed. Plus, referrals to specialists are accessible without requiring an initial visit with him. Honestly, what more could you ask for? As I sit here, I can't think of any criticisms about him; I'm truly at a loss.

text: Unfortunately, the frustration of being Dr. Goldberg's patient is a repeat of the experience I've had with so many other doctors in NYC -- good doctor, terrible staff.  It seems that his staff simply never answers the phone.  It usually takes 2 hours of repeated calling to get an answer.  Who has time for that or wants to deal with it?  I have run into this problem with many other doctors and I just don't get it.  You have office workers, you have patients with medical needs, why isn't anyone answering the phone?  It's incomprehensible and not work the aggravation.  It's with regret that I feel that I have to give Dr. Goldberg 2 stars.
paraphrase: Regrettably, being a patient of Dr. Goldberg is just as frustrating as my experiences with numerous other doctors in NYC—excellent doctor, but the staff is terrible. His team seems to never pick up the phone. It usually takes about 2 hours of persistent calling before someone finally answers. Who has the time or the patience for that? I've encountered this issue with many other doctors, and I simply don't understand it. You have staff, you have patients in need, so why isn't anyone picking up the phone? It's baffling and not worth the hassle. It's with a heavy heart that I feel compelled to rate Dr. Goldberg 2 stars.

text: Been going to Dr. Goldberg for over 10 years. I think I was one of his 1st patients when he started at MHMG. He's been great over the years and is really all about the big picture. It is because of him, not my now former gyn Dr. Markoff, that I found out I have fibroids. He explores all options with you and is very patient and understanding. He doesn't judge and asks all the right questions. Very thorough and wants to be kept in the loop on every aspect of your medical health and your life.
paraphrase: I've been seeing Dr. Goldberg for more than a decade now, likely being among his initial patients when he joined MHMG. Throughout the years, he has been excellent and truly focuses on the overall picture. Thanks to him, not my previous gynecologist Dr. Markoff, I discovered I have fibroids. He carefully considers all options with you and shows great patience and understanding. He is non-judgmental and knows exactly the right questions to ask. He's extremely thorough and wants to stay informed about every detail of your medical health and personal life.

text: Got a letter in the mail last week that said Dr. Goldberg is moving to Arizona to take a new position there in June.  He will be missed very much.  \n\nI think finding a new doctor in NYC that you actually like might almost be as awful as trying to find a date!
paraphrase: Received a letter last week informing me that Dr. Goldberg is relocating to Arizona for a new job in June. He will be greatly missed. Finding a doctor in NYC that you actually like might be nearly as dreadful as searching for a date!

text: I don't know what Dr. Goldberg was like before  moving to Arizona, but let me tell you, STAY AWAY from this doctor and this office. I was going to Dr. Johnson before he left and Goldberg took over when Johnson left. He is not a caring doctor. He is only interested in the co-pay and having you come in for medication refills every month. He will not give refills and could less about patients's financial situations. Trying to get your 90 days mail away pharmacy prescriptions through this guy is a joke. And to make matters even worse, his office staff is incompetent. 90% of the time when you call the office, they'll put you through to a voice mail, that NO ONE ever answers or returns your call. Both my adult children and husband have decided to leave this practice after experiencing such frustration. The entire office has an attitude like they are doing you a favor. Give me a break! Stay away from this doc and the practice. You deserve better and they will not be there when you really need them. I have never felt compelled to write a bad review about anyone until I met this pathetic excuse for a doctor who is all about the money.
paraphrase: I'm not sure what Dr. Goldberg was like before relocating to Arizona, but trust me, AVOID this doctor and this practice. I used to see Dr. Johnson until he left, and Goldberg took over his patients. He lacks compassion and seems focused only on collecting co-pays and insisting on monthly visits for medication refills. He doesn't provide refills and has no concern for patients' financial constraints. Obtaining a 90-day mail-order prescription from him is laughable. To add insult to injury, his office staff is inept. When you call, there's a 90% chance you'll end up in a voicemail that never gets answered or returned. After facing such frustration, my husband and adult children have chosen to leave this practice. The staff behaves as if they're doing you a favor. Seriously, give me a break! Steer clear of this doctor and this office. You deserve better, and they won’t be there when you truly need them. I've never felt the urge to write a negative review about anyone until I encountered this sorry excuse for a doctor who is solely motivated by money.

text: Dr. Eric Goldberg is a fantastic doctor who has correctly diagnosed every issue that my wife and I have had. Unlike many of my past doctors, Dr. Goldberg is very accessible and we have been able to schedule appointments with him and his staff very quickly. We are happy to have him in the neighborhood and look forward to being his patients for many years to come.
paraphrase: Dr. Eric Goldberg is an excellent doctor who has accurately identified every problem my wife and I have experienced. Unlike many of our previous doctors, Dr. Goldberg is highly accessible, allowing us to quickly arrange appointments with him and his team. We are glad he's in the area and anticipate being his patients for many years ahead.

text: I'm writing this review to give you a heads up before you see this Doctor. The office staff and administration are very unprofessional. I left a message with multiple people regarding my bill, and no one ever called me back. I had to hound them to get an answer about my bill. \n\nSecond, and most important, make sure your insurance is going to cover Dr. Goldberg's visits and blood work. He recommended to me that I get a physical, and he knew I was a student because I told him. I got the physical done. Later, I found out my health insurance doesn't pay for preventative visits. I received an $800.00 bill for the blood work. I can't pay for my bill because I'm a student and don't have any cash flow at this current time. I can't believe the Doctor wouldn't give me a heads up to make sure my insurance would cover work that wasn't necessary and was strictly preventative. The office can't do anything to help me cover the bill. In addition, the office staff said the onus is on me to make sure my insurance covers visits. Frustrating situation!
paraphrase:
I'm writing this review to warn you before visiting this Doctor. The office staff and administration behave very unprofessionally. I left messages with several people about my bill, and no one ever got back to me. I had to persistently contact them to get an answer regarding my bill.

Additionally, it's crucial to confirm that your insurance will cover Dr. Goldberg's visits and blood tests. He advised me to get a physical, even though I had informed him I was a student. I went through with the physical, only to later discover that my health insurance doesn't cover preventative visits. I received an $800 bill for the blood work. As a student with no current cash flow, I can't pay the bill. I can't believe the Doctor didn't alert me to ensure my insurance would cover non-essential, preventative work. The office can't assist me with the bill, and they stated it's my responsibility to verify insurance coverage for visits. It's a frustrating situation!

text: Wing sauce is like water. Pretty much a lot of butter and some hot sauce (franks red hot maybe).  The whole wings are good size and crispy, but for $1 a wing the sauce could be better. The hot and extra hot are about the same flavor/heat.  The fish sandwich is good and is a large portion, sides are decent.
paraphrase: Wing sauce mostly consists of butter with a bit of hot sauce, like Frank's Red Hot. The whole wings are nicely sized and crispy, but at $1 per wing, the sauce could use improvement. The hot and extra hot options have similar flavor and heat levels. The fish sandwich is tasty and generously sized, and the sides are adequate.

text: Decent range somewhat close to the city.  The mats are pretty solid; however, the grass range needs to be tended too.  It's like hitting out of US Open type rough...not very amenable to practicing.  Which kind of defeats the purpose of going to a golf range...Still gets 3 stars because the range is lit up at night which is excellent for those of us who are addicted to this amazing game, but are somewhat short on time (having a job kinda sucks sometimes, no?).
paraphrase: A reasonably good range not too far from the city. The mats are quite sturdy, but the grass area requires maintenance. It feels like trying to hit out of rough as tough as the US Open's, making practice difficult. This somewhat undermines the reason for visiting a golf range. Nevertheless, it earns 3 stars since it's well-lit at night, which is great for those of us passionate about this fantastic game but pressed for time (having a job can be a hassle, right?).

text: This place is absolute garbage...  Half of the tees are not available, including all the grass tees.  It is cash only, and they sell the last bucket at 8, despite having lights.  And if you finish even a minute after 8, don't plan on getting a drink.  The vending machines are sold out (of course) and they sell drinks inside, but close the drawers at 8 on the dot.  There are weeds grown all over the place.  I noticed some sort of batting cage, but it looks like those are out of order as well.  Someone should buy this place and turn it into what it should be.
paraphrase: This place is a complete disaster. Half of the tees aren't available, including every grass tee. It's cash only, and they stop selling buckets at 8, even though they have lights. Don't expect to get a drink if you finish after 8, even by a minute. The vending machines are predictably empty, and while they do sell drinks inside, they close promptly at 8. Weeds are growing everywhere. There's a batting cage, but that seems to be out of commission too. Someone should purchase this place and make it what it ought to be.

text: After waiting for almost 30 minutes to trade in an old phone part of the buy back program, our customer service rep incorrectly processed the transaction. This led to us waiting another 30 minutes for him to correct it. Don't visit this store if you want pleasant or good service.
paraphrase: After nearly a 30-minute wait to exchange an old phone through the buyback program, our customer service representative mishandled the transaction. This resulted in an additional 30-minute wait for him to fix the mistake. Avoid this store if you're seeking pleasant or satisfactory service.

text: This place was DELICIOUS!!  My parents saw a recommendation to visit this place from Rick Sebak's \"25 Things I Like About Pittsburgh\" and he's usually pretty accurate.  His recommendations were to try the Reuben, Fish Sandwich and Open-Faced Steak Sandwich.  We went early afternoon for a late lunch today (a Saturday) and were seated right away.  The staff is extremely friendly.  My Mom & I each had the fish sandwich, while my Dad & Brother had a Reuben sandwich.  The fish was very good, but the Reuben was to die for!  Both dishes were massive, and could very easily be shared between two people.  On top of being extremely large portions, it was incredibly affordable.  The giant fish sandwich was $8 and the giant Reuben was $7.50.  Our drinks were always filled and we were checked on several times during the meal.  We will definitely be back!!!  Oh and a bit of advice ahead of time - they take CASH ONLY.  So come prepared, but I'm pretty sure I saw an ATM there as well.  And I do believe they are closed on Sundays & Mondays.
paraphrase: This place was absolutely amazing! My parents found out about it from Rick Sebak's "25 Things I Like About Pittsburgh," and he's usually spot-on with his suggestions. He recommended trying the Reuben, Fish Sandwich, and Open-Faced Steak Sandwich. We went there early in the afternoon for a late lunch today (a Saturday) and were seated immediately. The staff was incredibly welcoming. My mom and I ordered the fish sandwich, while my dad and brother chose the Reuben. The fish was quite good, but the Reuben was exceptional! Both meals were huge and could easily feed two people. Besides being very generous in size, the prices were unbelievably reasonable. The large fish sandwich cost $8, and the sizable Reuben was $7.50. Our drinks were consistently refilled, and the staff checked on us multiple times during our meal. We are definitely planning to return!!! Just a heads-up – they only accept cash, so make sure to bring some, though I think I saw an ATM there. Also, I believe they are closed on Sundays and Mondays.

text: Old school.....traditional \"mom 'n pop\" quality and perfection. The best fish and chips you'll ever enjoy and equally superb fried shrimp. A great out of the way, non-corporate, vestige of Americana. You will love it.
paraphrase: Classic... the timeless quality and perfection of a traditional "mom and pop" establishment. The finest fish and chips you'll ever savor, along with equally outstanding fried shrimp. A fantastic, off-the-beaten-path, independent slice of Americana. You'll adore it.

text: Good fish sandwich.
paraphrase: Tasty fish sandwich.

text: Good beer selection. Understaffed for a light Monday night crowd, it wasn't her fault she was the only server. But it took about an hour to get our sandwiches. Mine was one of the best reubens I've ever had.
paraphrase: The beer variety was impressive. Despite it being a light crowd on a Monday night, they were short-staffed, and it wasn't the server's fault she was alone. However, it took nearly an hour for our sandwiches to arrive. My reuben was among the best I've ever tasted.

text: Very disappointed in the customer service. We ordered Reuben's  and wanted coleslaw instead of kraut. They charged us $3.00 for the coleslaw. We will not be back . The iced tea is also terrible tasting.
paraphrase: Highly dissatisfied with the customer service. We ordered Reuben sandwiches and requested coleslaw in place of kraut. They billed us $3.00 for the coleslaw. We won’t be returning. The iced tea also tastes awful.

text: Let there be no question: Alexions owns the best cheeseburger in the region and they have now for decades. Try a burger on Italian bread. The service is flawlessly friendly, the food is amazing, and the wings? Oh the wings... but it's still about the cheeseburger. The atmosphere is inviting, but you can't eat atmosphere... so go right now. Grab the car keys... you know you're hungry for an amazing cheeseburger, maybe some wings, and a cold beer! Easily, hands down, the best bar and grill in Pittsburgh.
paraphrase: Make no mistake: Alexions has been serving the top cheeseburger in the area for years. Their burgers on Italian bread are a must-try. The service is impeccably friendly, the food is fantastic, and the wings? Simply outstanding... but the cheeseburger remains the star. The ambiance is welcoming, but you can't consume ambiance... so head over immediately. Get your car keys... you know you're craving a fantastic cheeseburger, perhaps some wings, and a cold beer! Without a doubt, the best bar and grill in Pittsburgh.

text: Used to go there for tires, brakes, etc.  Their prices have gone way up-$400 for 4 mid-level tires for a Toyota.  Plus, 1 of the new tires went flat within 3 weeks.  Since they don\"t make appointments,  the wait to get the tire looked at was ~2 hours.  Sorry--can't wait that long to get a warranted repair,  They lost my business for good.
paraphrase: I used to visit them for tires, brakes, and other services. However, their prices have significantly increased—$400 for four mid-range tires for a Toyota. Additionally, one of the new tires became flat in less than three weeks. Without an appointment system, it took about two hours just to have the tire inspected. Unfortunately, I can't afford to wait that long for a repair under warranty. They've permanently lost me as a customer.

text: Terrible. Preordered my tires and when I arrived they couldn't find the order anywhere. Once we got through that process I waited over 2 hours for them to be put on... I was originally told it would take 30 mins. Slow, over priced, I'll go elsewhere next time.
paraphrase: Awful experience. I preordered my tires, but upon arrival, they couldn't locate the order at all. After sorting that out, I ended up waiting more than 2 hours for the installation, despite being informed it would only take 30 minutes. The service was slow and overpriced; I'll choose a different place next time.

text:
Rocky's has been in business since the 1980s and still going strong.  Their food is good.  Not haute cuisine, pretentious!  Everyday, well prepared and taste bud pleasing home style cooking.  I've mostly gone during supper hours and  and ate in the dining room, but, today I introduced an old friend to "linner" in the bar.
He ordered the large version of fish sandwich.  Rocky's is famous for their fish.  Three large breaded and fried fish filets, bun and fries for under $10.  Needless to say, he had no complaints and brought home more than he ate.
I ordered the meatball grill.  It was delish.  Served on thick cut Italian bread and made "grilled cheese" (mozzarella) style.  Meatballs and tomato sauce are both good!  Also, served with fries.  I brought home half the sandwich.
Another good meal there.  Friends, mother, and former boss all enjoy their food!
paraphrase: Rocky's has been operating since the 1980s and continues to thrive. Their food is quite good, not fancy or pretentious, but rather everyday, well-prepared, and satisfying home-style cooking. I usually visit during dinner hours and eat in the dining room, but today I introduced an old friend to a "linner" in the bar. He ordered the large fish sandwich, which Rocky's is known for. It includes three large breaded and fried fish fillets, a bun, and fries for under $10. Unsurprisingly, he had no complaints and took home more than he ate. I opted for the meatball grill, which was delicious. It was served on thick-cut Italian bread, made in a "grilled cheese" (mozzarella) style. Both the meatballs and tomato sauce were tasty! It also came with fries. I took home half of the sandwich. Another satisfying meal there. Friends, my mother, and a former boss all enjoy their food!

text:
If you want a true understanding of Pittsburgh in the morning, come here. This greasy spoon is always packed, and is one of the better of its kind south of the city.

They serve waffles in halves, which is great. The eggs and toast are good, the homemade hot sausage is excellent. The drawback are the barely cooked potatoes.

If you're hungry, get "The Mixed Grill"... Gab and Eat's brand of the "kitchen sink" breakfast that all Midwest places are about.
paraphrase: To truly grasp the essence of Pittsburgh mornings, pay a visit to this spot. This bustling greasy spoon is among the top of its kind south of the city. They serve waffles in halves, which is fantastic. The eggs and toast are decent, while the homemade hot sausage is outstanding. However, the downside is the undercooked potatoes. If you have a big appetite, try "The Mixed Grill"... Gab and Eat's version of the "kitchen sink" breakfast that's common in the Midwest.

text: Cheap, unpretentious, and, for this, one of my favorite breakfast joints in the country.  Simply put I LOVE it here.  The mixed grill, the sausage and egg on a biscuit, the home fries.  This it the very definition of diner.  Thank you Gab 'n' Eat!
paraphrase: Inexpensive and straightforward, this is why it's one of my top breakfast spots nationwide. To put it plainly, I ADORE this place. The mixed grill, the sausage and egg on a biscuit, the home fries. This is the epitome of a diner. Thanks, Gab 'n' Eat!

text: I miss this place. When I return home to pittsburgh this is always a must to ease myself into a saturday morning recovery after getting banged up on cheap south side cocktails the night before. When I think of pittsburgh, I remember cutting morning classes in highschool just to get after the best bacon egg and cheese i know of. Honestly i live in nyc where you can get a bacon egg and cheese on every single corner, and nowhere that i have been does it as well as this joint. Go here and you will be happy you did.
paraphrase: I long for this spot. Whenever I'm back in Pittsburgh, it's essential to start my Saturday morning recovery here after a night of indulging in inexpensive South Side cocktails. Reflecting on Pittsburgh brings back memories of skipping morning classes in high school to chase the finest bacon egg and cheese I've ever had. Even though I live in NYC, where you can find a bacon egg and cheese on every corner, no place I've visited does it quite like this one. Visit, and you'll be glad you did.

text:
OK, what is with all of these "CASH ONLY" places in Pittsburgh? I can appreciate that some smaller businesses might have trouble installing a credit card system in their store/resturant, but could you perhaps provide an ATM?  In the past, this would deter me from eating at this place. 

Looking at a standard breakfast menu, I decided to go with a tuna melt instead. What I got was a cold tuna salad sandwich with a slice of cheese on toast. I had ordered a melt being in the mood for a warm meal, but to no avail. One day later I can't even remember what my side was. I suppose it didn't leave much of an impression on me.  I have to say that the dessert was really good! The cherry pecan pie! I have not seen this concoction anywhere else but this was awesome! The cherries were not the sweet canned variety, but a pie worthy tart topped with sugar. I think I want to make this pie with my parents next harvest of our cherry tree!

My first impression is "Eh."  I am learning how crazy Pittsburghers are over breakfast! Seriously, waiting 30 minutes for a table at a tiny diner with a menu that reads as follows:  

"2 eggs and toast $x.xx"
"2 eggs, meat, and toast $x.xx"
"2 eggs and pancake $x.xx"

I'd rather go to a Denny's and order the "create your own Grand Slam!"
paraphrase:
What’s up with all these “CASH ONLY” spots in Pittsburgh? I get that some small businesses might struggle to set up a credit card system in their shops or restaurants, but maybe they could consider having an ATM? Previously, this would have kept me from dining at such places.

While browsing a typical breakfast menu, I opted for a tuna melt. What I received, however, was a cold tuna salad sandwich with cheese on toast. I had ordered a melt because I was in the mood for something warm, but that didn’t happen. A day later, I can’t even recall what side dish I had, which suggests it didn’t stand out. On a positive note, the dessert was fantastic! The cherry pecan pie was unique and impressive! The cherries weren’t the sweet canned type but a tart pie with sugar on top. I’m thinking of making this pie with my parents when our cherry tree yields its next harvest!

My initial reaction was “Eh.” I’m noticing how passionate Pittsburgh residents are about breakfast! Honestly, waiting 30 minutes for a table at a small diner with a menu like this: 

“2 eggs and toast $x.xx”
“2 eggs, meat, and toast $x.xx”
“2 eggs and pancake $x.xx”

I’d prefer to head to a Denny's and customize a Grand Slam breakfast!

text: BEST DINER IN THE COUNRTY!!! We've been to many famous diners across the country and we still give  Gab and Eat the best rating !! I was a little intimidated when I first walked in and there was like 2 pounds of butter just sitting on top of the homefires on the grill.  If you are looking for a healthy breakfast they probably can accomodate you, but Everything I ate was clearly the opposite of healthy.  After trying like every meal they have I would recommend the mix grill(half unless you are sharing) adding cajun seasoning with the texas toast.  Burgers are great too.\n\nIt's hard to find a place that makes a better breakfast than you could make by yourself at home, but this place does it.  The atmosphere is classy, old school Americana.
paraphrase:
TOP DINER IN THE NATION!!! We've visited numerous renowned diners nationwide, yet Gab and Eat consistently earns our highest praise!! Initially, I was a bit daunted upon entering and seeing about 2 pounds of butter atop the home fries on the grill. If you're after a nutritious breakfast, they likely can cater to you, but everything I consumed was decidedly not health-conscious. After sampling nearly every dish on their menu, I suggest the mix grill (a half portion unless you're sharing), with Cajun seasoning and Texas toast. The burgers are excellent as well.

It's rare to find a spot that prepares a breakfast better than what you can whip up at home, but this place achieves that. The ambiance is classy, with a vintage Americana vibe.

text: Great breakfast, good price. You might have to stand outside in line though, so I don't really recommend winter time to go. lol. Very friendly service, interesting coffee mugs. They have great deserts and such also. Bring your cash though as they dont' take cards.
paraphrase: The breakfast is excellent and reasonably priced, but you might have to wait in line outside, so it's not ideal to visit in winter. The service is very friendly, and the coffee mugs are unique. They also offer great desserts. Remember to bring cash, as they don't accept cards.

text:
I always love a good diner.  Gab and Eat was just what we were looking for on a Saturday morning.  Good food.  Great people. Pittsburgh ambiance. 

This place is obviously a beloved neighborhood gem.  I had french toast and bacon.  The french toast was made with the best Italian bread.  My fiance had what they called a 'mixed grill."  It consisted of eggs, peppers, onions and home made sausage.  Not something you would eat everyday, but he loved it!   They also had great coffee.  They actually ground up the beans in front of us.  Now that's fresh.

We want to return sometime for "turkey day."  Apparently they made fresh turkey every Tuesday.  Now that sounds good!

Can't wait to return.
paraphrase: I have a deep appreciation for a quality diner, and Gab and Eat perfectly fit the bill for our Saturday morning outing. The food was satisfying, the people were wonderful, and the place had that distinct Pittsburgh vibe. It's clear that this spot is a cherished local treasure. I enjoyed some French toast and bacon, with the toast made from top-notch Italian bread. My fiancé opted for a dish they called a 'mixed grill,' which included eggs, peppers, onions, and homemade sausage. It's not an everyday meal, but he really enjoyed it! They also served excellent coffee, grinding the beans right in front of us for ultimate freshness. We're eager to return for "turkey day," as they reportedly serve fresh turkey every Tuesday. That sounds delicious! We can't wait to go back.

text: I have been coming to Gab n Eat for almost 20 years and They have never let me down. I get a typical breakfast if eggs, ham, toast, and home fries. Delicious as usual. The ambience however is usually lacking. The walls are dark, with writing and signatures of semi famous people all over the place. Pictures of local people hang on the walls(i secretly want mine up there) along with posters galore. While its fun to look at the first 10 times, it gets a little boring after awhile. So today when I arrived I expected the same old experience. Wow was I wrong! As soon as I looked at the door I knew something was different. The place seemed lighter and brighter. To my pleasant surprise, they painted and got new counter tops!! They're not quite done yet but the place has a new Happy vibe to it. The awesome breakfast, the new decor and the 5 guys sitting at the counter making me laugh are why I will be back( maybe for lunch).
paraphrase: For nearly two decades, I've been visiting Gab n Eat, and they've consistently met my expectations. My go-to order is a classic breakfast of eggs, ham, toast, and home fries, which is always delicious. However, the atmosphere usually leaves something to be desired. The walls are dark and covered with signatures and notes from somewhat famous individuals. Photos of locals and a multitude of posters add to the decor—a novelty that wears thin after about ten visits. So, when I arrived today, I anticipated the usual experience. How wrong I was! The moment I approached the door, I sensed a change. The place felt brighter and more inviting. To my delight, they had painted and installed new countertops! While the renovations aren't complete, there's a new, cheerful vibe. The fantastic breakfast, fresh look, and the five guys at the counter who kept me laughing are reasons I'll return—perhaps even for lunch.

text: What a find! I stopped in here for breakfast while in town for business. The service is so friendly I thought I was down south. The service was quick, frankly and felt like I was with family. \nFantastic poached eggs, Cajun homefries and crispy bacon. Gab and Eat is definitely a place I world recommend to locals. I was stuffed and the bill was only $8.00.
paraphrase: What a discovery! I dropped by here for breakfast during a business trip. The service was incredibly welcoming, making me feel like I was in the South. It was fast and made me feel like I was with family. The poached eggs were fantastic, the Cajun homefries were delicious, and the bacon was crispy. Gab and Eat is definitely a spot I'd suggest to locals. I left feeling full, and the bill was just $8.00.

text: If I could give this place less than one star, I would. I have no idea who gave this place high reviews but they must either own the place or be time travelers from back in the day when this place might not have sucked. The decor is tired and grimy, the place reeked of smoke, and the bartender/server was surly, to put it mildly. We went there on a Saturday night with a mind to try the Irish food. Apparently, we were out of luck. I've always thought that the secrets of restaurant success is to actually stock food for people to eat. He told us before we ordered that they had no \"pies\". No chicken pot pie, no shepherds pie, etc. So, we gamely tried to order other things. We placed our order. My wife, for example, ordered the Irish stew and he came back 5 min later telling us they were out of that and even more things for several people in our party. At that point my wife picked out a third option, ham and cabbage, only to be told again that \"they were out\". At that point, realizing that the only food to be had in the place was what was crusted on the menus, we asked to pay for our drinks and left. They actually then gave us flack for not having enough to put it on a debit card. In short, unless you like your dinner with a side of disappointment and depression, I'd probably avoid this place like the plague. Speaking of the plague, I suppose we should thank Mr. Surly for inspiring us to walk out. I only have two bathrooms at my house and would've been hard pressed to accommodate several violently ill people at once.
paraphrase: If it were possible to give this place fewer than one star, I certainly would. I can’t fathom who has rated this place highly; they must either own it or be time travelers from a time when it might not have been terrible. The decor is outdated and filthy, the place stank of smoke, and the bartender/server was downright rude. We visited on a Saturday night hoping to try some Irish cuisine, but luck wasn't on our side. I've always believed a restaurant's success hinges on actually having food to serve. He informed us before we ordered that they had no "pies"—no chicken pot pie, no shepherd's pie, and so on. So, we tried ordering other items. After placing our order, my wife asked for the Irish stew, only for him to return five minutes later to say they were out of that and several other items our party had chosen. My wife then selected a third option, ham and cabbage, only to be told once more that it was unavailable. Realizing at that point that the only thing edible might be the crusted remnants on the menus, we opted to pay for our drinks and leave. They even gave us trouble for not spending enough to use a debit card. In short, unless you're in the mood for a meal accompanied by disappointment and gloom, I'd suggest steering clear of this place like it's the plague. On the topic of plagues, we should probably thank Mr. Rude for prompting us to leave. With only two bathrooms at home, accommodating several people falling violently ill would've been quite the challenge.

text: Best Irish food in the Burgh.  Great bar food too.  The service is maybe a bit surly and it's not the cleanest, nor the best decor.  But, that's not what this place is about.  It's a town Irish bar and it serves great solid Irish fare and suds to the locals.  And it does it better than anyone.  I go back again and again.
paraphrase: The top spot for Irish cuisine in the Burgh, with excellent bar food as well. The service can be somewhat gruff, and the cleanliness and decor leave something to be desired. However, that's not the focus here. It's a local Irish pub that offers hearty Irish dishes and drinks, outperforming others. I find myself returning repeatedly.

text:
The delivery driver mistakenly rang my doorbell, having confused 133 and 113.

Rather than taking a step back and analyzing the situation, he begins to accuse my wife and I of ordering and refusing to pay for this pizza.

The driver then gets on his cell phone and rather than calling the number than was given when the order was placed, begins to call his bosses and starts threatening me with felony charges. 

So I take the initiative and ask the fine upstanding gentleman what the phone number of the order-er was, phone my neighbor and discover the mistake. Rather than a thank you or a sorry, he just speeds off (breaking the speed limit on our block) to reach his destination 50 feet away.

I would call to complain, but based on the other reviews, its clear the owners do not care about Carnegie or it's residents, and its pretty well known around town just how awful their food is, so it would be pointless to boycott a place I'd never order from again anyway.

Do yourself a favor and order from ANY other establishment in town.
paraphrase: The delivery driver accidentally rang my doorbell after mixing up 133 with 113. Instead of pausing to assess the situation, he started accusing my wife and me of ordering a pizza and refusing to pay for it. He then used his cell phone, not to contact the number from the order, but to call his bosses and threaten me with felony charges. I decided to take matters into my own hands and asked the driver for the phone number associated with the order. I called my neighbor and discovered the mix-up. Without offering thanks or an apology, the driver sped off, exceeding the speed limit on our street, to reach his actual destination just 50 feet away. I considered filing a complaint, but based on other reviews, it's evident the owners don't care about Carnegie or its residents, and their food has a terrible reputation around town. So, there's no point in boycotting a place I'd never order from again anyway. Do yourself a favor and choose any other restaurant in town.

text:
Some of the worst pizza I've ever had.  We used a coupon from the paper for a 2 topping 8 cut Sicilian. First of all the pizza wasn't even cut through, and the sad attempt at cutting was so uneven that 4 of the slices were about an inch wide, while the others were about 4" each. The toppings were scarce, they used mini pepperoni and put maybe 8 on the whole pizza. The onions were huge chunks and the mushrooms were straight from a can. The worst part though was the thick doughy crust that tasted more like a fishy sourdough roll. I'm serious... It was so noticeable that it made me wonder if the dough was bad or if they for some weird reason put fish sauce in it. It was gross. 

We also ordered steak and Italian hoagies. The veggies were old and wilted, and there was no dressing on either. The Italian had deli meat that was clearly bottom of the line and not very generous. The "steak" (if you an call it that) was greyish instead of brown and looked like it was a processed meat chopped into pieces. No flavor or seasoning and the texture was reminiscent of spam. It was so bad that I only ate 1/4 of it and tossed the rest. 

I have ordered from here in the past and always been disappointed. I thought I would give them another try since I'd never ordered a Sicilian pizza from there. What a mistake. I will never order from them again!
paraphrase:
This was among the worst pizza experiences I've had. We used a newspaper coupon for a Sicilian pizza with two toppings and eight slices. To start, the pizza wasn't properly sliced, resulting in inconsistent pieces—four slices were about an inch wide, while the others were nearly four inches. The toppings were sparse, with just a few tiny pepperonis spread across the pizza. The onions were in large chunks, and the mushrooms seemed to come straight from a can. The most disappointing aspect was the thick, doughy crust that tasted oddly like a fishy sourdough roll. It was so distinct that I questioned whether the dough had gone bad or if fish sauce had somehow been added. It was unappetizing. 

We also ordered steak and Italian hoagies. The vegetables were old and wilted, and neither had any dressing. The Italian hoagie contained lower-grade deli meat and was not generously filled. The "steak" was a grayish color instead of brown, looking like processed meat cut into pieces, with no flavor or seasoning, and had a texture similar to spam. It was so unappealing that I barely ate a quarter of it before throwing the rest away.

I've ordered from this place before and have always been let down. I decided to give them another chance since I hadn't tried their Sicilian pizza, but that was a big mistake. I won't be ordering from them again.

text: I needed alterations done to my suit very quickly. Like three days quickly. Not only did Filberto at Alteration World have it ready, the price was beyond reasonable and the work was EXCELLENT! I plan on bringing all my clothes to him from now on.\n\nOh and he let me know that they also dry-clean. Wonderful shop!
paraphrase:
I urgently required some adjustments made to my suit—in just three days. Filberto at Alteration World not only completed it on time, but the cost was extremely fair and the quality was OUTSTANDING! I'll be taking all my clothes to him from here on out.  

Plus, he informed me that they offer dry-cleaning services too. Fantastic shop!

text: Are you are really looking at the review for LJS?  Its fried stuff, with extra fried stuff in a cardboard boat.  You will regret eating it 30 minutes after you finish, but it is delicious.
paraphrase: Are you seriously checking out the LJS review? It’s just fried food, piled with more fried food in a cardboard container. You'll feel regret half an hour after eating it, but it tastes amazing.

text: By far the worst most unprofessional dentist I have ever experienced her staff is too busy watching soap operas on all the TVs in each room instead of cleaning and she gave me a filling which was too deep which could have been understandable as her son had his pictures delivered from Hawaii and she had to stop 3 times to pay for them and show her whole staff all of them must be nice to have a 14 year old go to Hawaii on her moms dime and have her patience wait to see all of this happen I had to have oral surgery and have the tooth removed because she put the filling in too deep an abscessed tooth was the result I wouldn't recommend her to an ex girlfriend or someone I hate because of the three months of extreme tooth pain I had to deal with because of her lack of care and follow through even after I called and asked them for advice and follow up they simply could care less and did not even offer to reschedule an appointment other than prescribing me vicodin and antibiotics do not recommend and do not go to her license should be pulled!!
paraphrase: This is by far the most unprofessional dentist I've ever encountered. Her staff seems more interested in watching soap operas on the TVs in every room than in maintaining cleanliness. During my visit, she gave me an overly deep filling, which might have been understandable given that she was distracted by the delivery of her son's photos from Hawaii. She interrupted the procedure three times to pay for them and show them to her entire staff. It's astonishing that a 14-year-old could vacation in Hawaii thanks to his mother's money, while her patients are left waiting. As a result of her deep filling, I ended up with an abscessed tooth that required oral surgery for removal. I wouldn't recommend her even to an ex-girlfriend or someone I dislike, due to the three months of severe tooth pain I endured because of her negligence and lack of follow-up. Despite my calls for advice and follow-up, they showed no concern and didn't even attempt to reschedule an appointment, merely prescribing Vicodin and antibiotics. I strongly advise against visiting this dentist, as her license should be revoked.

text: When I moved to the area, Dr. Weinberg was recommended to me by my cousin who had been a client for years. I've had nothing but positive experiences with her and her staff since seeing her two years ago. The team is professional, nice and courteous. I recently had to reschedule an appointment due to a last minute work thing and they were very accommodating. My daughter looks forward to coming to the dentist so that's saying something. I DO wish they would turn off FOX news in the lobby as I'd rather watch paint dry but... nobody's perfect.
paraphrase: When I relocated to this area, my cousin, a long-time client, suggested Dr. Weinberg. Over the past two years, my experiences with her and her team have been exclusively positive. The staff is professional, friendly, and polite. I had a last-minute work conflict and needed to reschedule, and they were very accommodating. My daughter actually enjoys visiting the dentist, which speaks volumes. However, I wish they wouldn't play FOX news in the waiting area—I'd prefer watching paint dry—but, of course, nobody's perfect.

text:
In all fairness - I did not see this dentist but did schedule an appointment and waited 15 minutes for them to open.

When I look at Yelp reviews, I usually weed out the outliers (the best and the worst).   Unfortunately, there are only two reviews.

It was my first time at the office.   The office admin (very nice) asked for me to come early (before 9am) to complete paperwork.   I arrived at 8:50.    I waited.   I watched 2 employees walk by me (unlocked the door and re-locked it).    They did not acknowledge me.

The door I was at had signage (open hours).    I decided to leave because of a review where the staff seemed distracted or disinterested in my presence.

The office admin (very nice) called me to inform I am missing my appointment.   I explained to her that there were too many indications where this was not going to be a good fit and wished her the best of luck.

I recommend if you see this dentist you go to the front door (furthest away from the parking lot) for the entrance.

Poor recommendation because the staff who walked by me seemed to be distracted which was pointed out in a previous review.
paraphrase:
To be fair, I didn't actually see the dentist but had set up an appointment and waited 15 minutes for the office to open. When I check Yelp reviews, I usually ignore the extremes (the highest and lowest ratings). Unfortunately, there are only two reviews available.

This was my first visit to the office. The office administrator (very nice) asked me to arrive early (before 9am) to fill out paperwork. I got there at 8:50. I waited and observed as two employees walked past me, unlocking and re-locking the door, without acknowledging me.

The door I was at displayed the opening hours. I chose to leave because a review mentioned the staff seemed inattentive or uninterested in my presence.

The office administrator (very nice) called to tell me I missed my appointment. I explained that there were too many signs suggesting it wouldn't be a suitable match and wished her all the best.

I suggest that if you visit this dentist, you use the front door (furthest from the parking lot) as the entrance.

This is not a strong recommendation because the staff who passed by seemed distracted, which was also noted in a previous review.

text: I've been to this office a good amount of times.  Each time the receptionists give of a vibe of entitlement and it is very uncomfortable.  Dr. Weinberg herself is very nice, but I doubt her ability. Every procedure she has done to my teeth has backfired. The third and last issue I have is that i always paid cash at the end of my visits and somehow I have received an invoice for $55. I called and of course the receptionist was sassy and rude so I gave up. Will never go back or refer anyone else
paraphrase: I've visited this office quite a few times. Each visit, the receptionists exude an entitled attitude, which makes for a very uncomfortable experience. While Dr. Weinberg is quite pleasant, I question her competence. Every dental procedure she's performed has ended poorly. Additionally, despite always paying cash at the end of my visits, I somehow received a $55 invoice. When I called about it, the receptionist was predictably rude and dismissive, so I gave up. I won't be returning or recommending this place to others.

text:
I used to order takeout here once every month or so. The food tastes average and the price is good. It's your typical low-end Chinese place. 

The last (and final) time I ordered here I just so happened to be picking up my order while a health inspector from allegheny county health department was making his rounds. While I was waiting on my food (which took much longer than usual) the inspector was pointing out violations. He also requested a copy of a certificate that the man behind the register was unable to produce. The inspector looked at me like I was crazy for waiting on my food despite the obvious poor conditions.  

As for the rest of the place - it's always been kind of dirty inside. The carpets are just FILTHY and the walls match. There are always children's toys scattered about the floor because the owners children are always there, so be careful not to trip. 

The workers do seem like nice people just trying to make a living but when it comes to cleanliness Don Don is lacking.
paraphrase:
I used to get takeout from this place about once a month. The food is mediocre, but the prices are reasonable. It’s your standard budget Chinese restaurant.

The last time I ordered here, I happened to be there while an inspector from the Allegheny County Health Department was conducting an inspection. As I waited for my meal, which took unusually long, the inspector was identifying various violations. He also asked for a certificate that the cashier couldn't provide. The inspector gave me a look that suggested he thought I was out of my mind for waiting for my food, given the clearly poor conditions.

Regarding the rest of the restaurant, it has always been somewhat unclean. The carpets are incredibly dirty, and the walls aren't any better. There are often children's toys all over the floor since the owners' kids are always around, so watch your step.

The staff seems like decent people just trying to earn a living, but Don Don definitely falls short in terms of cleanliness.

text: I belong to this gym...  I live in the South section of Pittsburgh, and I find that this gym is not too far from me.  The staff is friendly, the equipment is quite good.  You get two free personal training sessions when you join.  They have lots of weights (which my boyfriend uses) and a decent cardio room.  The only thing I would say is to increase some of the cardio equipment.  Water is only $1 a bottle!
paraphrase: I'm a member of this gym. I live in the southern part of Pittsburgh, and this gym is conveniently located for me. The staff are welcoming, and the equipment is pretty decent. When you sign up, you get two complimentary personal training sessions. They offer plenty of weights (which my boyfriend uses) and have a satisfactory cardio room. My only suggestion would be to add more cardio machines. Bottled water costs just $1!

text: I bought a three month membership last year, mainly to attend the Silver Sneakers classes. I was in my early sixties and hadn't exercised in a while. I tried the evening classes but they were very crowded and difficult, so I tried a morning class for seniors. I like to stretch out before my class actually starts, so I went to the tail end of a yoga class. The women in this class were very nasty to me and told me that I wasn't welcome to stretch out in their class. I never went back thanks to the attitude of the other members. I didn't feel welcome here. I complained to the manager, who said that I was welcome to stretch out in whatever class I wanted to, but the hostility of these yoga students was very evident. Do they think that this is their private gym?
paraphrase: Last year, I signed up for a three-month membership, primarily to join the Silver Sneakers classes. Being in my early sixties and having been inactive for some time, I initially tried the evening classes, but they were overcrowded and challenging. So, I switched to a senior morning class. Preferring to stretch before it began, I joined the end of a yoga session. However, the women in that class were quite rude, telling me I wasn't welcome to stretch there. Their attitude made me feel unwelcome, so I never returned. I reported this to the manager, who assured me I could stretch in any class I chose, but the yoga participants' unfriendly behavior was clear. Do they consider this their personal gym?

text: If you like being greeted in the morning by a gym worker slopping down eggs from a frying pany instead of working....this is your gym....complain to the manager and she gets nasty....try getting in touch with the owner....impossible!  Most unprofessional gym ever!!!!
paraphrase: If you enjoy having a gym employee serve you eggs from a frying pan in the morning instead of doing their job, this is the place for you. Complain to the manager and she becomes unpleasant, and contacting the owner? Good luck with that! This is the most unprofessional gym I've ever encountered!

text:
I've been to Papa J's twice and had mixed experiences.

Both times I had the banana pepper appetizer, which is great and goes really well with the FRESH and delicious bread and cheese they give you at the start of your meal.

For entrees, me and my girlfriend have had mixed experience.  I've had the fish sandwich (very good) and the eggplant parm sandwich (okay).  My girlfriend got the salad with bread and basil on it, but the basil was over powering and the bread was soggy with the dressing. 

The service is also a mixed bag.  The first time our server went out of her way to take care of us and even MADE me cocktail sauce for my fish sandwich.  The second time, the server was lackluster, didn't know anything about the menu and wasn't able to take proper care of us.  

I would return to Papa J's, but I my terrible experience last time isn't enough to say it would be my first pick of places to eat around Carnegie/Robinson.
paraphrase: I've visited Papa J's on two occasions and had varied experiences. Each time, I ordered the banana pepper appetizer, which is excellent and pairs nicely with the FRESH, tasty bread and cheese served at the beginning of the meal. Regarding the main courses, my girlfriend and I have had differing experiences. I've tried the fish sandwich (very good) and the eggplant parm sandwich (average). My girlfriend ordered a salad with bread and basil, but the basil was overwhelming and the bread became soggy from the dressing. The service also varied. On our first visit, our server was exceptionally attentive, even going so far as to make cocktail sauce for my fish sandwich. On the second visit, however, the server was unimpressive, lacked menu knowledge, and failed to provide adequate service. I would consider going back to Papa J's, but my disappointing experience last time doesn't make it my top choice for dining in the Carnegie/Robinson area.

text:
What a wonderful surprise found in Carnegie PA, just south of Pittsburgh.  I was in town on business with a couple co-workers and after having a not so great meal the night before, we asked one of the locals for a recomendation.  They told us to try this little Italian place called Papa J's and boy did they send us to a great place.

First off, when you walk up to the place, the front and side windows give you a fantastic open view into the kitchen from the outside.  You can stand outside and watch the chef making all the wonderful food and you can smell the fresh bread before you even walk in the door.

I am not sure how long this place has been around but the kitchen looked well used, while the dining area as well as the bar looked very clean and modern, almost as if it had been freshly updated.  

The server was quick to welcome us and suggested several different items off the menu as his favorites when asked.  The three of us were very hungry and wanting to try a few different things, so we began with a Margherita pizza which was absolutely perfect.  They obviously make their own breads and dough for the pizza and I don't think this pizza could have tasted any better.  Then came bowls of Italian wedding soup which was pretty good.  Of everything there, this was the one item I probably could have done without.  Not that it was bad, but there was nothing particularly special about it.  

We also ordered the Garlic Rolls (not that the bread they already give you and the pizza was not enough carbs for 1 meal) and they were to die for.  Crunchy on the outside and warm and chewy inside, these things were a meal by themselves.  One of the guys with me said the pizza sauce would be good on them so he ordered a cup of the pizza sauce (which is fresh made there, not out of some can) and he was right.  As if these little killer bread balls with garlic were not good enough, dipped in the pizza sauce they were even that much better.  

By the time our meals came, we were all pretty full, but we pushed on and continued to eat.  I had the Veal Parmigiano which was tender and very good.  It came with a side of pasta which I tasted just to try and it was excelent as well.  Had I not been eating a weeks worth of carbs in one sitting with all the pre-meal stuff, I certainly would have finished it.  As it was, I ate most but not all of the generous portion of veal.  

One of my co-workers had the Chicken Picatta which he said was one of the best he had ever had and the other guy tried a pasta dish which he said was very good as well.  

They had a great wine selection as well at reasonable prices by the glass or bottle.

Overall a fantastic experience with great food and atmosphere.  I don't really know how expensive the area is for food, but coming from the Chicagoland area, I fouknd this place to be pretty reasonable.  The three of us ate very very well for an average of aproximately $30 each not including alcahol.  Fantastic fresh food at a reasonable price, who could ask for more.

My only complaint is that I had to return to Chicago and I am now craving to return to Papa J's.  I hope work sends me out here again sometime soon!!!
paraphrase:
What an amazing discovery we made in Carnegie, PA, just south of Pittsburgh. I was visiting for work with a couple of colleagues, and after a disappointing meal the previous night, we asked a local for a suggestion. They directed us to a small Italian restaurant called Papa J's, and it turned out to be an excellent recommendation.

As you approach the restaurant, the large windows at the front and side offer a fantastic open view into the kitchen from outside. You can stand and watch the chef preparing delicious food, and the aroma of fresh bread greets you before you even step inside.

I'm not sure how long this spot has been around, but the kitchen looks well-used, while the dining area and bar appear very clean and modern, as if they've been recently updated.

Our server promptly welcomed us and recommended several menu items as his favorites when we asked. We were quite hungry and eager to try a few different dishes, so we started with a Margherita pizza that was absolutely perfect. It's clear they make their own breads and pizza dough, and I doubt this pizza could have tasted any better. Next, we had bowls of Italian wedding soup, which was decent. Of all the dishes, this was the one I could have skipped—not bad, but nothing particularly special.

We also tried the Garlic Rolls (not that there wasn't enough bread with the pizza) and they were incredible. Crunchy on the outside and warm and chewy inside, they could have been a meal in themselves. One of my colleagues suggested trying them with pizza sauce, so he ordered a cup of the fresh-made sauce, and he was right. These little garlic-infused bread balls were even better dipped in the sauce.

By the time our main courses arrived, we were quite full, but we pressed on. I had the Veal Parmigiano, which was tender and very good, accompanied by a side of pasta that was excellent as well. Though I'd already consumed enough carbs for a week, I managed to eat most of the generous portion of veal.

One of my colleagues had the Chicken Picatta, which he claimed was one of the best he's ever had, while the other tried a pasta dish he found very good.

The restaurant also offered a great wine selection at reasonable prices, whether by the glass or bottle.

Overall, it was a fantastic experience with great food and atmosphere. I'm not familiar with the typical food prices in the area, but coming from Chicagoland, I found this place reasonably priced. The three of us enjoyed a very satisfying meal for about $30 each, excluding alcohol. Fantastic fresh food at a reasonable price—what more could you ask for?

My only regret is having to go back to Chicago while craving another visit to Papa J's. I hope work brings me back here soon!

text:
Yay, I'm a fan but sometimes service is a little slow, it was very good for us this visit.  Go to Papa j's every once in a while but mostly for the White Pizza.  It is the best white pizza I have ever had.  Order the white pizza on our visit this  weekend... it has garlic, spinach, feta cheese and we usually add some veggie on top.  It was delicious!  Order fried calamari and it was OK...note to self next time try the calamari roman style.

Like the dinning room with the hardwood floors and bright lighting.  

The bar was jumping thou never go to the bar.
paraphrase:
I'm a fan, though the service can be a bit slow at times. However, during our recent visit, it was excellent. We occasionally visit Papa J's, mainly for the White Pizza, which is the best I've ever had. During our visit this weekend, we ordered the white pizza topped with garlic, spinach, feta cheese, and usually add some extra veggies. It was delicious! We also tried the fried calamari, which was okay, but next time, I'll try the calamari Roman style.

I like the dining room with its hardwood floors and bright lighting. The bar was lively, although I never go there.

text: Terrible service.  Food unremarkable.  Waiter disappeared for 45 minutes to serve larger group due to staffing mismanagement.  Saved his tip by discounting meal after I complained.  All and all, a very crude and unpleasant dining experience for me and my guests.  Not to be repeated, never again!
paraphrase: Awful service. The food was nothing special. The waiter vanished for 45 minutes to attend to a larger group because of staffing issues. He only saved his tip by reducing the bill after I raised a complaint. Overall, it was a very rough and unsatisfactory dining experience for me and my guests. I will not return, ever!

text:
Yay, I'm a fan of the white pizza.  Had take out.  

The bar was jumping when I picked up our order.

The white pizza is so delicious...with garlic, spinach, feta, and added some veggies.  Tried the Roman Style calamari, very good better than the fried calamari we had last time.
paraphrase:
Woohoo, I love the white pizza! Got it as takeout. 

The bar was lively when I went to get our order.

The white pizza tastes amazing... with garlic, spinach, feta, and some extra veggies. Tried the Roman Style calamari this time, very tasty, definitely better than the fried calamari we had before.

text:
OK, its Friday night and have about 70 minutes to eat. Driving by Papa J's and W says, lets stop in and get a quick dinner. 

Advice host that we are in a hurry.  The host advised us it is a 90 minute service. The place was crowded, we were seated at the last open table. However our waiter, Jason, said he will do his best to get as out on time. We ordered our dinner immediately, with a salad and a glass of wine. In a few minutes we had our wine, then in a few more seconds we had our salads and some great homemade Italian bread with some olive oil and balsamic ving. for dipping.  Yum!

Just as we were finishing our salads our dinners were delivered. Great job Jason! The Fettuccine Carbanara was very good; a thick cream sauce with bacon and ricotta.  In and out in 60 minutes.  Now that is some good service.
paraphrase:
It's Friday night, and we have roughly 70 minutes to eat. As we drive by Papa J's, W suggests stopping in for a quick meal. We inform the host that we're in a rush, and they mention that the usual service takes about 90 minutes. The place is bustling, and we're seated at the last available table. Our waiter, Jason, reassures us he'll try to get us out on time. We quickly place our order, including a salad and a glass of wine. Within minutes, our wine arrives, followed shortly by our salads and some delicious homemade Italian bread with olive oil and balsamic vinegar for dipping. Yum!

Just as we finish our salads, our main courses arrive. Great job, Jason! The Fettuccine Carbonara was excellent, featuring a thick cream sauce with bacon and ricotta. We were in and out in 60 minutes. That's what I call good service.

text: Great little gem! The food was fantastic was very food and just the right price. The Italian food was amazing, but you can not leave without a dessert and be prepared for giant serving. I had the carrot cake yummy!! Def a place everyone should try.
paraphrase: Wonderful hidden treasure! The cuisine was excellent and reasonably priced. The Italian dishes were outstanding, but you absolutely must try a dessert and expect large portions. I enjoyed the carrot cake—delicious! Definitely a spot everyone ought to visit.

text: san francisco california visitor liked the food and the local is great. I am thinking about relocating to pttsbrgh area. I liked Carnegie because u can stroll down Main street and hit a couple places for lunch. I imagine this place would be crazy busy on friday or saturday night. we ate a lunchtime and it was nice.
paraphrase: A visitor to San Francisco, California enjoyed the food and found the local scene impressive. I'm considering moving to the Pittsburgh area. I enjoyed Carnegie because you can walk along Main Street and find a few spots for lunch. I can picture this area being extremely busy on a Friday or Saturday night. We had lunch there, and it was pleasant.

text:
Coming here you get the feeling they haven't changed much.  The tables, the decorations, the menu.  All the same.  Does that make you a little worried?  Yes.

I ordered the mushroom ravioli and it was... oily.  Very very oily.  The actual ravioli was okay, enough mushrooms.  but it was oily.  Did I mention that?  

Ambiance, like I said, left much to be desired.  I don't think I'd have this place on my list of "musts" for downtown.  Much better restaurants to visit!
paraphrase:
When you arrive here, you get the sense that not much has changed. The tables, the decorations, the menu—everything remains the same. Does that cause a bit of concern? Yes.

I ordered the mushroom ravioli, and it was... greasy. Very, very greasy. The ravioli itself was alright, with a sufficient amount of mushrooms, but it was greasy. Did I mention that?

As for the ambiance, like I said, it leaves a lot to be desired. I wouldn't consider this place a "must-visit" for downtown. There are much better restaurants to explore!

text: Although the first impression through the door is charming and the aroma enticing  as you enter this restaurant- much can be improved upon for the prices asked for the dining experience. Our group of 7 had called a week ahead and were seated in the back of the far dining room, right by a door where clinking pots /pans and  dishes were heard throughout our meal, as well as a Darth Vader type noise coming from the ceiling vent. Our waitress was not knowledgable of all menu items, and three of our  very simple requests did not come out as ordered. ( dressing on the side of a salad,  less feta on an entree and no oil on another entree) The waitress blamed it on the cook, but did offer to get one entree changed. Also we were asked if we wanted pine nuts, feta or Gorgonzola on our salad and then were charged 2.00 extra per item in addition to the salad price (6.99) which brought the salad up to 10.99 ! Outrageous !\nThere is so much possibility for ambiance in this restaurant , but they miss the mark on service and atmosphere. I will most likely not return. Very overpriced for the quality and service.
paraphrase: While the initial impression upon entering this restaurant is delightful and the aroma is captivating, there is significant room for improvement considering the prices charged for the dining experience. Our party of seven had made a reservation a week in advance, yet we were seated at the back of the far dining room, right next to a door, where the clatter of pots, pans, and dishes was audible throughout our meal, along with a sound resembling Darth Vader coming from the ceiling vent. Our waitress was not familiar with all the menu items, and three of our simple requests were not fulfilled as ordered (such as dressing on the side, less feta on one dish, and no oil on another). The waitress attributed the errors to the cook but did offer to have one dish corrected. Additionally, we were asked if we wanted pine nuts, feta, or Gorgonzola on our salad and were then charged an extra $2.00 per item on top of the $6.99 salad price, bringing the total to $10.99! Outrageous! The restaurant has a lot of potential for ambiance, but they fall short in terms of service and atmosphere. I am unlikely to return. The quality and service are not worth the high prices.

text: Papa J's is expensive and inconsistent.  The ambiance is nice but it doesn't justify the prices.  There are better restaurants in Carnegie.
paraphrase: Papa J's charges a lot and lacks consistency. The atmosphere is pleasant, but it doesn't warrant the high prices. Carnegie has superior dining options.

text:
I was pleasantly surprised by this restaurant.  Came here with no expectations.  

I was greeted by a large open (garage style) window, that opened the whole lounge bar area to the street.  It had modern furnishings and the staff were quite friendly.

Ordered the quattro formaggi pizza.  Quite good!!!  Great balance of cheeses.  Not too rich at all.  Crust was excellent too.  Can't wait to come back.
paraphrase: I didn't know what to expect when I visited this restaurant, but it pleasantly surprised me. Upon arrival, I was welcomed by a large open (garage-style) window that connected the entire lounge bar area with the street. The place had modern decor, and the staff were very friendly. I tried the quattro formaggi pizza, and it was really good! The cheeses were perfectly balanced, not overly rich, and the crust was excellent. I'm looking forward to returning.

text: Absolutely Awful Service.  Went to Papa J's awhile ago and the service was bad - the waiter was surly, rude and unhelpful..  Decided to try it again since it is close to Stage 62.  Big mistake.  Greeted by a dour manager that seemed mad that we had a reservation. 30 minutes to get a glass of wine.  At one hour and 45 minutes complained to the manager that we still had not received out entrees.  He said 'What do you want me to do about it?\" and said they had a busy night.  This is when tables around us sat empty. I then had to go back to the manager to give us our bill so we could get away from this ruined evening.  The bill was prepared incorrectly. If you want average food and terrible service, this is your place.  We will never go back.
paraphrase: Terrible service experience. I visited Papa J's some time ago and found the service lacking—the waiter was unfriendly, impolite, and not helpful. I gave it another shot because it's near Stage 62, but it was a huge error. We were met by a gloomy manager who seemed annoyed about our reservation. It took 30 minutes just to get a glass of wine. After an hour and 45 minutes, I complained to the manager about not having received our entrees yet. He dismissively asked, "What do you want me to do about it?" and mentioned they were having a busy night, although many tables around us were vacant. I had to approach the manager again to request the bill so we could leave this ruined evening behind. The bill was also incorrectly prepared. If you're looking for mediocre food and dreadful service, this is the spot for you. We won't be returning.

text: I love this place! The food is always so fresh and delicious. The staff is always friendly, as well.
paraphrase: I adore this spot! The meals are consistently fresh and tasty. The staff is consistently welcoming, too.

text:
spinach/feta cheese salad:  B-
pesto alfredo penne :  :A+
Coconut Cake huge piece:  A+
bread small portions:  B
Service B 
host seemed grumpy, waitress good, but then she got busy .
PRICE: Pasta was $15 and the portion smallish more like a $12.99 dish 
ARRIVE EARLY ON SATURDAY NIGHT THIS PLACE PACKED after 6pm
paraphrase:
spinach and feta cheese salad: B-
pesto alfredo penne: A+
Coconut Cake large slice: A+
bread limited servings: B
Service: B
host appeared irritable, waitress was efficient, but later got overwhelmed.
PRICE: Pasta cost $15, yet the serving size was closer to that of a $12.99 meal.
ARRIVE EARLY ON SATURDAY NIGHT; THE LOCATION GETS CROWDED after 6pm.

text: It was just what we were looking for. The service was great. My husband had the veal sausage on green pepper appetizer in the larger size. It was great (spicy) and enough for a meal. I had the stuffed eggplant. That was very good but the sauce could have been a bit heartier. I would definitely go back. Really nice atmosphere and free parking in the back.
paraphrase: It was exactly what we wanted. The service was excellent. My husband ordered the larger portion of veal sausage with green pepper as an appetizer. It was deliciously spicy and substantial enough to be a meal. I chose the stuffed eggplant, which was very tasty, although the sauce could have been richer. I would certainly return. The atmosphere was very pleasant, and there was free parking available at the back.

text:
Finally, great Tuscan-style Italian food in Pittsburgh, not that tasteless stuff that locals think is Italian.

The bread was warm and fresh. The bolognese sauce was creamy and rich, and unlike most restaurants was made with veal. I love the fresh smell of baking bread throughout the restaurant.

I'm looking forward to working my way through the menu of chicken options, and next time, I'm saving room for dessert! The display of cakes and pastries at the door was amazing.
paraphrase:
At last, authentic Tuscan-style Italian cuisine in Pittsburgh, unlike the bland dishes some locals consider Italian.

The bread was warm and freshly baked. The bolognese sauce was creamy and flavorful, and uniquely made with veal. I enjoyed the fresh aroma of baking bread wafting through the restaurant.

I'm eager to try all the chicken dishes on the menu, and next time, I'll make sure to leave space for dessert! The array of cakes and pastries at the entrance was impressive.

text: Man has this place gone down hill.  My husband and I went a couple of weeks ago.  We had salads and pasta.  Both were on the verge of inedible.  With a single glass of wine, I felt downright robbed at the $50 price tag.  Seriously, I could have easily made what we had with jarred sauce and boxed pasta at home. Classico is better.  And the service was pretty absent to make it even worse... seemed she knew it was terrible and didn't want to deal with it.  Seriously, I'd rather eat at the OG.
paraphrase: Wow, this place has really deteriorated. My husband and I visited a few weeks back. We ordered salads and pasta, both of which were nearly inedible. Spending $50 for just one glass of wine left me feeling completely ripped off. Honestly, I could have prepared the same meal using jarred sauce and boxed pasta at home. Classico tastes superior. Plus, the service was almost nonexistent, as if the server was aware of how bad it was and chose to avoid it. Honestly, I'd prefer dining at the OG.

text: Talk about overpriced. $18 for a fairly basic pasta with some obviously frozen chicken chopped up over it. The latter was terrible, thin and flabby and rather unappealing. The pasta itself was ok, as was the sauce. The desserts are pretty good. But honestly, that is a $10 dish whose price has been inflated.
paraphrase: That's what I call overpriced. Paying $18 for a simple pasta dish topped with clearly frozen chicken is excessive. The chicken was awful—thin, limp, and quite unappetizing. The pasta and sauce were decent. The desserts are quite nice. However, this is essentially a $10 dish that's been marked up.

text: I've decided where I stand with Emmert Dental. They are unprofessional, rude and have no clue what they're doing (behind the desk). I will not be going back nor will I have nice things to say about them.
paraphrase: I've made up my mind about Emmert Dental. They lack professionalism, are impolite, and seem incompetent at their front desk duties. I won't return, and I won't speak positively about them.

text: THE WORST DENTAL EXPERIENCE OF MY LIFE.  THEY ARE BUTCHERS!  My husband and I went to Emmert Dental in Bethel Park for a routine cleaning and check up.  I had one tooth that I had a recent root canal on and a few small cavities that needed filled.  After the so called dentist - Dr Carnavali - did my exam, I needed 3 additional root canals and an extraction! An extraction on the tooth that just had a root canal. I was told that part of the tooth had broken off and that a crown was no longer possible.  I didn't know any better, so I agreed to schedule the extraction for a few weeks later. The \"dentist\" wanted to extract it that day, but I had plans for the weekend and was not about to be in pain. So, 2 weeks later, I went in for the extraction.  After they gave me novocaine they get me with \"you need a bone graft also in that area since we are extracting that tooth\". I have never had any issues with bone loss with previous extractions so I didn't feel it was necessary. I had 3 employees surrounding me, pressuring me for this bone graft. So, I reluctantly agreed. I had to pay $259.00 for this \"bone graft\". So, I signed everything. I wasn't even given enough time to read the document, so I should have known and got up and left right then and there. The \"dentist\" started the extraction. I have never been so uncomfortable in my life. He pulled on my tooth, used a metal instrument to pound on my tooth and was pulling so hard that my head was coming up and slamming back down against the head rest.  Finally, after a good 15 minutes of this torture, he ripped the tooth out of the side of my jaw. I was literally ganging from all the blood running down the back of my throat. He then stuck some pink powder up in the extraction area. I'm assuming this was the bone graft.  I ended up with many stitches. I was given an antibiotic and instructed to schedule an appointment for the following Saturday so they could start the bridge. Then I was hit with a bill of $569.00! My insurance covers 100% of extractions.  I was in so much pain for 2 weeks following the extraction. Pain severe enough that I had to call off work and I couldn't sleep at night.  I refused to go back to Emmert Dental.  And after a visit to a very reputable dentist, an endodontist and an oral surgeon, I have to have oral surgery to try and repair the damage Emmert Dental has done to my mouth.  They caused a very large deformity in my jaw and I am at risk to lose additional teeth.  Save yourself a lot of time, money and pain.  Emmert dental only cares about the money, will over charge you and leave you less than happy with the dental work.  They are butchers and should not even call themselves dentist. And if you have an issue with their billing practices, don't expect to that resolved easily.  I have called numerous times and I am always told that the manager and or billing department is not in.  I have contacted the BBB and a lawyer.  I expect and want a full refund.  No one should have to go thru what I am currently going thru.  They do unnecessary extractions so they can do a more expensive procedure such as a bridge or an implant.  Now I have to go thru oral surgery, more pain and suffering and more time missed from work because of Emmert Dental.  And the root canals they said I needed, my current dentist and my endodontist both agreed that I do not need tooth canals.  In fact, the teeth are perfectly healthy.  They are crooks and butchers.
paraphrase: THE MOST HORRIFIC DENTAL EXPERIENCE I'VE EVER HAD. THEY ARE NOTHING SHORT OF BUTCHERS! My husband and I visited Emmert Dental in Bethel Park for a routine cleaning and checkup. I had recently undergone a root canal on one tooth and had a few minor cavities that needed filling.
