However, after the so-called dentist, Dr. Carnavali, conducted my exam, I was suddenly told I needed three more root canals and an extraction! The extraction was for the same tooth that had just undergone a root canal. I was informed that a part of the tooth had broken off, making a crown impossible. Unaware of the implications, I agreed to schedule the extraction for a few weeks later. The "dentist" wanted to proceed immediately, but I had weekend plans and didn't want to endure the pain. Two weeks later, I returned for the extraction. Once numbed with Novocaine, I was confronted with a surprise: "you need a bone graft also in that area since we are extracting that tooth." Having never experienced bone loss with previous extractions, I felt it was unnecessary. Yet, surrounded by three employees pressuring me, I reluctantly consented. I paid $259.00 for this "bone graft." I signed the paperwork without enough time to read it fully, a clear warning sign to leave then and there. The "dentist" began the extraction, and I was in excruciating discomfort. He yanked on my tooth, pounded it with a metal tool, and pulled so forcefully that my head repeatedly slammed against the headrest. After 15 minutes of this ordeal, he finally tore the tooth from my jaw. I was nearly gagging from the blood pouring down my throat. He then applied some pink powder into the extraction site, presumably the bone graft. I ended up with numerous stitches and was prescribed antibiotics, with instructions to schedule a bridge procedure the following Saturday. Then came a bill for $569.00! My insurance fully covers extractions. The pain following the extraction was so intense for two weeks that I had to miss work and couldn't sleep. I refused to return to Emmert Dental. After consulting a reputable dentist, an endodontist, and an oral surgeon, I now require oral surgery to repair the damage inflicted by Emmert Dental. They have left me with a significant deformity in my jaw, risking the loss of more teeth. Spare yourself the time, money, and agony. Emmert Dental is solely motivated by profit, overcharges for their services, and leaves you dissatisfied with their work. They are butchers and should not even be considered dentists. If you encounter issues with their billing, don't expect an easy resolution. Repeated calls lead to responses that the manager or billing department is unavailable. I've reached out to the BBB and consulted a lawyer, seeking a full refund. No one should endure what I am currently experiencing. They perform unnecessary extractions to push more expensive procedures like bridges or implants. Now, because of Emmert Dental, I face oral surgery, additional pain, suffering, and missed work. As for the root canals they claimed I needed, both my current dentist and endodontist confirmed that they were unnecessary. In fact, the teeth are perfectly healthy. They are deceitful and butchers.

text: The worst dental office I ever been. No one can beat it!!! You should avoid it at any time.
paraphrase: This is the most terrible dental office I've ever encountered. It's unbeatable in its awfulness! You should steer clear of it always.

text:
My expectations for McDonalds are t rarely high. But for one to still fail so spectacularly...that takes something special!
The cashier took my friends's order, then promptly ignored me. I had to force myself in front of a cashier who opened his register to wait on the person BEHIND me. I waited over five minutes for a gigantic order that included precisely one kid's meal. After watching two people who ordered after me be handed their food, I asked where mine was. The manager started yelling at the cashiers for "serving off their orders" when they didn't have their food. But neither cashier was anywhere near those controls, and the manager was the one serving food to customers and clearing the boards.
The manager was rude when giving me my order. She didn't make sure that I had everything ON MY RECEIPT, and never even had the decency to apologize that I felt I was getting poor service.
I've eaten at various McDonalds restaurants for over 30 years. I've worked at more than one location. I expect bad days, bad moods, and the occasional mistake. But I have yet to have a decent experience at this store. It will remain a place I avoid unless someone in my party needs to avoid illness from low blood sugar. Perhaps I should go back to the racially biased service of Steak n Shake instead!
paraphrase: I don't usually hold high expectations for McDonald's, but to fail so miserably like this... that's quite remarkable! The cashier took my friend's order and then completely ignored me. I had to force my way to a cashier who had just opened his register for the person behind me. I waited over five minutes for a massive order that consisted of just one kid's meal. After seeing two people who ordered after me receive their food, I inquired about mine. The manager started shouting at the cashiers for "serving off their orders" when they didn't even have their food. However, neither cashier was in control of that, as the manager was the one distributing meals and clearing the boards. The manager was impolite when handing me my order, failing to verify that everything on my receipt was included and didn't bother to apologize for the poor service I received. I've been dining at various McDonald's locations for over 30 years and have worked at more than one. I understand there are bad days, bad moods, and occasional errors. Yet, I've never had a satisfactory experience at this particular store. I'll continue to avoid it unless someone with me needs to prevent a low blood sugar incident. Maybe I should return to the racially biased service at Steak n Shake instead!

text:
I've tried to give this McDonalds a chance but my patience has run out. The only reason why I came here as much as I did is because I like the $1 iced coffees. But it's not even worth it. This McDonalds is poo and so is most of the staff. 

Whenever I want to go through the drive-thru I can't because there is a line of cars wrapped half away around the building. And even when the line is shorter there's always a hold up of some sort. So most of the time I just park and go inside the restaurant. 

Today was a horrible day. I ordered an iced coffee (no cream or sugar) and a chocolate chip cookie. It only takes 30 seconds to fill a cup with coffee and 30 seconds to take a cookie out of the case. I waited 10 minutes!! Sorry but that's too long just to get a coffee and a cookie! It's not like I ordered a meal! During that time I saw people come in after me and get their milkshakes and iced coffees within two minutes! WTH?! I told the petite sleepy eyed cashier I wanted my money back! Her eyes opened wide in surprise, like I really woke her up or something. She said,"Youre the one who ordered the iced coffee? I can get it for you!" For real? She was the one who took my order! I told her I waited too long and I just want my money back. The manager offered to make my iced coffee, but  I refused because I had just had enough.

There are *few* employees there who work hard but everyone else is dragging their feet. There is no organization or teamwork, everyone is scrabbling. It looks like none of them want to be there. I used to work in fast food and I know it sucks, but come on!

I'm sorry but I can't recommend this Mcdonalds. It's poo. I'm not coming back here anymore. Guess I'll be going to Starbucks- and their iced coffees taste better anyway!
paraphrase:
I've attempted to give this McDonald's multiple chances, but my patience has finally worn thin. The main reason I frequented this location was for the $1 iced coffees, but even that isn't worth it anymore. This McDonald's is terrible, and so is the majority of the staff.

Whenever I attempt to use the drive-thru, there's a line of cars stretching halfway around the building. Even if the line is shorter, there's always some delay. So, most of the time, I end up parking and going inside.

Today was particularly awful. I ordered an iced coffee (no cream or sugar) and a chocolate chip cookie. It should take only 30 seconds to pour a coffee and another 30 seconds to grab a cookie. Yet, I waited 10 minutes! That's too long for just a coffee and a cookie. It's not like it was a full meal! During my wait, I watched as others, who came in after me, received their milkshakes and iced coffees in under two minutes. What on earth?! I told the small, sleepy-eyed cashier that I wanted a refund. Her eyes widened in surprise, as if I really startled her. She said, "You're the one who ordered the iced coffee? I can get it for you!" Seriously? She was the one who took my order in the first place! I told her that the wait was too long and I just wanted my money back. The manager offered to make my iced coffee, but I declined since I was fed up.

There are a few employees who work hard, but the rest seem to be sluggish. There's no organization or teamwork; everyone is scrambling around. It seems like none of them want to be there. I used to work in fast food, so I know it's not easy, but come on!

I'm sorry, but I can't recommend this McDonald's. It's awful. I'm not coming back. I guess I'll start going to Starbucks—and their iced coffees taste better anyway!

text:
We had the worst possible experience at the Eat N Park at Waterfront on 2/12/10 at around 10pm. Our waitress (Annemarie) was inattentive and got us the wrong order. We ended up with a dish we had not even ordered and still got charged for it on the check. 

One of my friends ordered a Buffalo Chicken Sandwich, which according to the menu description, should've had lettuce and tomato in it. But the sandwich that arrived on our table had none of those ingredients. When pointed out to the waitress, she just brought some lettuce and tomatoes in a plate! How crude! 

Spoke to the manager after paying the full check and first of all, she did not even offer to re-imburse for the item we did not even order, and that had been forcibly thrust upon us. 

When I asked the manager about the Buffalo Chicken sandwich not having any lettuce and tomato, she said they just got new menus and that description is not correct and that Buffalo Chicken Sandwich is supposed to be without lettuce and tomato! What a lame excuse! Even your online menu says under the description for a Buffalo Chicken sandwich that its supposed to have both those ingredients - "Served with lettuce and tomato on a fresh-baked Kaiser roll." - That's what the last line in the description of Buffalo Chicken Sandwich says. Shame on you Manager for lying! Instead of admitting her fault, she was blaming it to a new menu!

Not only did we have to pay for something that we didn't order or eat, but we got sub-standard food that was different from what was described in the menu, and had to deal with a really bad waitress and insensitive and deceitful manager to top off the whole experience. 

I am never going to come back to any Eat N Park location ever again, and will never recommend one to any of my friends either. What a waste of time and money! Plus dealing with callous and lying people - my goodness, I surely had my patience tested this evening!
paraphrase:
Our experience at the Eat N Park at Waterfront on 2/12/10 around 10pm was absolutely terrible. Our server, Annemarie, was not attentive and brought us the wrong order. We ended up receiving a dish we hadn't requested and were still billed for it. 

One of my friends ordered a Buffalo Chicken Sandwich, which the menu described as including lettuce and tomato. However, when the sandwich arrived, it lacked both ingredients. When we mentioned this to the waitress, she simply brought a plate of lettuce and tomatoes—how unsophisticated!

After paying the full bill, we spoke to the manager, who didn't even offer a refund for the item we hadn't ordered but were served anyway. 

When I inquired about the missing lettuce and tomato on the Buffalo Chicken Sandwich, the manager claimed they had new menus and the description was incorrect, asserting that the sandwich wasn’t supposed to include them. What a pathetic excuse! Even your online menu states that the sandwich is "Served with lettuce and tomato on a fresh-baked Kaiser roll." That's exactly what the description says. Shame on the manager for lying! Instead of admitting her mistake, she blamed a new menu.

Not only did we have to pay for something we neither ordered nor consumed, but the food we received was substandard and different from the menu description, compounded by a poor waitress and a dishonest, insensitive manager, making the entire experience dreadful.

I will never return to any Eat N Park location, nor will I recommend it to any of my friends. It was a complete waste of time and money! Plus, having to deal with uncaring and dishonest individuals—my patience was thoroughly tested this evening!

text: Mmm...peppermint mocha\n\nIn my quest to visit every Starbucks in the USA I have stopped at yet another location.  This one is located in the center of a circle road -- very cache and in a newer part of the area.
paraphrase:
Mmm...peppermint mocha

In my journey to visit every Starbucks across the USA, I've paused at yet another branch. This particular one sits right in the middle of a roundabout—very trendy and situated in a more recently developed section of the region.

text: If there was ever a \"get your coffee and go\" Starbucks, this is it. Right in the center of the Waterfront, this Starbucks has no sofa, comfy chairs, only table seating. I love the baristas here, But I don't like the location of the building.
paraphrase: This Starbucks is definitely a "grab your coffee and leave" kind of place. Located right in the heart of the Waterfront, it lacks sofas and comfy chairs, offering only table seating. I really appreciate the baristas here, but I'm not a fan of where the building is situated.

text:
This Starbucks is teeny-tiny!

Seating inside is VERY limited.  This is a Starbucks to grab and go and continue your shopping at the Waterfront.

Baristas are friendly and fast.
paraphrase:
This Starbucks location is quite small!

There is VERY little seating available indoors. It's a spot where you grab your coffee and head back to your shopping at the Waterfront.

The baristas are pleasant and efficient.

text:
As far as Starbucks go, this is a pretty nice one.  The baristas are friendly and while I was here, a lot of regulars must have come in, because they bantered away with almost everyone.  The bathroom was clean and well maintained and the trash wasn't overflowing in the canisters around the store.  The pastries looked fresh, but I didn't partake.  The noise level was also at a nice working level - not too loud, music just barely audible.

I do wish there was more seating.  It is nice that this location has a counter at the end of the bar for sole workers, but it doesn't replace more tables.  I'm sure this isn't as much of a problem in the summer when there's the space outside.

There was a treat receipt promo going on, but the barista didn't tell me about it, which I found odd.  Usually when they have promos like that going on, they ask everyone if they want their receipt to come back later in the day to claim whatever the offer is.  Today it was one of their new pastries for $1, I know in the summer they do $2 grande iced drinks with that morning's receipt.

Overall, nice working or socializing environment.  Very friendly and inviting.  It's what I've come to expect from Starbucks, so points for consistency.
paraphrase:
For a Starbucks location, this one is quite pleasant. The baristas are welcoming, and during my visit, it seemed many regulars came in, as they chatted with nearly everyone. The restroom was clean and well-kept, and the trash bins throughout the store were not overflowing. The pastries appeared fresh, although I didn't try any. The noise level was conducive to work—not too loud, with the music just barely audible.

I do wish there were more seating options. It's nice that there is a counter at the end of the bar for individual workers, but it doesn't make up for the lack of tables. I'm sure this is less of an issue in the summer when there's outdoor space available.

There was a treat receipt promotion happening, but the barista didn't inform me about it, which I found unusual. Usually, when such promotions are active, they ask if customers want their receipt to return later and take advantage of the offer. Today, it was one of their new pastries for $1. In the summer, they offer $2 grande iced drinks with that morning's receipt.

Overall, it's a nice place for working or socializing, very friendly and welcoming. It aligns with what I expect from Starbucks, so they get points for consistency.

text: I'm not a huge fan of this location. I think that it was oddly built- the small, alley-like front makes it difficult to get past people on your way to/from the bathroom or tables when it's busy. And there's hardly any tables to sit at. Furthermore, people tend to clog the front on their way in, which makes things particularly difficult (especially in the winter weather). The staff were pretty impersonal to be, but maybe that was due to the high traffic of the place and the time I was there. And the coffee that I had was cold- I'm sure it was probably the bottom of the batch. I'd probably only walk in here again if someone else suggested it before or after a movie or while we were shopping in the area.
paraphrase: I'm not particularly fond of this spot. It seems oddly designed—the narrow, alley-like entrance makes navigating around people tricky when heading to or from the bathroom or tables during busy times. There are also very few tables available. Additionally, people tend to block the entrance as they come in, which is especially problematic in winter. The staff seemed rather impersonal, though that might have been due to the place being crowded and the time I visited. The coffee I had was cold, likely the last of the batch. I'd probably only return if someone else suggested it while we were in the area for a movie or shopping.

text: This Starbucks is 5-Star Friendly. The shift supervisor Lucious keeps the energy in there moving and light. He recently helped me with a merchandise return and embodied everything a good manager and all around mensch person should - patience, kindness, generosity and fairness. I left that afternoon loving Starbucks even more than when I went in. It's great that a company and their employees can be as loyal to their customers as their customers are to them.
paraphrase: This Starbucks location offers a 5-star friendly experience. Shift supervisor Lucious ensures the atmosphere is lively and welcoming. He recently assisted me with a merchandise return and exemplified the qualities of an excellent manager and genuinely good person—patience, kindness, generosity, and fairness. I departed that day with an even greater appreciation for Starbucks than I had when I arrived. It's wonderful to see a company and its staff being as devoted to their customers as the customers are to them.

text:
For someone who straddles the line between sometimes craft-er, this store can seem vast and confusing.  There's rows and rows of baskets and bedazzling jewels and paper and...oh my...scrap booking!  When I want to find boring old felt....it's like...where do I start?  

I'm in a hurry, as always, and I rush in and the first thing I look for is an employee.  No one to be found.  I could call out and my voice would come echoing back.  I run between rows of paper, wood and gosh darn scrap booking....no felt!  Where do I find felt!  Where do I find an employee!?

FINALLY, I find one after 15 minutes of wondering around.  She's incredibly nice and leads me to the felt.  This is not the first time I've been hard up to find help.  I tell her she was hard to find, and she tells me that they're "low on payroll" and can't have any one the floor BUT HER!  In that WHOLE store, JUST HER!  That's ridic!

Not a month ago I was, again, in a slight hurry and stood in line for about 15 minutes to check out, with 2 employees at the register and 6 people per line, I gave up and walked out.  

Hey Michael, do you pick up what I'm putting down?  You're not making any money because your costumer service SUCKS....in fact, there's no service to be found!
paraphrase:
For someone who occasionally indulges in crafting, this store can feel overwhelming and disorganized. There's an endless array of baskets, sparkling jewels, paper, and... oh my... scrapbooking! When I need to find something as simple as felt, I have no idea where to begin.

I'm always in a rush, so I dart in and immediately search for an employee. No one is around. I could shout, and my voice would just echo back at me. I dash between rows of paper, wood, and, of course, scrapbooking supplies... but no felt! How do I locate felt? How do I find an employee?

After wandering for 15 minutes, I finally find one. She's extremely kind and guides me to the felt. This isn't the first time I've struggled to find assistance. I mention to her how difficult it was to find help, and she explains they're "low on payroll" and she's the only one on the floor! In the entire store, just her! That's absurd!

Less than a month ago, I was in a bit of a hurry again and waited about 15 minutes in line to check out, with two employees at the register and six people in each line. I eventually gave up and left.

Hey Michael, do you understand what I'm saying? You’re losing money because your customer service is terrible... in fact, there's no service at all!

text:
I have been frequently shopping at this Michaels store over the last couple months since I began taking a cake decorating class and hosted a large baby shower at my home. The store has typically had the supplies that I need from cake and cupcake baking and icing to scrap booking supplies. This location is pretty average compared to other Michaels - it's exactly what you would expect when going to this store. It's not a large store, but it's not a small store -- just average.

I do enjoy using the Michael's coupons when shopping. I definitely save a lot of money! Instead of toting around the paper versions, I actually have the iPhone app that has coupons available to use from the app. 

My only complaint is that sometimes it takes a while to check out at this location. I don't normally have a problem - usually only one or two people ahead of me - but sometimes it can be much slower. Today, for example, there were only two customers ahead of me, but one of them was having issues with a coupon. The line began to get longer and longer, and though a couple associates were lingering up front, they kept calling for someone else. From personal experience of being a manger in a retail store, do not linger or let your associates linger near the cashiers without jumping on register. Whatever the reason may be that you are not helping customers, it still appears rude and make everyone more impatient....
paraphrase:
Over the past few months, I've been regularly visiting this Michaels store since I started a cake decorating class and hosted a big baby shower at my house. Generally, the store carries the items I need, from cake and cupcake baking and icing supplies to scrapbooking materials. This particular location is quite typical compared to other Michaels stores—it's exactly what you'd expect. It's neither large nor small—just average in size.

I really appreciate using the Michael's coupons when shopping. I manage to save a significant amount! Instead of carrying the paper versions, I use the iPhone app, which offers coupons directly through the app.

My only issue is that sometimes the checkout process can be slow at this location. Usually, there's just one or two people ahead of me, so it's not a big problem, but occasionally it can be much slower. Today, for instance, there were only two customers ahead of me, but one had a coupon issue. The line started to grow longer, and even though a couple of associates were hanging around up front, they kept calling for someone else. From my personal experience as a retail store manager, it's important not to linger near the cashiers without jumping on the register. Regardless of the reason for not assisting customers, it still comes across as rude and makes everyone more impatient.

text:
The one good thing I can say about Michael's is that they have a wide variety of hobbies and crafts included in their selection. They've got items for woodwork, painting, knitting, kids crafts, scrapbooks, you name it. The problem is, the variety of products for your particular craft is probably dismal. They've got lots of cool items all over the store that you're convinced that somehow you need. (Because it is soooo cute, and you could TOTALLY do something with it. Right..?) The store feels cluttered because there is so much stuff everywhere. However, if you're looking for something specific, good luck. It's not even a matter of the product being difficult to find in the store. It's that when you find the right section, there's either nothing in stock, or they simply don't even carry what you need. 

I stopped in for crochet supplies, and the yarn selection was laughable compared to many other craft stores. I ended up going elsewhere for my stuff anyway. 

Plus the prices at Michael's are significantly more than other area craft stores. I usually only stop there if I'm already at tend Waterfront. I'd recommend checking other craft stores first. Unless you need glitter. Especially fine glitter. Michael's has the best glitter selection/quality of any store I've ever been to.
paraphrase:
The only positive thing I can mention about Michael's is their extensive range of hobbies and crafts in stock. They have materials for woodworking, painting, knitting, children's crafts, scrapbooking, and more. However, the selection for any specific craft is often lacking. The store is filled with numerous enticing items that seem essential simply because they are so appealing (and you imagine you could definitely create something with them, right?). The store appears cluttered due to the abundance of items. But if you're searching for something particular, good luck with that. It's not just about the difficulty in locating the product in the store; it's often either out of stock or not available at all in your desired section.

When I went in for crochet supplies, the yarn selection was quite disappointing compared to other craft stores. I ended up purchasing my supplies elsewhere.

Additionally, Michael's prices are considerably higher than those at other local craft stores. I generally only visit if I'm already near the Waterfront. I suggest checking other craft stores first. Unless you need glitter, especially fine glitter, as Michael's offers the best selection and quality of any store I've visited.

text:
This is the absolute WORST Steak N Shake I've ever been to. 

The bf and I got lost around Pittsburgh for 40 min. trying to find this location and on top of the unnecessary 1 hour wait for our food, the food itself was just so crappily made. It was so bad and we had waited a ridiculous amount of time (c'mon, Steak N Shake is basically a glorified McD's), our waiter comped our WHOLE MEAL.

If not for the compensation, I would have been pissed that we had to pay for such a crappy meal. The bf, on the other hand, was more upset about the principle of the matter... we had trekked all that way just to get our Steak N Shake fix (since both of us haven't had it since our undergraduate days) and it was a major fail.

I think I'm good with my Steak N Shake craving for a long time now...



PS. The whole joint smells like major B.O.
paraphrase:
This is by far the WORST Steak N Shake I've visited. My boyfriend and I wandered around Pittsburgh for 40 minutes trying to locate this place, and after an unnecessary hour-long wait, the food we received was poorly prepared. It was incredibly bad, and after waiting an absurd amount of time (come on, Steak N Shake is essentially a souped-up McDonald's), our server ended up comping our ENTIRE MEAL. 

Without the compensation, I would have been angry about paying for such a terrible meal. Meanwhile, my boyfriend was more upset about the principle of the situation... we had traveled all that way to satisfy our Steak N Shake craving (since neither of us had had it since our college days), and it was a huge letdown. 

I think I'm satisfied with not having Steak N Shake for a long time now...

P.S. The whole place reeks of intense body odor.

text: I went here at 3 PM between the lunch rush and the dinner rush, and the restaurant was maybe 20% full. I ordered the Chili Mac Supreme to go. I thought I would be in and out within 5-10 minutes. Keep in mind that this restaurant even has a drive-thru; the expectation is quick service.\n\nLong story short, my food never came... the waitress told me (after I had been sitting there waiting for thirty minutes) that the kitchen hadn't even started on my order yet, so I told her to cancel it and walked out. Now I see that others on Yelp have warned about the slow service at this location. Wish I had looked it up earlier.
paraphrase: I arrived at this restaurant at 3 PM, right between the busy lunch and dinner hours, and it was only about 20% occupied. I placed a to-go order for the Chili Mac Supreme, expecting to be out the door in 5-10 minutes. Given that they even have a drive-thru, I anticipated fast service. However, my food never arrived. After waiting for thirty minutes, the waitress informed me that the kitchen hadn't even started preparing my order, so I asked her to cancel it and left. I later discovered on Yelp that others have reported slow service at this location. I wish I had checked the reviews beforehand.

text: In general I do like Shake N' Steak, but this location is a hit or miss location!  You never know what kind of quality or service you're going to find here.  A friend and myself went a few weeks back after a movie and it had to be one of the worst trips there EVER!  You can't entirely blame the waitress since she was the only one there for the entire place...poor scheduling on the manager's part. However, while she can't be accountable for the slooooow service, she was accountable for both orders being incorrect.  The burgers were over cooked and the fries were soggie and the milkshake was runny at best...\n\nBy far my worst visit to Steak n' Shake!
paraphrase: Overall, I do like Shake N' Steak, but this particular location can be unpredictable. You never really know what quality or service to expect. A friend and I visited a few weeks ago after a movie, and it turned out to be one of the worst experiences I've had there! The waitress can't be fully blamed since she was the only staff member for the whole place, which shows poor scheduling by the manager. While she wasn't responsible for the very slow service, she was at fault for both orders being wrong. The burgers were overcooked, the fries were soggy, and the milkshake was extremely runny. This was undoubtedly my worst visit to Steak n' Shake!

text: Rather typical SnS.  Had a good lunch crowd.  Milkshake was good but not as good as EnP down the street.  It took to long to get the burger for some reason, 25 minutes, I realized cooked to order but this is a little long for SnS.  Ordered the Guacamole Steakburger and it only had a small portion of Gauc...not your usual amount..kitchen was not up to speed on portion sizing for some reason.  Definitely did not look like the picture on the website.  Oh well!
paraphrase: Pretty standard SnS experience. The lunch crowd was decent. The milkshake was enjoyable, though not as good as the one at EnP nearby. The wait for the burger was unusually long, about 25 minutes, which seemed excessive for SnS even considering it's cooked to order. I got the Guacamole Steakburger, but it came with just a small amount of guacamole—not the usual serving size. The kitchen didn't seem to have the portion sizes right for some reason. It definitely didn't match the picture on the website. Oh well!

text: Ah, Steak n Shake, you hit the spot after a night of drinking. Open 24 hours, drive thru or dine in, with wonderful, cheap greasy burgers, chicken fingers, breakfast sandwiches, and shoestring fries. You're faster (and cheaper!) than Eat N Park. Plus your employees wear those adorable bow ties. Granted, if we were at a fancy party, I might act like I don't know you, but deep down inside you know I heart you.
paraphrase: Ah, Steak n Shake, you really satisfy the cravings after a night out drinking. Open 24/7, you offer drive-thru or dine-in options with delightful, inexpensive greasy burgers, chicken fingers, breakfast sandwiches, and shoestring fries. You're quicker (and more affordable!) than Eat N Park. And your staff wears those charming bow ties. Sure, if we were at a high-class event, I might pretend not to recognize you, but deep down, you know I adore you.

text: Terrible wait staff  couldn't even seat us. Before we, and another party walked out without even being greeted properly, I observed several employees traipse out the front door, several tables who were waiting for their food for a long time,  several employees just standing around out of direct view of the tables, a morbidly obese hostess that could barely physically walk from one end of the store to the other. I think one server was high. \n  \n  Going there was a  special request by a 7 year old getting over a long-ish illness, so that makes the shabby treatment more enraging.
paraphrase: The incompetent wait staff failed to seat us, and before we, along with another group, left without a proper greeting, I noticed several employees leaving through the front door, numerous tables waiting endlessly for their food, and several staff members idling out of sight from the tables. The hostess, who was extremely overweight, struggled to walk across the restaurant. I suspect one server was under the influence. Our visit was a special request by a 7-year-old recovering from a lengthy illness, which made the poor service even more infuriating.

text: This place is s l o w....like so slow I have got up and left after waiting 30 minutes and not even being acknowledged by a server. The food is okay. Def not worth the wait. Milkshakes are just okay as well. Booooooo.
paraphrase: This location moves at a snail's pace... it's so sluggish that I've left after 30 minutes without even being noticed by a server. The food is mediocre and certainly not worth the delay. Milkshakes are just average too. Booooooo.

text:
Fast food done right, what more can you want?

A throwback to old timey diners, steak and shake is where you get a decent meal at a good price. 

My sisters and I arrived on a Tuesday afternoon around 3pm catching their 2-4 happy hour, which we read on the door upon entry; a lovely surprise. This meant that all shakes and drinks were 50% off! 

I ordered the western BBQ burger, which was 2 thin patties, cheese, bacon, BBQ sauce, and fried onions. It came with shoe string fries (my favorite), all for the low price of $4.79. Are you kidding me? What's not to like! The food was actually pretty good! I liked it a lot. Since it was happy hour I also "splurged" on a chocolate covered strawberry shake. It was meh. Could have gone without it. The overpowering taste of artificial strawberry really turned me off, but the hot fudge saved it. But, how can you really go wrong with hot fudge?

All in all, 3 meals and 3 shakes came to $20. A steal. Also the food is good! I enjoyed it. I'd come back.
paraphrase:
Fast food done well, what else could you ask for?

Reminiscent of classic diners, Steak 'n Shake is the place to enjoy a satisfying meal at an affordable price.

My sisters and I arrived on a Tuesday afternoon around 3 PM, right in time for their 2-4 PM happy hour, which we noticed on the door as we entered—a pleasant surprise. This meant that all shakes and beverages were 50% off!

I chose the Western BBQ Burger, which included two thin patties, cheese, bacon, BBQ sauce, and fried onions. It was served with shoestring fries (my favorite), all for just $4.79. Seriously, what’s not to like? The food was genuinely good! I really enjoyed it. Since it was happy hour, I also decided to "indulge" in a chocolate-covered strawberry shake. It was just okay. I could have skipped it. The strong artificial strawberry flavor was off-putting, but the hot fudge was its saving grace. But really, can you go wrong with hot fudge?

In summary, 3 meals and 3 shakes totaled $20. A bargain. Plus, the food is good! I liked it. I’d return.

text: The food is good. Unfortunately the service is very hit or miss. The main issue seems to be with the kitchen, the waiters and waitresses are often very apologetic for the long waits and it's pretty obvious that some of them avoid the tables after taking the initial order to avoid hearing complaints.
paraphrase: The food is enjoyable, but the service can be quite inconsistent. The primary problem appears to be in the kitchen, as the servers frequently apologize for the lengthy delays, and it’s clear that some of them steer clear of the tables after taking orders to dodge complaints.

text: The service was fast but the food was terrible and so was the service. I had a bus to catch so I couldn't wait around waiting for the check so I went to the counter to pay. She never brought my check, never checked to see if my drink needed refilled, she just mopped the floors. \n\nThe burger was really greasy, the fries were Luke warm and the cheese on them wasn't as flavorful as it should be. As a new Pittsburgher I'm not impressed.
paraphrase: The service was quick, yet the food and service were awful. I had a bus to catch, so I couldn't wait for the check and went to the counter to pay. She didn't bring my check, didn't check if my drink needed a refill, and just focused on mopping the floors. The burger was excessively greasy, the fries were lukewarm, and the cheese lacked flavor. As someone new to Pittsburgh, I'm not impressed.

text: Burgers are not the best quality. Better than McDonald's but not much better. The fries are fairly thin, but crispy. They aren't bad. The prices are also quite decent. However, their shakes are amazing. Thick and creamy. It's also great that they have half off shakes on weekdays in the early morning like 1-3am.
paraphrase: The burgers aren't top-notch, though they slightly surpass McDonald's. The fries are thin yet crispy and are okay. The prices are reasonable. The shakes, on the other hand, are exceptional—thick and creamy. It's a bonus that they offer half-priced shakes on weekdays during the early morning hours, from 1 to 3 am.

text: Every time we come here the service is laughably bad. On this visit a tabe which was sat after us had their food before our server graced us with her surly presence. Good restaurant elsewhere, just avoid this location if you want to get your food inside an hour.
paraphrase: Each time we visit this place, the service is ridiculously poor. During this visit, a table that was seated after us received their food before our server appeared with her unpleasant demeanor. There are better branches of this restaurant, so steer clear of this location if you want to receive your meal within an hour.

text: Wow. Dirty and slow.  The floors felt like they had the days burger grease spilled all over, and it took 30 m8n to get our take out order.
paraphrase: Wow. Filthy and sluggish. The floors felt as if they were coated in the day's burger grease, and it took 30 minutes to receive our takeout order.

text: This location is terrible. The drive-thru workers are rude and they give you crap food. I waited 5 minutes before being able to order, even when there was only 1 car ahead of me. Then the lady proceeded to ask me if I would like anything on my burger, and (thinking the pictures on the menu are what they're supposed to look like and they would never false advertise) I asked \"doesn't it come with the fixings like in the picture?\" she then told me it only comes with cheese. So I then went on to tell her what I wanted on my burger. Pulled around and got my order pretty quick, but later when I open my burger its just meat and cheese. The meat is badly burned and cold. It was clear to me this burger was not fresh. I didn't even attempt to eat it, what a waste of a place. They definitely need better workers.
paraphrase: This place is awful. The drive-thru staff are impolite and serve terrible food. I had to wait 5 minutes to place my order, despite there being only one car in front of me. When I finally got to order, the woman asked if I wanted anything on my burger. Assuming the menu pictures were accurate and not misleading, I asked, "Doesn't it come with the toppings shown?" She informed me that it only includes cheese. So, I specified what I wanted on my burger. I received my order quickly, but when I opened my burger later, it was just meat and cheese. The meat was overcooked and cold, clearly not fresh. I didn't even try to eat it. This place is a complete waste. They definitely need better employees.

text: Steak 'n Shake's food is pretty good and the prices aren't bad for the quantity of food. I ordered a grilled chicken salad with no dressing. The salad looked thrown together and was $5.49 for 2 croutons, tomatoes, carrots, cabbage, lettuce and chicken breast. (They forgot the cheese!) The staff wasn't very friendly or helpful and seemed like they didn't want to be there. There usually is a long wait at Steak 'n Shake, but if you catch them when they aren't busy, you will breeze through- drive-thru that is. I do like their food and you can eat healthy options, but the staff isn't helpful and the quality isn't always there.
paraphrase: Steak 'n Shake offers decent food, and the prices are reasonable given the portion sizes. I got a grilled chicken salad without dressing. It appeared haphazardly assembled and cost $5.49 for a few croutons, tomatoes, carrots, cabbage, lettuce, and chicken breast. (They forgot to add the cheese!) The staff wasn't very friendly or attentive and seemed uninterested in being there. There's often a long wait at Steak 'n Shake, but if you visit during a less busy time, you can quickly get through the drive-thru. I enjoy their food and appreciate the healthy options, but the service is lacking and the quality can be inconsistent.

text: It's okay. Food is cheap but portions are small. For example I order the chicken tenders, and I got 3 little tiny tenders, and a decent amount of fries. Also the restaurant is not really clean either.  This restaurant is not my number 1 choice.
paraphrase: It's alright. The food is inexpensive, but the servings are small. For instance, when I ordered the chicken tenders, I received three very small tenders and a reasonable portion of fries. Additionally, the restaurant isn't particularly clean. This place isn't my top pick.

text:
As far as I'm concerned, Filene's Basement is good for two things: cheap dresses and cheap accessories.

As a young performer, I need good-looking dresses for frequent auditions -- and I need a couple, so I'm not wearing the same thing every time. The dresses on the second floor usually range from $30-60 and are good for over a year. It can be hard to find a full-length dress that's not reminiscent of a prom dress, but their selection of cocktail-length dresses is ample.

As far as accessories go, Filene's tends to have cheap versions of popular trends. If I feel the need to stock my wardrobe with a transient fad, Filene's is the place to go.
paraphrase: In my opinion, Filene's Basement serves two main purposes: affordable dresses and budget-friendly accessories. As a young performer, I require stylish dresses for frequent auditions, and I need a variety so I'm not repeating outfits every time. The dresses on the second floor usually cost between $30 and $60 and last over a year. While finding a full-length dress that doesn't look like a prom dress can be challenging, their selection of cocktail-length dresses is extensive. Regarding accessories, Filene's often has inexpensive versions of trendy items. If I want to add a temporary fashion trend to my wardrobe, Filene's is the place to visit.

text:
Even when we didn't have a car Filene's Basement was worth the bus trip to the Waterfront. I always find something (usually I find 3-4 things and spend about $60) and better still, I am always still wearing the clothes and shoes 3 months later. 

I kind of suspect this is the best shopping in Pittsburgh; it's much better than the usual department stores, better than Marshall's and TJ Maxx and better than the Saks downtown, even when it has a sale. Selection, bargains AND quality.

I like this Filene's better than Gabriel Brothers, which are harder to get to. Gabriel Brothers are a real discount shopper's challenge and I'm afraid I didn't live in Pittsburgh long enough to develop the necessary skills . . . Filene's was still up and running in June 2007 when I left town.
paraphrase:
Even when we lacked a car, Filene's Basement made the bus journey to the Waterfront worthwhile. I always discover something there (typically 3-4 items for around $60), and what’s even better is that I’m still wearing those clothes and shoes three months later. 

I have a feeling this might be the top shopping spot in Pittsburgh; it surpasses the standard department stores, Marshall’s, TJ Maxx, and even the downtown Saks, even during sales. The selection, deals, and quality are superior.

I prefer this Filene’s over Gabriel Brothers, which are more difficult to reach. Gabriel Brothers present a true challenge for discount shoppers, and I didn't stay in Pittsburgh long enough to hone those skills. Filene's was still operating in June 2007 when I moved away.

text: Love this store!  Don't always have much luck with the sales, but I always find something I like here.  This isn't your average Marshall's or TJ Max.  I think the green dress I'm wearing is from Filene's!
paraphrase: I really like this store! I don't always have great luck with the sales, but I can always find something I enjoy here. This place isn't your typical Marshall's or TJ Maxx. I'm pretty sure the green dress I'm wearing is from Filene's!

text: In my opinion, Sing Sing is really not worth it. It gets expensive because you drink, maybe start to sing a few lyrics, drink some more, think of a song you wanna hear, put $5 and title of said song on a piece of paper, you drink, they play it, everyone else is trying to get their songs played so you put $10 on your next sticky note...and so on and so forth. I wasn't really into it when I first got there but as the night went on and lager went in I started to warm up to the place. I feel that if I have to drink in order to have a good time, it's not worth it. But again, that's just me.\n\nThe 2 stars are for the piano players, who are entertaining and very talented, and the Rock Bottom brews they serve.
paraphrase: I personally don't think Sing Sing is worthwhile. The costs add up as you drink, maybe sing a few lyrics, drink more, think of a song you want to hear, put $5 and the song title on a note, drink again, they play it, and with everyone else trying to get their songs played, you end up putting $10 on your next note... and it continues like that. Initially, I wasn't too keen on the place, but as the night progressed and I drank more lager, I began to enjoy it. However, needing to drink to have a good time makes it not worth it for me. But that's just my perspective. The 2-star rating is for the entertaining and talented piano players and the Rock Bottom brews they offer.

text: Sing Sing is awesome!! Give a buck or more and they will play anything, and if they don't know it, they will search for who requested the song so they can do something else the person will enjoy! :) Great humor too!! :)
paraphrase: Sing Sing is fantastic!! Donate a dollar or more, and they'll play any song, and if they aren't familiar with it, they'll find the requester to offer an alternative they'll like! :) They have a wonderful sense of humor too!! :)

text:
What a great time!  This place was awesome!  I wish I lived right down the road, because I could see myself being a regular here.

Honestly, these guys have got some talent, like real talent.  They can play the piano, sing, play guitar, and the drums.  Did I mention that they are all pretty hilarious, too.  I'd easily pay the $7 cover to get in here any day because it really is worth it.  

I am sure I will be back in no time at all.  Next time, I am bringing a big group of friends.
paraphrase:
What an amazing experience! This place was incredible! I wish I lived nearby because I could see myself coming here often.

Truly, these folks are exceptionally talented. They can play the piano, sing, handle the guitar, and the drums, all while being quite funny. I'd gladly pay the $7 cover to enter here any day; it's definitely worth it.

I'm certain I'll return soon. Next time, I'll bring a large group of friends.

text: This place has gone significantly down hill.  I've gone to Sing Sing about once a year for the last 6 or 7 years.  When we started going there you couldn't get a seat unless you got there early.  It was always packed.  However, each year the crowd seems to get smaller and smaller.  Crowd is an important component of a piano bar due to the interactive nature of it all.  I was there last Saturday night (8/4/12) and there was about 20-30 people there.  Very slow and boring.  It appears the crowd is no longer allowed up on stage, taking a lot of the interactive fun out of what this place used to be.  This was likely my last time at Sing Sing.  Save your $7 and skip this place.  A real shame because this place was once a lot of fun.
paraphrase: This venue has deteriorated significantly. I've visited Sing Sing annually for the past 6 or 7 years. Initially, you had to arrive early to find a seat as it was always bustling. However, each year, the number of patrons seems to dwindle. A crowd is crucial for a piano bar because of its interactive nature. When I went last Saturday night (8/4/12), there were only about 20-30 people present. It was quite slow and dull. It seems that the audience is no longer permitted on stage, which removes much of the interactive enjoyment that this place used to offer. This was probably my last visit to Sing Sing. Save your $7 and avoid this place. It's really unfortunate because it used to be a lot of fun.

text:
Went here for the first time this past weekend. Needless to say I'll be returning! It was a blast!!!

It was cheap to get in and since we got there a bit earlier we got a semi-good table. Though it was very annoying that people had reserved tables right up front and never showed up. WHAT IS WRONG WITH THESE PEOPLE?! Everything if you ask me! Seriously though if you're going to reserve a table, you better freaking go! 

I had no complaints about the place itself! The guys were hilarious and watching my brother get made fun of multiple times was just the cherry on top! Anddd this has to be the only place I've ever enjoyed being around people who were sloppy drunk, because the comedians take no prisoners! 

I did enjoy when Matt and Joey were the ones up there because they are just amazing and make it so much better! They are great at what they do. Can't wait to go back!
paraphrase:
I visited this place for the first time over the weekend and I'm definitely going back! I had an amazing time!!! The entry fee was low, and since we arrived a bit early, we managed to get a decent table. However, it was really frustrating that some people had reserved tables at the front but never showed up. What's wrong with them?! Seriously, if you reserve a table, you should definitely show up! 

I had no issues with the venue itself! The entertainers were hilarious, and seeing my brother get teased repeatedly was just the icing on the cake! And honestly, this is the only place where I've actually enjoyed being around people who were very drunk, because the comedians spared no one! 

I especially loved when Matt and Joey were performing; they are simply fantastic and make the experience even better! They truly excel at what they do. I can't wait to return!

text:
Always a good time, although it's definitely not a place I would visit often. It's great for groups....which is quite obvious since there are always several folks there for bachelorette  parties and birthdays. 

Shut out to the piano players, who can not only play the piano, but also sing, play the drums and the sax. They are funny and know how to keep a crowd entertained. Also know some pretty obscure songs. 

My big criticism is the set up. If you don't have a reservation and/or come later in the evening, it's difficult to get seats. Believe me, I don't mind standing, but it does become a bit awkward when you have to keep scooting over to let waitresses and patrons past you. If you do find a seat, it is often littered with someone else's empty glasses.
paraphrase:
It's always enjoyable, though it's not somewhere I'd go frequently. It's perfect for groups, which is evident given the number of people there for bachelorette parties and birthdays. 

Kudos to the piano players, who not only play the piano but also sing, drum, and play the saxophone. They're amusing and skilled at entertaining the crowd, with a repertoire that includes some pretty obscure songs. 

My main issue is the layout. Without a reservation or if you arrive later in the evening, finding seats is challenging. I don't mind standing, but it gets a bit awkward when you constantly have to move aside for waitresses and other patrons. If you do get a seat, it often comes with someone else's empty glasses.

text: The musicians at this place work hard to entertain the crowd, so I can't say they are all bad.  However the minimum for a song request is $5.  I spent $20 on a song for my girlfriend and they NEVER played it!!  They were playing songs for $5 and $10, but my $40 got me no where.  Even if they didn't know the song, which I doubt, they could have picked one they knew and improvised,  my tip, save your $$ on the requests.\n\nOtherwise it's fun for special events every once in awhile.
paraphrase: The performers at this venue put in a lot of effort to keep the audience entertained, so I can't say they're all terrible. However, the minimum cost for a song request is $5. I spent $20 on a song for my girlfriend, and it was NEVER played! They played songs for $5 and $10, but my $40 was wasted. Even if they weren't familiar with the song, which I highly doubt, they could have chosen one they knew and improvised. My advice: hold on to your money when it comes to requests. Otherwise, it's an enjoyable place for special occasions now and then.

text: I went here with a bachelorette party. We had reservations, which was good because it was packed that Saturday night. The performers were very talented, but the acoustics were strangely off.   The theme that ran through the whole show was, to quote some phrases they kept repeating, \"you bitch, you slut, you whore\" and  \"twinkle twinkle little slut, why don't you take it up the butt\" which one of the pianists wrote on the mirror at the back of the stage... You get the idea?  Perhaps if I had not been the designated driver, alcohol would have made these jokes funny the fifth, sixth, seventh time. Or maybe not.
paraphrase: I attended this place with a bachelorette party. Thankfully, we had reservations because the venue was crowded that Saturday night. The performers were quite skilled, but the sound quality was unexpectedly poor. The entire show revolved around a theme highlighted by repeated phrases like "you bitch, you slut, you whore" and "twinkle twinkle little slut, why don't you take it up the butt," which one of the pianists had written on the mirror at the back of the stage... You get the picture? Perhaps if I weren't the designated driver, drinking might have made these jokes amusing by the fifth, sixth, or seventh time. Or perhaps not.

text:
I usually have nothing but good things to say about this place. I've dealt with Fran and others and have had very good experiences. However, this time was very different. I called to find out how much they might charge to install some brakes and rotors that I had, i was reffered to Mark, the mechanic. I made the appointment with him for Tuesday Sept 30th, he quoted me between $179 - $200 for the work (though the first person I spoke to quoted $150-$179) and told me to bring it at 8am and it would be finished the same day.when I brought it on Tuesday, Septempber 30th at 8 am, Mark forgets that i even made the appointment with him (or didnt bother to make a note of it) saying, "oh, I thought you were supposed to bring this by yesterday". He says I should expect this to probably be done before 3pm. 

I soon recieve a call from Mark at 10:25am, where he tells me the charge will actually be $240 (for four hours of labor + $13 worth of materials, before even getting started to see how long the job would actually take) and that the car would be ready by 3pm, if not possibly before that. Also for good measure he decides to randomly throw in, "oh, and we don't take checks". 

The next phone call is at 3:50pm to ask if i actually need to have the car back today, I tell Mark, "yes, of course". (I was expecting to be ready by 3, if not before like he said) When I express that I definitely need it today, he says that he just has to finish this one side and it will be ready by 415. This leaves me with barely enough time to get a bus and get over there before they close at 430. Honestly if what he had to finish on my car could easily be completed between 3:50 and 4:15, i dont think he would bother asking if i still needed to have it that day. As a result I feel that he may have rushed something while putting my car back together, because now something feels slightly off with the feeling of how the cars is aligned or the way the car drifts at times.
paraphrase:
I typically have only positive remarks about this place, having had excellent experiences with Fran and others. However, this visit was quite different. I called to inquire about the cost of installing brakes and rotors I had, and was directed to Mark, the mechanic. I scheduled an appointment with him for Tuesday, September 30th. He quoted me between $179 and $200 for the job (even though the first person I talked to quoted $150-$179) and told me to bring the car in at 8 am for same-day completion. When I arrived on Tuesday, September 30th at 8 am, Mark seemed to forget about our appointment or didn’t make a note of it, saying, "Oh, I thought you were supposed to bring this by yesterday." He mentioned it should be done by 3 pm.

I received a call from Mark at 10:25 am, informing me the charge would actually be $240 (for four hours of labor plus $13 for materials, before even starting the job to see how long it would take) and the car would be ready by 3 pm, maybe earlier. Additionally, he casually mentioned, "Oh, and we don't take checks."

The next call came at 3:50 pm, asking if I needed the car back that day. I replied, "Yes, of course," expecting it to be ready by 3, as he had said. When I insisted I needed it that day, he assured me it would be ready by 4:15, as he just had to finish one side. This left me with barely enough time to catch a bus and get there before they closed at 4:30. Honestly, if he could finish the job so quickly between 3:50 and 4:15, I doubt he would have asked if I still needed it that day. Consequently, I suspect he might have hurried while reassembling my car because now it feels slightly off, either with the alignment or the way it drifts sometimes.

text: Would like to give NO STARS.  bought all new kitchen appliances there.  Refrigerator is defective.  They\nsend an outside company to repair.  Man arrives with NO TOOLS and wrong size seal for freezer.  not once, twice.  The first time, he tries to use my spoon from counter to install it!  I complain, too bad...they have to comeTHREE times before replacing it!  NEVER AGAIN.
paraphrase: I wish I could give zero stars. I purchased all my new kitchen appliances from this place. The refrigerator turned out to be faulty. They sent an external repair company, and the technician showed up without tools and with the wrong-sized freezer seal, not once, but twice. The first time, he attempted to use my spoon from the counter to install it! I complained, but they said it was unfortunate... they had to come three times before finally replacing it! I will never shop there again.

text: This local Lowe's serves its purpose for my husband and me -- a convenient home improvement store that we run into every month or two. I am most impressed by the lawn and garden section of the store. I am not sure how it compares to other Lowe's stores, but when shopping at the Home Depot in North Fayette last fall, their flower selection was dismal compared to this Lowe's.
paraphrase: This Lowe's in our area fits our needs perfectly as a handy home improvement store that we visit every month or two. The section that impresses me the most is the lawn and garden area. Although I can't say how it stacks up against other Lowe's locations, I found the flower selection here much better than the disappointing one at the Home Depot in North Fayette last fall.

text: Waterfront Lowe's beats Monroeville Lowe's for lawn and garden.  Hate to admit it, but it's true.
paraphrase: The lawn and garden section at Waterfront Lowe's is better than the one at Monroeville Lowe's. I reluctantly acknowledge this, but it's accurate.

text: The food here can actually be a little hit and miss, but I love the grilled cheese club with fries. I get it way too much. People make fun of me.\n\nThey have a ten dollar minimum for credit cards, fyi.
paraphrase: The food at this place is somewhat inconsistent, but I'm a big fan of the grilled cheese club with fries. I order it more often than I should, and people tease me about it. Just so you know, there's a ten dollar minimum for credit card payments.

text: Before today I probably would have given this place three stars based mostly on their beer selection; however, today the service was terrible. We waited two hours for a pizza sub, French onion soup, fries, and buffalo chicken tenders. About an hour and a half in we finally got the small pizza sub and the wrongly made buffalo chicken tenders (ie, no hot sauce). We were told by the waitress that she realized it was wrong, but decided to still bring it out to see if my gf would eat it. She sent it back. By a half hour later, we still had not received the buffalo chicken tenders, nor the French onion soup (which should have been mostly made already), and no fries.  We were told the chef was new, but no effort was made really to make the situation better. No quick and easy app or beers on the house. To boot, the beer was pretty expensive. I will probably give the place another chance based on their beer selection, but it won't be for a while. The food has always been average at best, and the pizza sub was no different. We ended up leaving after two hours without receiving 75% of our order.
paraphrase: Before today, I might have rated this place three stars, mainly due to their beer selection; however, the service today was awful. We waited two hours for a pizza sub, French onion soup, fries, and buffalo chicken tenders. After about an hour and a half, we finally received the small pizza sub and incorrectly prepared buffalo chicken tenders (without hot sauce). The waitress admitted she knew it was wrong but still brought it out to see if my girlfriend would accept it. She sent it back. Half an hour later, we still hadn't received the buffalo chicken tenders, the French onion soup (which should have been mostly prepared already), or the fries. We were informed that the chef was new, but no real effort was made to improve the situation. No complimentary apps or beers. Additionally, the beer was quite pricey. I might give the place another try because of their beer selection, but not anytime soon. The food has consistently been average at best, and the pizza sub was no exception. We left after two hours without receiving 75% of our order.

text:
I'm reading on here that the food is really hit or miss. I've only gone to Duke's one time, but the food was good. It wasn't too busy so our service was very attentive and it didn't take long to get our food at all.

The place is separated between a bar area and a more family style dining area. We sat in the dining area because the bar area was pretty full. The dining room area definitely fills up with an older, have lived in Pittsburgh all their lives crowd, while the younger crowd is in the bar area. 

I'll definitely be back since I live in the area.
paraphrase:
I've been reading that the food here can be quite inconsistent. I've only visited Duke's once, but the meal I had was good. It wasn't crowded, so the service was very attentive, and our food arrived quickly.

The establishment is divided into a bar section and a more family-oriented dining area. We chose to sit in the dining area since the bar was quite packed. The dining room tends to attract an older crowd who have lived in Pittsburgh their whole lives, whereas the bar seems to draw a younger crowd.

I'll certainly return since I reside nearby.

text:
I've been to Duke's three times now. When I first came to PGH, a lot of locals were telling me that this was a good place to catch a game, drink some brews and get great food. Most of that is true, but the bad has mostly outweighed the good.

They have an excellent new tap system. Last time I went, they had Thirsty Dog Leghumpper on draft, and it was perfect. The system really elevates already awesome beer. Plus, the selection is awesome. 

It is a sports-themed bar, so you can expect drunk locals, loud TV's and plenty of yelling. I'm sort of into that, so it doesn't bother me, although I do think their staff is not well equipped enough to work among the chaos. Service is often slow, and they give the waitresses ZERO training in the beers that they offer. This is not a good place for trying something new, as they can't tell you a thing about it. 

The biggest downfall of Duke's, though, is the food. I've had three different, but equally simple, dishes there; A Burger, a Cheesesteak and Chicken Tenders. The first was flavorless and cold. The second was passable, but nothing special. And the last were absolutely awful. Honestly, the batter and size of the chicken made me so ill, I had to call it an early night. I ate two of the 7 tenders (SEVEN!) and had to quit. 
Their fries are very good. And so are the wings.

I'd say Duke's is a good place to drink, but that's about it.
paraphrase:
I've visited Duke's three times so far. When I first arrived in PGH, many locals recommended it as a great spot to watch a game, enjoy some beers, and savor great food. While much of that is accurate, the negatives tend to overshadow the positives.

They've got a fantastic new tap system. On my last visit, they had Thirsty Dog Leghumpper on draft, and it was excellent. The system really enhances the quality of already great beer. Plus, the selection is impressive.

As a sports-themed bar, it features inebriated locals, loud TVs, and lots of shouting. I kind of enjoy that vibe, so it doesn't bother me, but I do think their staff isn't adequately prepared to handle the chaos. Service is frequently slow, and the waitresses receive no training on the beers they serve. It's not the best place to try something new because they can't provide any information about it.

However, the biggest drawback of Duke's is the food. I've tried three different, yet equally simple, dishes there: a Burger, a Cheesesteak, and Chicken Tenders. The Burger was tasteless and cold. The Cheesesteak was decent, but nothing extraordinary. And the Chicken Tenders were downright terrible. The batter and size of the chicken made me so queasy that I had to call it a night early. I managed to eat only two out of seven tenders (SEVEN!) before stopping.

Their fries are quite good, and the wings are too.

Overall, Duke's is a decent spot for drinks, but that's about it.

text:
D&B is nice once in a while but the food is insanely unhealthy. They are also really clever about designing the psychological context in ways to extract money from their customers. You feel a bit milked afterwards. Also much fatter.

You pay for games using credits from mag-stripe cards. This lets D&B give their games arbitrary prices and makes it harder for you to really know how much you are spending. Menus for drinks and food have no prices. $6.05 for a Banana Split cocktail? $3.40 for a Michelob?  I have no idea whether that is expensive but not showing the prices is clever because they can price their things and not have to obey pricing psychology (like anchors and so forth).

Waitstaff hassle you repeatedly to order desserts. Oh, and the dining room has plastic demos dessert trays to get that thought going in your mind as you walk to your table.

The games are all violent shooters or racing games don't appear to have changed much since 2002 when I last went to D&B in California. Hope they can find more innovative arcade games in the future.

Not a pleasant experience, but at least it's different from going to an ordinary restaurant.
paraphrase: D&B is enjoyable occasionally, but the food is extremely unhealthy. They are quite adept at crafting a psychological environment designed to extract money from patrons, leaving you feeling somewhat exploited and possibly heavier. You purchase game credits via mag-stripe cards, allowing D&B to set arbitrary game prices, making it challenging to track your spending. Drink and food menus lack prices, like a $6.05 Banana Split cocktail or a $3.40 Michelob—it's unclear if these are expensive, but the absence of prices is strategic, sidestepping traditional pricing psychology like anchors. The waitstaff frequently urge you to order desserts, and the dining room features plastic dessert displays to influence your mind as you approach your table. The games, mostly violent shooters or racing ones, haven't evolved much since 2002, when I last visited a D&B in California. Hopefully, they'll introduce more innovative arcade games in the future. It’s not an enjoyable experience, but at least it’s a change from a typical restaurant visit.

text:
This was actually the first time I've ever been to a Dave and Buster's.

Walking into such a huge space, especially coming from the often narrow venues in San Francisco was strange.

Un-nerving actually. 

Because Dave and Buster's is all about excess. Almost obscenely so.

As far as food - their stuff is standard. Most of it is fried and as another reviewer pointed out - not very healthy. At all actually. You have to really scour the menu to find some somewhat decent viable and nutritious options.

But most people don't come to Dave & Buster's for food. They come for the games.

And while I've never been that into video games or arcade games - my brother and sister always have.  I was apprehensive about having a good time here  - but after awhile - my siblings' enthusiasm about the place became contagious and I found myself really enjoying myself.

Especially once i realized that I have mad skills at Skee Ball. And proceeded to dominate.

Trust.
paraphrase: This was actually my first visit to a Dave and Buster's. Entering such a massive venue was quite unusual, especially since I'm used to the often cramped spaces in San Francisco. It was somewhat unsettling, to be honest. Dave and Buster's is all about excess, almost to a shocking degree. Regarding the food, it's pretty standard fare. Most of it is fried, and as another reviewer mentioned, it's not very healthy—at all. You really have to comb through the menu to find any decent, nutritious options. However, most people don't visit Dave & Buster's for the food; they come for the games. Although I've never been particularly interested in video or arcade games, my brother and sister have always been fans. I was hesitant about having fun here, but eventually, my siblings' excitement rubbed off on me, and I ended up really enjoying myself. Especially when I discovered my impressive skills at Skee Ball and went on to dominate the game. Trust me.

text:
Dave and Buster's is an enigma, is it dining, a bar, or  playground for video game lovers? Let me get the bar part out of the way first, it's overpriced, and they try to get you to buy a glass to jack up your bill. 

Next, the food. Just like all the chain places at the Waterfront, there are sandwiches, burgers (yes they have a blue cheese burger, who doesn't?) pasta, salads, chicken fingers, yadda yadda. 

The games, I swear over three quarters of the games have been there since 2000 when the place opened. I guess in ten years no new games have hit the scene.

Overall Dave and buster's is unique to the Waterfront, but it's time to update the menu, update the games, and get into the second decade of the 21st century.
paraphrase: Dave and Buster's is a puzzle: is it a dining spot, a bar, or a haven for video game enthusiasts? First, let's tackle the bar aspect—it's overpriced, and they push you to purchase a glass to inflate your tab. Moving on to the food, it's typical of the chain restaurants at the Waterfront: sandwiches, burgers (yes, including a blue cheese burger, which is hardly unique), pasta, salads, chicken fingers, and so forth. As for the games, I swear over 75% of them have been there since the place opened in 2000. It seems no new games have appeared in the last decade. In conclusion, Dave and Buster's stands out at the Waterfront, but it's high time they refreshed the menu, updated the games, and stepped into the second decade of the 21st century.

text:
When did Dave and Buster's become a daycare?? Everywhere you look, there are signs that say kids must be accompanied by an adult, but apparently no one was honoring that at 6PM on a Saturday. I couldn't even walk through the place without someone shoving me. AHHH!
I get that Dave and Buster's is an arcade and that you're going there to play some games, maybe eat, and kill some time. When I last went there a few years ago, we there for my bachelor party, and while I'm sure we got into some shenanigans, I don't remember children running wild there. We were having adult beverages, playing games, and having a good time. This time, it was ridiculous. It isn't Chuck E. Cheese, yet that's how parents seem to be treating the place.
On top of that, the customer service sucked. The place is gigantic, with tables and serving areas everywhere, yet on a Saturday at 6PM, the hostess told us (after interrupting us three times to chat with the other hostess) told us it would be a three hour wait, despite the fact that there were empty tables all over the dining room. I suspect this is a ploy to get you to play games and spend money until you're table is ready, but the food at Dave and Buster's is not worth a three hour wait on any day.
The hostess told us we could be served right away if we went to the midway and found an empty table, so we attempted that. Most of those tables were empty with usually one or two adults at a giant empty table filled with coats who informed us that they needed the table because their kids would be back soon after playing games. Next, i engaged in the most rudest thing I've ever done- I began stalking tables where people were finishing up. I would walk up to people paying their check, ask if I could have their table when they were done, and they would inform me that there were already two or three people who asked them and I should get in line.
Finally, we found a table and proceeded to spend two hours eating dinner. It wasn't because we ordered a lot of food- it was that the waiter took forever to bring our food out. He seemed to have no problem getting us fresh drinks, until we drank as much as we could and then asked for water. After that, he seemed to come around a lot less. 
I had the build your own chicken tacos, which comes on a giant plate with all kinds of fixings. The fixings were fresh, but the chicken was cold and dry, and the tortillas were dried out as well. For $14, it wasn't worth it. 
After dinner we proceeded to play games, which entails shoving people to get through areas and waiting in line to play anything. 
All in all, I didn't like my experience at Dave and Busters. I remembered it being a fun place to go and unwind as an adult. but between all the kids and the crappy service, can't recommend this place. If anyone asks me to go back, I may elect to stay home and poke my eyes with a fork.
paraphrase: When did Dave and Buster's turn into a daycare? Signs everywhere indicate that kids must have an adult with them, but it seems like no one was following that rule at 6 PM on a Saturday. I couldn't even navigate the place without getting pushed. AHHH! I understand that Dave and Buster's is an arcade meant for gaming, dining, and passing time. When I last visited a few years back for my bachelor party, while we likely got up to some mischief, I don't recall kids running rampant. We enjoyed adult drinks, played games, and had a great time. This visit, however, was absurd. It's not Chuck E. Cheese, yet parents seem to treat it as such. Additionally, the customer service was terrible. Despite the venue being enormous with tables and service areas aplenty, the hostess claimed there'd be a three-hour wait at 6 PM on a Saturday, even though empty tables were visible all around. I suspect this is a tactic to make you play games and spend money while waiting for your table, but the food isn't worth a three-hour wait on any day. The hostess mentioned we could be served immediately if we found an empty table in the midway, so we tried that. Most of those tables were occupied by one or two adults guarding empty tables full of coats, saying they needed the space because their kids would return after playing games. I then did something incredibly rude—I started hovering over tables where people were wrapping up. I'd approach them as they paid their bill, asking if I could have their table once they left, only to be told others had already asked and I should get in line. Eventually, we secured a table and spent two hours on dinner. It wasn't due to ordering a lot—it was the waiter's delay in serving our food. He had no trouble bringing us fresh drinks until we switched to water, after which he appeared much less frequently. I ordered the build-your-own chicken tacos, which came on a large plate with various toppings. The toppings were fresh, but the chicken was cold and dry, and the tortillas were also dried out. For $14, it wasn't worth it. After dinner, we played games, which involved pushing through crowds and waiting in line for everything. Overall, my experience at Dave and Buster's was disappointing. I used to remember it as a fun spot for adults to relax, but with all the children and poor service, I can't recommend it. If someone asks me to return, I might prefer to stay home and stab my eyes with a fork.

text:
Dave and Buster's has to be split up into separate experiences to rate it. 

Dining: 2 stars

I had a party of 20 coming in; I had called ahead of time, and arrived early. They immediately set up the table while about 4 of my friends and I waited; the hostess, after taking my name, never spoke to us again.
