She did not tell us when we could sit, how long it would be, or any other information. And then they made us wait for 75% of our party to show up before they would seat us. Mind you, there was no one else waiting, and the table was ready, and we were ready to order. But they didn't tell us this; I didn't even know why we were being forced to wait until I heard a supervisor whispering to the waitress about making us wait. I still have no idea why we were forced to wait. 

When my full party was finally seated, we were helped by two waiters who were perfectly polite and nice. BUT, our service was rather haphazard. People would order drinks and food, and get things at wildly different times. One person was done eating appetizers and drinks when another was just getting their first drink. I know there was chaos to a big party, but it got a little out of hand.

The food is pretty standard American chain food, comparable to Appleby's and Chili's - not offensive, but nothing to knock your socks off. It has a decent selection, and a few good desserts and appetizers, and everything was competently prepared. Overall, it is a fine enough meal for a large group before going to enjoy the arcade at fairly reasonable prices, but some of the service standards are poor. In the future we will probably eat at one of the other restaurants in the area and then move over to D&B's for games.

Bar: 1 Star

Drinks are predictably expensive, but there is a wide variety. The problem is that about half of them don't even kind of resemble what is on the menu, and most don't have enough alcohol to even taste. Worse yet, while you can get a drink at the bar in the arcade, the staff is either completely non-interactive or just rude. You have to track them down, and then have a minimum of interaction before they stomp off like you ruined their life.

Arcade: 4 Stars

There are a lot of great games here to play, and many that are fun with groups or pairs. It is a great place for a large party, because you can mingle with all your friends playing many different very fun games. If you are a couple, there are also many great selections that everyone enjoys. The staff on the floor is helpful and fast to fix games that have problems, and are typically polite. The ticket prizes are cheap as expected, but kind of fun, and the people in the ticket shop were always nice and quick to help.


Overall: 3 Stars

If you want to hang out here, I would suggest eating somewhere else, and then playing games and having fun here, but only order beer at the bar and don't expect a smile.
paraphrase:
Dave and Buster's needs to be evaluated by breaking it down into different experiences.  

Dining: 2 stars  
I had a reservation for a party of 20, called ahead, and showed up early. The staff set up the table as four friends and I waited, but after the hostess took my name, she didn't communicate with us further. We weren't informed about our seating time or any other details. We were made to wait until 75% of our party arrived before being seated, even though there was no one else waiting, the table was ready, and we were prepared to order. I only found out the reason for our wait after overhearing a supervisor speaking to the waitress. To this day, I'm unclear about the waiting requirement. 

Once seated, two polite waiters served us, but the service was erratic. Orders for drinks and food arrived inconsistently. While one person finished their appetizers and drinks, another was just getting their first drink. Managing a large party can be chaotic, but it was a bit excessive. The food is typical American chain fare, similar to Applebee's or Chili's—not bad, but not exceptional. The menu offers a reasonable variety, with some good desserts and appetizers, all adequately prepared. Overall, it's a decent meal option for a large group before heading to the arcade at reasonable prices, though the service could use improvement. In the future, we might dine at another local restaurant before visiting D&B’s for the games.

Bar: 1 Star  
The drinks are predictably pricey, though the selection is vast. However, about half don't match the menu descriptions and lack sufficient alcohol. Worse, the bar staff in the arcade are either unengaged or outright rude. You have to seek them out and endure minimal interaction before they leave as if inconvenienced.

Arcade: 4 Stars  
The arcade offers a wide array of games, suitable for groups or pairs, making it an excellent venue for large parties. Couples will also find plenty of enjoyable options. The floor staff are responsive in fixing game issues and are generally courteous. While the ticket prizes are predictably cheap, they are somewhat entertaining, and the ticket shop staff are consistently friendly and helpful.

Overall: 3 Stars  
For a visit here, I recommend dining elsewhere before coming to play games and enjoy yourself, sticking to beer at the bar, and not expecting friendly service.

text:
Chuckie Cheese for grown ups...Yeah, that could sum up my review for sure (but I'll try to make it a bit more interesting than that).

First let me say that this isn't my first trip to a Dave & Buster's.  Second, I actually experience all of it while here.  We will start with the food/service.  Staff, you were wonderful.  You were polite, you greeted us as soon as we walked in the door, gave us no qualms while ordering and were swift with your delivery.  Food, you were not wonderful.  I ordered the BBQ chicken breasts.  This came with a side of broccoli and their type of mashed potatoes.  The chicken was a bit dry and in some places you can tell it was left on the grill a little too long.  There were actually some bites that all I could taste was charred meat.  There wasn't enough BBQ sauce on the the chicken either.  The broccoli appeared to have been cooked prior and then thrown back into a pan to be seared.  Some of my broccoli actually had some burnt crisp areas on it.  Also I noticed that there were still some leaves left on the broccoli.  The mashed potatoes I wanted to like (which I hoped would be the best part of the dish, I'm a bit of a potato freak), but alas, I was let down. *sad face* They were cold and such a tiny portion.  With a tiny amount of shredded cheese and bacon but into that divot they made with the scoop to get it out of it's container.  Oh food, how if I didn't need you to survive, I wouldn't complain about you.

The building right now is in disarray.  They are in the midst of a remodel with lovely plastic curtains covering up certain sections of their dining area.  No bar out in the dining area right now and the pool tables were absent as well.  If you wanted a drink you had to go back into the game room.

Speaking of the game room, by far the best part.  With the meal I ordered I got the $20 play card (the best part of the meal, super charge it for $3 more and it's even better).  The game room  you can tell is in need of an update.  The carpet is worn and other signs of wear are noticeable as well.  One good thing is the games.  They change them up so often that there is always something new once you return.  Who knew that playing Connect 4 could be so fun, or for that matter Skee Ball rises to the top of my list to play.

I will return again, but only after I eat elsewhere first.
paraphrase:
Chuckie Cheese for adults... Yep, that pretty much captures my review (though I'll try to make it a tad more engaging). 

To start, this isn't my first visit to Dave & Buster's. Also, I've had the full experience here. Let's begin with the food and service. The staff was fantastic. They were courteous, greeted us as soon as we walked in, had no issues with our order, and delivered it promptly. The food, however, was not fantastic. I ordered the BBQ chicken breasts, which came with a side of broccoli and their version of mashed potatoes. The chicken was somewhat dry and seemed over-grilled in some areas, with some bites tasting solely of charred meat. There wasn't enough BBQ sauce on the chicken either. The broccoli seemed pre-cooked and then reheated in a pan, as it had some burnt spots and still had a few leaves on it. I wanted to enjoy the mashed potatoes (as a potato enthusiast, I hoped they'd be the highlight of the meal), but I was disappointed. They were cold and came in a small portion, with just a sprinkle of shredded cheese and bacon in the indentation made by the scoop. Oh food, if only I didn't need you to survive, I wouldn't grumble about you.

Currently, the building is in a state of disorder. They're undergoing renovations, with plastic curtains covering parts of the dining area. There's no bar in the dining area right now, and the pool tables are missing too. To get a drink, you have to go to the game room.

Speaking of the game room, it's definitely the highlight. With my meal, I got the $20 play card (the best part of the meal, and for $3 more you can supercharge it and make it even better). The game room clearly needs an update. The carpet is worn, and other signs of wear are evident too. However, the games are a plus. They change them frequently, so there's always something new when you come back. Who would've thought playing Connect 4 could be so enjoyable, or that Skee Ball would become one of my top choices?

I'll visit again, but only after I've eaten somewhere else first.

text: Not being from the area, I traveled an hour to come here from Uniontown, PA. I was excited! I haven't been to this Dave and Buster's since I was younger and was ready to go back and play me some games! I went with my cousins, mind you, we hadn't ate all day and were very hungry. So, we decided to do the Eat & Play Combo, naturally. Here's where it gets bad. First of all, we get a very snobby hostess that shows us to our table. Then, we wait...and wait...and finally after about 25 mins we get a waitress. Her name was Ashley. We had plenty of time to decide what we wanted, so we quickly put our order in. Next, we were sitting and talking, waiting, we had empty glasses and our waitress had never even come around to see if we needed refills except for one time. She said she'd come back right away with our refills...yea, right. 20 mins later we get our refills. Mind you, our food still hasn't came yet. I see other people getting their food that had come in after us. I was frustrated. After waiting 45 mins for our food, I asked to speak with a manager. One of the servers who I asked replied rudely with \"Well, he's really busy right now. I doubt he'll be able to come out and talk to you\". Finally, 15 mins later...that's one hour, folks. We get our food. What do you think the next problem was? Yep, the food was cold. I was completely fed up at this point, and told my waitress how disappointed I was, especially after driving one hour just to have this terrible service. The manager never came to speak with me, and we didn't even receive our meals for free. The only compensation we got were $10 game cards. Woopty-doo. The only thing good about this place is the games, but the prizes you get for your tickets are all junk and worthless. So, in conclusion...I DO NOT RECOMMEND THE WATERFRONT DAVE AND BUSTERS OF HOMESTEAD, PA. It was horrible, and I will not be returning for a very long time!
paraphrase: As an outsider, I made a one-hour journey from Uniontown, PA, to visit this place. I was eager to return to Dave and Buster's, where I hadn't been since my younger days, and was looking forward to playing some games. I went with my cousins, and we were quite hungry since we hadn't eaten all day. Naturally, we opted for the Eat & Play Combo. Here's where things went downhill. First, we were greeted by a rather snobby hostess who seated us. Then, we waited... and waited... and after about 25 minutes, a waitress named Ashley finally came to our table. We had ample time to decide on our order, so we quickly told her what we wanted. As we sat chatting, our glasses remained empty, with Ashley only once checking if we needed refills. She promised to return with them right away... but it took 20 minutes. Meanwhile, our food hadn't arrived. I noticed others, who came after us, receiving their meals. I was frustrated. After a 45-minute wait, I requested to see a manager. A server responded rudely, "Well, he's really busy right now. I doubt he'll be able to come out and talk to you." Finally, 15 minutes later—making it a full hour—our food arrived. And guess what? It was cold. I was completely frustrated and expressed my disappointment to the waitress, especially after a long drive just to endure such poor service. The manager never spoke with us, and our meals weren't even comped. The only compensation was $10 game cards. Big deal. The games are the only redeeming aspect, but the prizes you get for your tickets are worthless junk. So, to sum up, I DO NOT RECOMMEND THE WATERFRONT DAVE AND BUSTERS IN HOMESTEAD, PA. It was terrible, and I won't be returning anytime soon!

text:
This place just plain sucks
Bad management lost them biz
Dumb arcade drama*

* they have recently changed their under 21 policy so no one underage can even be in the arcade or restaurant after 10pm despite having a guardian over 30 years old
paraphrase: This place is simply terrible. Poor management cost them customers, and there's ridiculous drama about the arcade. Recently, they altered their policy so that anyone under 21 is not allowed in the arcade or restaurant after 10 PM, even if accompanied by a guardian over 30.

text:
DAVE & BUSTERS!  It's a nice place to eat, have fun and drink!!  All ages can enjoy this place.  I was so EXCITED to hear them announce that after 10p, all children whether accompanied by a guardian or not had to go!  It made for a nice evening for adults only.  

Everytime we go whether we play or not we enjoy ourselves.  I didn't give it FOUR STARS because every time I've went I haven't been able to eat in the restaurant area of there.  I know I still get the same food that everyone else does wherever you sit.  Sometimes, I like the nice relaxing atmosphere of the restaurant.  I haven't as of yet, been seated there.  

Not to mention that the servers around the bar, are a little slow at responding to customers as well.  So all in all, D&B's is a good spot to stop at for food and entertainment.  Just don't expect to be seated in the restaurant portion, and expect to wait awhile for food service in the bar area.
paraphrase:
DAVE & BUSTERS! It's a great spot for dining, entertainment, and drinks! People of all ages can enjoy this venue. I was thrilled to hear the announcement that after 10 p.m., all children, whether with a guardian or not, had to leave! This created a pleasant adults-only evening. 

Whenever we visit, whether we play games or not, we always have a good time. I couldn't give it FOUR STARS because each time I've visited, I haven't been able to dine in their restaurant area. I understand that the food is consistent no matter where you sit, but sometimes I prefer the calm atmosphere of the restaurant. So far, I haven't been seated there. 

Additionally, the bar servers are somewhat slow in attending to customers. Overall, D&B's is a solid choice for food and fun. Just don't anticipate being seated in the restaurant section, and be prepared for a wait for service in the bar area.

text: A fun place and lots to do but a bit pricy. $20.00 will buy you a hundred points which will last you a little over an hour.I recommend the bowling game ,ball racing car game and connect 4 also there are several games that can be played in groups of 4
paraphrase: It's an enjoyable spot with plenty of activities, though it's somewhat expensive. Spending $20.00 gets you a hundred points, which will last just over an hour. I suggest trying the bowling game, the ball racing car game, and Connect 4, plus there are several games suitable for groups of four.

text: Visited the Dave and Busters in Homestead, PA last night. Waitress mixed up our credit cards and gave my brothers credit card to someone else and he theirs. Charged them already too - both incorrectly My brother had to walk around and find out who it was. Waitress never apologized. GM was extremely rude and refused to give my brother his original receipt back saying they had thrown it out. DID NOTHING TO REMEDY the situation and ignored us the rest of our visit - not even a refill. After spending over $300 plus games, we will never go back. Whatever happened to the customer is always right? HORRIBLE service
paraphrase: I went to the Dave and Busters in Homestead, PA last night. The waitress mixed up our credit cards, giving my brother's card to someone else and vice versa. They both were incorrectly charged. My brother had to search for the person who had his card. The waitress did not apologize. The General Manager was extremely rude, refused to return my brother's original receipt, claiming it had been discarded. They did nothing to resolve the issue and ignored us for the rest of our visit, not even providing a refill. After spending over $300 plus games, we will never return. Whatever happened to the idea that the customer is always right? The service was TERRIBLE.

text: My boyfriend and I are in our 20s, and have visited this D&B multiple times. After our visit yesterday, however, I don't think we'll be back. When we arrived we were greeted by a long line of people waiting to buy game cards. At first there were 2 extremely slow workers trying to do this, then one left while the line was still out the door! The girl that was left was more than incompetent, and was taking extremely long to fill these game cards. We stood in line for over 20 minutes before being told that there was another attendant in the actual game room. Needless to say, the line in the game room was just as long, and they still only had one girl in the booth. It was a Saturday! After we finally got our cards we had to wait yet again to get a beer. 2 bartenders on staff while the bar was completely full. It took us almost an hour before we actually could start gaming. The floors were filthy with trash, and the bathrooms: repulsive! I had to use the restrooms twice during our visit, and not only did both bathrooms I used have no toilet paper in them, but there was TP all over the floors, and the sinks were less than cleanly. My boyfriend mentioned that the guys restroom was no better, as it smelled like pee and the paper towel dispenser didn't work. All I have to say is get your crap together. We could see a difference in the type of crowed that was there on Saturday compared to what it used to be, and I'm I have no doubt it's due to the poor service and attention to cleanliness. For how expensive they are they need to staff better on weekends. It's a shame how quickly this place has gone down hill and we will NOT be returning anytime soon!
paraphrase: My boyfriend and I, both in our 20s, have frequented this D&B several times. However, after our experience yesterday, I doubt we'll return. When we got there, we faced a long queue of people waiting to purchase game cards. Initially, there were two very slow workers handling this, but one left while the line still stretched out the door! The remaining girl was incredibly incompetent and took an extremely long time to load the game cards. We waited in line for over 20 minutes before being informed about another attendant inside the game room. Unfortunately, the line there was equally long, and only one girl was working in the booth. It was a Saturday! After finally obtaining our cards, we had to wait again to get a beer. With the bar fully packed, only two bartenders were on duty. It took us nearly an hour before we could start playing. The floors were littered with trash, and the bathrooms were disgusting! I had to use the restrooms twice, and both times, there was no toilet paper, TP was scattered all over the floors, and the sinks were far from clean. My boyfriend said the men's restroom was just as bad, with a urine smell and a broken paper towel dispenser. All I can say is that they need to sort themselves out. We noticed a change in the crowd type on Saturday compared to before, and I’m sure it's because of the terrible service and lack of cleanliness. Given their high prices, they should have more staff on weekends. It's unfortunate how rapidly this place has deteriorated, and we will definitely not be coming back anytime soon!

text:
ehh, 3.5 -- I can't rate it super low because i do smile a lot during my visits. Speaking of that, I've been here a few times, but never wrote a review for D&B. 

I'm a kid at heart and honestly do enjoy the games. However, there have been multiple instances where I swiped my game card & it took my credits, but no game began. There have also been times that I won and no tickets were released. I guess nothing is perfect but it does happen atleast once a visit (each scenerio)  It's hard to find a worker walking around and for half-price games, you kind of just take your loss with the 1.3 credits for skee ball (hmph!)

The food- it really isnt anything too fancy. I've had okay food, but super long wait times for a simple chicken fingers & fries for example. It's your basic sandwiches, salads, appetizers & a few pastas. I believe all sandwiches & burgers are around $10 & served with fries.  You can get the Eat & Play deal which includes food from a specific menu + a game card. Besides the game card deal, if you wanted to make D&B a date night you might be better off heading to another place to eat in Waterfront for amazing food.. but to each their own.  Try it once I suppose. The brownie sundae that i devoured last night was satisfying (2 brownies, 2 scoops of ice cream.. one with chocolate syrup; the other with raspberry sauce & fresh strawberries. Oh & whip cream)

Drinks- pretty weak. We ordered 2 liquid maryjanes (2 each that is) which are filled with multiple shots of alchy & didn't really catch a buzz. Oh welllll. They do have a happy hour from 5-7pm. I'd go during that time for the best deal. I believe cocktails are half off & $3 off  bottles of wine. I've never seen anyone walking around with a wine glass though lol. 

The prizes- as others have noted, the prizes are a little cheap for the amount of tickets they "cost" However, it's cool to leave with a D&B coffee mug, cup or shot glass. Last night for 2500 tickets we decided on magnetic-style dart board (1,300?) a coffee mug, a D&B glass (our plastic one bit the dust) & a D&B coffee travel mug.. I might be missing a few odds & ends.

Overall it's a fun time but can be pricey. It's best to head here Wednesday for half price games or get the Eat & Play deal while dining. 

I can't lie though, I find myself smiling ear to ear a few times within those 3 hours (woah, seemed longer than amount of time :D ) Speaking of which, in that time-frame we played 250 credits (cost= $40) but it was half-price games.
paraphrase:
Ehh, I'd give it a 3.5. I can't rate it super low since I find myself smiling a lot during my visits. I've been here a few times but never got around to writing a review for D&B until now. 

I'm a kid at heart and genuinely enjoy the games. However, there have been several times when I swiped my game card, only for it to take my credits without starting a game. There have also been occasions where I won, but no tickets came out. I suppose nothing is perfect, but this tends to happen at least once every visit (each scenario). It's challenging to find a staff member nearby, and with half-price games, you kind of just accept the loss of 1.3 credits for skee ball (hmph!).

The food isn't anything out of the ordinary. I've had decent meals, but there are often long waits for something as simple as chicken fingers and fries. The menu includes basic sandwiches, salads, appetizers, and a few pasta dishes. Most sandwiches and burgers are about $10 and come with fries. You can opt for the Eat & Play deal, which includes a meal from a specific menu and a game card. If you're planning a date night at D&B, you might be better off dining elsewhere in Waterfront for a great meal, but to each their own. Give it a try once, I suppose. The brownie sundae I indulged in last night was satisfying (two brownies, two scoops of ice cream—one with chocolate syrup, the other with raspberry sauce and fresh strawberries, topped with whipped cream).

As for the drinks, they’re pretty weak. We ordered two Liquid Maryjanes each, which are supposed to be loaded with multiple shots, but we didn’t really get buzzed. Oh well. They do have a happy hour from 5-7 PM, which is the best time to go for deals. I think cocktails are half off, and bottles of wine are $3 off. I’ve never seen anyone with a wine glass, though, lol.

Regarding the prizes, as others have mentioned, they seem a bit cheap for the ticket cost. Still, it’s nice to leave with a D&B coffee mug, cup, or shot glass. Last night, for 2,500 tickets, we chose a magnetic-style dartboard (1,300?), a coffee mug, a D&B glass (our plastic one broke), and a D&B coffee travel mug. I might have missed a few smaller items.

Overall, it’s a fun experience but can be expensive. It’s best to go on Wednesdays for half-price games or take advantage of the Eat & Play deal. 

I must admit, I do find myself grinning from ear to ear several times during those three hours (woah, seemed longer than that amount of time :D). Speaking of which, in that span, we played 250 credits (costing $40) because of the half-price games.

text: I used to love D&B when it first opened in the Waterfront, but it has gone down hill over the years. The games are not as fun and do not give you as many tickets and the prizes have gotten cheaper in quality. It takes a whole heck of a lot of tickets for you to even get a pencil! The atmosphere is okay but it used to be so much better with the funnest games and diverse groups of people! Now, it is run down and many of the games are app related games (Fruit Ninja) and 3D Experience rides. With such \"games\", you can't even earn tickets and they take a lot of tokens! Last time I went, back in the winter, many of the games were broken, which made for a negative player experience. I would go to D&B to play some games again in the future, but it is no longer one of my favorite places to go due to the decline of fun games where you can earn tickets.
paraphrase: When D&B first launched in the Waterfront, I was a big fan, but over time, it's really deteriorated. The games aren't as enjoyable anymore and don't reward as many tickets, plus the prize quality has declined. You need an absurd amount of tickets just to get something as simple as a pencil! The atmosphere is still alright, but it used to be much better, with more exciting games and a wider mix of people. Now, the place feels shabby, with many games being app-based like Fruit Ninja or 3D Experience rides. These "games" don't let you earn tickets and require a lot of tokens. During my last visit in the winter, many games weren't even functioning, which made the experience quite disappointing. While I might return to D&B for some gaming, it has fallen off my list of favorite spots due to the reduction of engaging ticket-earning games.

text: I was here on 10/3 and I have to say this was a horrible experience.  Me and a friend went here to eat and play a couple games afterwards.  It took the waitress at least 10-15 minutes to greet us and by that time we knew we should just order then because we might not see her again.  We ordered and got our drinks and well let's just say we didn't see the waitress again until she was giving us our check (some manager delivered the food to the table).  No refill on my beverage, no how is your food, no ketchup for my burger or fries was offered.  Lots of people running around like crazy and if your understaffed hire more people!  I will not be back again to eat.
paraphrase: I visited this place on October 3rd, and I must admit it was a terrible experience. A friend and I went there to have a meal and play some games afterward. It took about 10-15 minutes for the waitress to acknowledge us, so we decided to order right away, fearing we might not see her again. We received our drinks, but that was the last we saw of her until she brought us the bill (a manager actually served our food). There was no beverage refill, no check on how our meal was, and no ketchup offered for my burger and fries. The staff seemed to be running around in chaos, and if they are understaffed, they should hire more people! I won't be returning to eat here.

text: I'll have a biased opinion of any place like this. What could be wrong with playing old and new school games, drinking, and eating all at the same place? The one thing I can think of is the price. On the surface, this is a pretty expensive place; however, they give a bunch of good deals depending on the time you go, whether or not you \"donate\" money to the Make-a-Wish Foundation, and if you're lucky when you take a spin on the wheel of prizes! But honestly, know what the deals are before you go to make sure to get the best price available. \nIn regards to the games, there's a plethora for your enjoyment. Although it is nice to play games that spit out tickets, the last couple of times I've gone I've gotten 2 hours of arcade games for a very cheap price (Mario Kart, anyone?!). Either way, it's a really fun place that you'll most certainly have a good time at.
paraphrase: I tend to have a partial perspective on places like this. What's not to love about a venue where you can enjoy both classic and modern games, have drinks, and eat all in one spot? The only downside I see is the cost. At first glance, it appears quite pricey; however, they offer numerous great deals based on when you visit, whether you "donate" to the Make-a-Wish Foundation, and if you're fortunate when spinning the prize wheel! Still, it's wise to familiarize yourself with the deals beforehand to ensure you get the best rate. As for the games, there's an abundance to keep you entertained. While playing games that dispense tickets is enjoyable, the last few times I visited, I secured 2 hours of arcade gaming at a very low cost (Mario Kart, anyone?!). Overall, it's an incredibly enjoyable spot where you're sure to have a fantastic time.

text: Horrible beer, horrible service, horrible menu
paraphrase: Terrible beer, terrible service, terrible menu

text:
SUMMARY:
If I was in the area and no one had a better suggestion for where to go, I might come back for beer and appetizers.

GOOD:
- Decent beer...
- Original pretzel appetizer.  You take chunks of soft pretzel and dunk it in spinach cheese dip.
- Huge portions.  Most of us had leftovers.  The RB Appetizer Sampler was multi-story and way more food that we could handle.  The half order of ribs looked like a full rack.
- Attentive service with quick seating and frequent drink/water refills.
- Amusing name.

BAD:
- Lots of food choices... burgers... steak... chicken... salads.  Lots of drink choices... beers... wines... mixed drinks.  I guess this could be seen as good, but it takes a while for everyone to pick something and since no one's awesome at everything, you could tell right away that nothing distinguishes this place.
- None of the food was super awesome.  Nothing was awful, but nothing stood out in any way.  I've had better beer, appetizers, and a better main course at plenty of breweries.
- Kind of dead on a Monday night with 3/4 of the place empty.

Come if you want bar food, but keep your expectations low.
paraphrase:
SUMMARY:
If I happened to be in the vicinity and there were no better recommendations, I might return for a drink and some snacks.

GOOD:
- Acceptable beer...
- Unique pretzel starter. You dip soft pretzel pieces into a spinach cheese dip.
- Generous servings. Most of us had leftovers. The RB Appetizer Sampler was towering and more than we could eat. The half order of ribs resembled a full rack.
- Attentive staff with prompt seating and regular drink/water refills.
- Entertaining name.

BAD:
- Numerous food options... burgers... steak... chicken... salads. Numerous drink options... beers... wines... mixed drinks. This might be seen as a positive, but it takes time for everyone to decide, and since expertise in everything is rare, it was clear that nothing set this place apart.
- None of the food was particularly impressive. Nothing was terrible, but nothing was remarkable either. I've enjoyed better beer, appetizers, and main courses at many other breweries.
- Fairly quiet on a Monday night with much of the place unoccupied.

Visit for bar food, but keep your expectations in check.

text:
Not the greatest ale house in the city but certainly not the worst.

I like the food and I have tried all the beers.  They're just "OK" as far as beer goes.

Large portions. Friendly staff.
paraphrase: This ale house isn't the best in the city, but it's definitely not the worst either. I enjoy the food, and I've sampled all the beers. They're just "average" when it comes to beer. The portions are large, and the staff is welcoming.

text: My friends got everyhing from steak(they didn't even get a well done steak right) to mahi tacos and the only great menu item was the seasoned vegetables- give it a miss. Sing sing next door is awesome but get dinner somewere else. After we were done the waiter asked if we were ready for the checks and I had ordered meal that came with a desert! Really dissapointing all around.
paraphrase: My friends ordered a variety of dishes, from steak (even the well-done steak was not up to par) to mahi tacos, and the only exceptional item was the seasoned vegetables—it's best to skip this place. Sing Sing next door is fantastic, but find somewhere else for dinner. After we finished, the waiter asked if we wanted the checks, even though I had ordered a meal that included dessert! Overall, it was quite disappointing.

text: Little slow on the service and didn't have the beer I wanted on the menu. But you get those things when they brew their own beers on-site.   But the stadium pretzels are good.  The cream stout is tasty.\n\nThe burgers are good and big with the little skinny fries.  I'm not a fan of the skinny fry, but hey the burgers would choke a horse - and almost did.  :o)
paraphrase: The service was a bit sluggish, and they didn't offer the beer I was hoping for on the menu. However, that's to be expected when they craft their own beers in-house. On a positive note, the stadium pretzels are enjoyable, and the cream stout is flavorful. The burgers are hefty and come with thin fries. I'm not really fond of skinny fries, but the burgers are enormous—almost overwhelming. :o)

text: Lately, the Rock Bottom is hit or miss. The beers are always good and the Uppity Jagoff IPA is one of the best India Pale Ales I've ever tasted. The low point lately has been the food. I will never forgive the idiot in management who took the chicken quesadilla off the menu. That said, I ordered the lobster roll and when it arrived, the bread was as hard as a brick and cold. The filling was okay, but the lobster flavor was masked by over spicing. I suggest you eat somewhere else and imbibe at the Rock Bottom.
paraphrase: Recently, the quality at the Rock Bottom has been inconsistent. The beers remain excellent, and the Uppity Jagoff IPA stands out as one of the finest India Pale Ales I've ever had. However, the food has been disappointing. I can't overlook the decision by management to remove the chicken quesadilla from the menu. When I tried the lobster roll, the bread was tough and cold like a brick. The filling was decent, but the lobster taste was overwhelmed by too many spices. I recommend dining elsewhere but enjoying drinks at the Rock Bottom.

text: Went with a group after work for dinner and a brew.  We ordered a few appetizers first.  The wings are good and the soft pretzels with spicy spinach cheese dip was fun.  Had a burger, you can create your own from veggie, turkey, or fresh ground beef. Had a fresh ground beef and the meat was fresh and cooked perfectly with a fresh bun.\n\nThe brew pub is nice and was surprised it wasn't busier.
paraphrase: After work, our group headed to a place for dinner and some drinks. We started with a few appetizers. The wings were tasty, and the soft pretzels with spicy spinach cheese dip were enjoyable. I opted for a burger, where you can customize it with veggie, turkey, or fresh ground beef. I chose fresh ground beef, which was fresh and perfectly cooked, served on a fresh bun. The brew pub itself is pleasant, and I was surprised it wasn't more crowded.

text: Beer selection was good, but they were out of the ones we wanted. 4 of the 6 people in our group ordered steaks all of them were cooked to their satisfaction. The bourbanzola was a good addition. Defiantly a place I would go again. Joe, our server, was awesome and super friendly.
paraphrase: The variety of beer was impressive, though they didn't have the ones we were looking for. Four out of the six people in our party had steaks, and each was cooked to their liking. The bourbanzola was a nice touch. I would definitely visit this place again. Joe, our server, was fantastic and very personable.

text:
I always feel like I am constantly bashing breweries for their food, but in my opinion, I feel the bar is raised for places like this. It seems there are so many breweries out there that offer great beer, and slack off on the food. Unfortunately Rock Bottom is one that falls into that category. Let's be honest, when you go to a brewery, you want the full experience. If it was only about the beer, I'd go buy a growler and take it home. 

So, about the beer....EXCELLENT!!!!! The Jagoff IPA is with out a doubt one of the best IPAs I have ever had! They only I can think of that I think is better is Dogfish Head, but that's a matter of opinion. The beer selection is constantly changing, and they always have some excellent seasonal beers. You can't go wrong, there's bound to be a beer everyone will like. 

As for the food.......it's pretty much a crap shoot. Sometimes it's good, sometimes it's terrible. My biggest problem is there is absolutely no consistency when it comes to the food. One time it's perfect, then maybe it's cold, or it's burned, or it tastes completely different from the last time you had it. Unfortunately, it seems there is zero attention to food quality. 

That's pretty much it. This place fixes it's food consistency, and it's a five star place. Till then, it's hit or miss!
paraphrase:
I often find myself criticizing breweries for their food, but I believe the expectations are higher for such places. It seems that many breweries excel in their beer offerings but neglect the food aspect. Sadly, Rock Bottom fits this description. Let's be real; when you visit a brewery, you want the complete experience. If it were just about the beer, I'd simply buy a growler and enjoy it at home.

Now, about the beer—it's EXCELLENT! The Jagoff IPA ranks among the best IPAs I've ever had! The only one I might prefer is Dogfish Head, but that's just my personal taste. Their beer selection is ever-changing, with fantastic seasonal options. There's something for everyone.

Regarding the food, it's quite unpredictable. Sometimes it's good, other times it's awful. The main issue is the lack of consistency in food quality. One visit might be perfect, but the next it could be cold, burnt, or completely different from before. Unfortunately, it seems there's no focus on maintaining food quality.

That's about it. If they could address the food consistency, this would be a five-star establishment. Until then, it's a gamble.

text:
I've always been pleasantly surprised with the food I've gotten at Rock Bottom.  I say "surprised' because with any restaurant that falls into the slippery territory of a "chain", you expect to find your fair share of culinary duds.  But I've really had a lot of good meals at Rock Bottom.  Now, granted, you do owe it to yourself when ordering to not forget where you are (a "bar-straunt" if you will), and don't get anything above and beyond this place's call of duty.  For instance, I will never be ordering the ahi tuna.  I'll leave that to my favorite sushi bars and restaurants a bit closer to the water (the Mon doesn't count.....).

But besides that, I always really enjoy my meal choices.  I've recently gotten the chicken avocado sandwich.  Chicken was grilled perfectly, plenty of avocado, and it was a good sized sandwich without being excessive.  I've also ordered the Margherita pizza, which could definitely be split between two people.  It's an eight cut that comes to you on a big wooden pizza paddle.  The pizza was really great.  The crust was crispy, it had a good balance of cheese and tomatoes, and the basil pesto was fresh and tasty.  And not greasy whatsoever - BIG bonus points there.  But my biggest surprise as of late was when I ordered their chicken flautas.  I'm a HUGE Mexican food fan and always hesitate to order Mexican outside of a Mexican restaurant for fear of being disappointed.  But I had an insatiable craving one night and decided to go for it.  It was DELICIOUS.  The flautas are deep fried which gives you an amazing crunch, and they still managed to not dry out the inner ingredients.  The accompanying rice was nice and moist as well.  I was so happy!

I've always been quite happy with the service there as well.  I go on Thursday evenings for trivia, and nine times out of ten the service is really spot on.  The ladies servicing the bar area are friendly and attentive.  Only once or twice, to my memory, has my service been sub-par, and that can usually be chalked up to an extremely packed Trivia Night with only two visible servers helping 50+ people.

I definitely give Rock Bottom two thumbs up for taking your typical chain-found food and making it fresh, yummy, and worth coming back for more.
paraphrase:
I've consistently been pleasantly taken aback by the meals I've had at Rock Bottom. I use "taken aback" because, with any establishment that falls into the ambiguous category of a "chain," you're bound to encounter a fair share of culinary letdowns. However, I've genuinely enjoyed numerous meals at Rock Bottom. Granted, it's important to remember the nature of the place (a "bar-straunt," if you will) and not to order anything that exceeds its typical offerings. For example, I would never choose the ahi tuna; I'll save that for my favored sushi spots closer to the coast (the Mon doesn't count...).

Other than that, I consistently find my meal selections satisfying. Recently, I tried the chicken avocado sandwich. The chicken was perfectly grilled, there was plenty of avocado, and the sandwich was well-sized without being too much. I've also had the Margherita pizza, which is certainly shareable between two people. It's an eight-slice pizza served on a large wooden paddle. The pizza was excellent. The crust was crispy, it had a nice balance of cheese and tomatoes, and the basil pesto was fresh and flavorful. Plus, it wasn't greasy at all, which earns big bonus points. My biggest surprise recently was the chicken flautas. As a HUGE fan of Mexican food, I usually hesitate to order it outside of a dedicated Mexican restaurant, fearing disappointment. But I had an irresistible craving one night and decided to try it. It was DELICIOUS. The flautas are deep-fried, providing an amazing crunch while keeping the inside from drying out. The accompanying rice was moist and enjoyable. I was thrilled!

The service has also consistently pleased me. I visit on Thursday evenings for trivia, and most of the time, the service is top-notch. The ladies attending the bar area are friendly and attentive. Only once or twice do I recall having sub-par service, and that's typically due to a very busy Trivia Night with only two servers visible for over 50 people.

I wholeheartedly give Rock Bottom two thumbs up for transforming typical chain offerings into fresh, tasty dishes that keep me coming back.

text:
I went to Rock Bottom this past weekend, and it was "nice" to see that nothing has changed in the year or two since my last visit. While the beer is pretty good, it has never outweighed the fact that the service isn't good and the food is average, at best.

It was even difficult to get beers at the bar...we tried to have the girls order, thinking they'd have better luck...when that failed, the men tried...that didn't work either. If we hadn't REALLY wanted a cold beer after a comedy show at The Improv, we would've just left. It definitely wasn't busy enough to be noticed and ignored by the bartender, so I can't overlook it. Of course, the service has always been spotty at Rock Bottom, so it was hardly surprising.

Combine the weak food and poor service with Rock Bottom being too far from home for a night out just to drink, and you get the exact formula that's kept me from going there in the past...and will keep me from going often in the future.
paraphrase: I visited Rock Bottom last weekend and found it "nice" that nothing had changed since my last visit a year or two ago. Although the beer is quite good, it has never made up for the fact that the service is lacking and the food is merely average. Even getting beers at the bar was a challenge. We had the women attempt to order, thinking they might have better success, but when that failed, the men tried with no luck either. If we hadn't REALLY wanted a cold beer after attending a comedy show at The Improv, we would have left. It wasn't busy enough to warrant being overlooked by the bartender, so I can't excuse it. The service at Rock Bottom has always been inconsistent, so it wasn't a shock. Coupled with the mediocre food and poor service, the distance from home for a night out just to drink is why I haven't frequented Rock Bottom in the past and why I won't be going often in the future.

text: The only reason Rock Bottom gets 3 stars is because of the brews. Food was \"eh\", not many vegetarian options and hostesses who will ignore you because they are too busy chatting about who didn't text them back. This place has the potential to be great, but sadly, it falls way too short in almost all areas.
paraphrase: Rock Bottom earns its 3-star rating solely due to its beers. The food was mediocre, with limited vegetarian choices, and the hostesses tend to overlook guests as they are preoccupied with discussing personal matters. Although this place could be outstanding, it unfortunately falls significantly short in nearly every aspect.

text: Rock Bottom was our first stop on a 17 mile pub crawl.  As expected, the bar was lively, the food was good, and the service was accommodating,  The beers are better than Church Brew Works and the bar was populated.  The bartender readily offered a taste of beer and gave us a hat to wear for the rest of the crawl.  \nI vote for Rock Bottom as the anchor store of the Water Works.
paraphrase: Our 17-mile pub crawl kicked off at Rock Bottom. True to form, the atmosphere was vibrant, the food was tasty, and the service was welcoming. The beers surpass those at Church Brew Works, and the place was bustling. The bartender was quick to offer a beer sample and even gave us a hat for the remainder of our crawl. I nominate Rock Bottom as the flagship venue at the Water Works.

text:
I haven't been to Rock Bottom in years.  Maybe even decades...is that even possible?  Well, either way, it's been awhile.  So with my gift certificate I took my brother out for a birthday dinner.  

As I get older, my tolerance for things like crowds and youngsters gets lower and lower.  As a result, I told my brother to be ready by 4pm so we could head to the Waterfront before everyone goes out for date night.  Plus the Pirates were playing at 4:05 so we could enjoy our beers and the game.

When we arrived, the bar side was already pretty packed but we were still able to get a table.  Our server was very attentive, friendly and helpful.  Right off the bat we both ordered samplers so we could try all of their beers.  We loved the fact that as she approached our table with a full tray of samples our server said, "I have to go back for the rest."  In all we were served 10 different beers each (in 3 oz glasses, maybe 4 - I can never tell).  I gotta say, Rock Bottom knows their beer.  I liked more than I didn't, and very few were not to my liking.  So the $6.95 10 beer sampler was well worth it.  

During our sampling we indulged on some Southwest Egg Rolls.  They were okay, but not quite what I expected.  The spicy ranch was top notch, though.  As we ran out of beer, we each ordered up a Kobe Burger.  They were both cooked exactly to our liking (medium).  And to top off our wonderful evening, the Pirates brought home a win!

My only complaint, as a beer drinker, is the cost for a growler.  The glass container itself is sold for $12.95.  That's the empty price.  To me, that's a little ridiculous when East End only charges $3 for the bottle and Penn Brewery $5.  Paying more for the bottle than the beer inside seems like insanity to me.  So I walked out empty handed.    But it won't deter me from returning.
paraphrase:
I hadn't visited Rock Bottom in a long time, possibly even decades. Is that even feasible? Regardless, it had been a while. So, using my gift certificate, I decided to take my brother out for his birthday dinner.

As I've aged, my patience for things like crowds and young people has decreased. Consequently, I asked my brother to be ready by 4 PM so we could head to the Waterfront before the date night crowd arrived. Plus, the Pirates game was starting at 4:05, allowing us to enjoy our beers while watching the game.

When we got there, the bar area was already quite busy, but we still managed to get a table. Our server was very attentive, friendly, and helpful. We immediately ordered samplers to try all their beers. We appreciated it when our server approached with a tray full of samples and said, "I have to go back for the rest." We were served 10 different beers each (in 3 oz glasses, maybe 4 - it's hard to tell). I must say, Rock Bottom really knows their beer. I liked most of them, with only a few not to my liking, making the $6.95 sampler of 10 beers well worth it.

While sampling, we tried some Southwest Egg Rolls. They were alright, but not quite what I expected. However, the spicy ranch was excellent. As we finished our beer, we each ordered a Kobe Burger, which was cooked perfectly to our preference (medium). To cap off our great evening, the Pirates won!

My only gripe, as a beer enthusiast, is the growler pricing. The glass container alone costs $12.95, which is just for the empty bottle. I find it a bit absurd when East End charges $3 for the bottle and Penn Brewery $5. Paying more for the container than the beer inside seems crazy to me. So, I left without one, but it won't stop me from coming back.

text:
Went to Rock Bottom for lunch the other day and was very pleased. My coworker had printed out a coupon for a free mini appetizer. The waitress told us that they were not making mini appetizers and gave us a full sized chicken quesadilla. If you have ever had the southwestern egg rolls from Chili's - the filling tasted like that and I am a big fan! 

Mondays are half off burger days and I tried the just hot enough burger - topped with crispy jalapenos, pepper jack cheese, pico de gallo and housemade habanero ketchup. It was delish! The ketchup was so good and the fries were extremely crispy. 

The prices are a little steep for lunch but if you go on Monday, it's totally worth it. I think that the quality of food more than makes up for it but don't expect for pay under $10 for a sit down. 

I felt that the staff was very nice and the waitress was very attentive. The service was quick and I think you can get in and out in under an hour.
paraphrase:
I recently had lunch at Rock Bottom and was quite satisfied. My coworker had brought a coupon for a free mini appetizer, but the waitress informed us that mini appetizers weren't available, so she gave us a full-sized chicken quesadilla instead. If you've ever tasted the southwestern egg rolls from Chili's, the filling was similar, which I really enjoyed. 

On Mondays, they offer half-priced burgers, so I tried the "just hot enough" burger, which comes with crispy jalapeños, pepper jack cheese, pico de gallo, and housemade habanero ketchup. It was delicious! The ketchup was excellent, and the fries were very crispy. 

Although the lunch prices are a bit high, going on a Monday makes it worthwhile. I believe the quality of the food justifies the cost, but don't expect to pay less than $10 for a sit-down meal. 

I found the staff to be very friendly, and the waitress was very attentive. The service was prompt, and I think you can finish your meal in under an hour.

text:
Rock Bottom is a chain brew pub one that, in my opinion, is just "OK".  The restaurant is rather large with lots of seating and a pool table. They have many of their own brews that you can sample but to tell you the truth none of them are that good. I have had several of their beers and all of them have been forgettable. 

Their food is pretty much like their beers, forgettable. I have had one of their burgers and their pizza and they really weren't anything to write home about.

I feel they will impress those who have never been to a brew pub but I would probably describe them as the Chili's of Brew Pubs. If you are in homestead and are looking for a burger and a beer it might be a good choice for a bite, or at least until the Burgatory opens!
paraphrase:
Rock Bottom is a chain brew pub that I find to be just "OK." The place is quite spacious, offering plenty of seating and a pool table. They offer a variety of their own beers for sampling, but honestly, none stand out. I've tried several of their beers, and all have been unremarkable.

Their food is much like their beer—forgettable. I've tasted both their burger and pizza, and neither were noteworthy.

I think it might appeal to those new to brew pubs, but I'd liken it to the Chili's of brew pubs. If you're in Homestead and want a burger and a beer, it might serve as a decent option until Burgatory opens.

text:
Nothing special. Just like Applebee's, Chili's or Fridays. Stick to the bar food (burgers, sandwiches, pizza, wings and salads) and you will be okay. 

This was our second attempt at trying Rock Bottom on Fathers Day. The first time almost ten years ago. We went in the early afternoon and they had already ran out of steaks. How do you do that on Fathers Day?! This time we went around 12 noon and beat the rush.

My wife got and loved the Berry Bash Sangria. She just said it had too much ice in it. Our daughter got the root beer float which she love and I started with a Mango Ice Tea, but later switched for a raspberry ice tea as the mango ice tea wasn't sweet enough.

We started off the the Kobe beef sliders as our app. It took at least 15 minutes to get them. You get three sliders and they were very good.

My wife got a small greenhouse salad with Jalapeno ranch dressing to start off with which she enjoyed and the lobster & shrimp enchiladas as her main entree. She complained about how salty it was and the fact that there was no lobster in it at all.

Our daughter got the kids burger and fries. No surprise there.

I ordered the New York Strip with mashed potatoes and it came with green bean as well. My strip was cooked perfectly and has a decent taste. Longhorn has a much, much better quality of steak though. The green beans were pretty good and my mashed potatoes very bland and pretty average. 

The kitchen sounded like an live episode of Hell's Kitchen. The waiters were repeatedly asking for orders at least 5 to 6 times. The kitchen was backed up the whole time we were there.

On a different note, our waitress Susan was very good. Come here for drinks and the bar food. Skip everything else.
paraphrase:
Nothing remarkable. It's similar to Applebee's, Chili's, or Fridays. Stick with the bar fare like burgers, sandwiches, pizza, wings, and salads, and you'll do fine.

This was our second visit to Rock Bottom on Father's Day; the first was nearly a decade ago. We went in the early afternoon back then and they had already run out of steaks. How does that happen on Father's Day?! This time, we arrived around noon and avoided the crowd.

My wife had the Berry Bash Sangria, which she enjoyed but thought had too much ice. Our daughter loved her root beer float, and I started with a Mango Iced Tea, but switched to a raspberry iced tea as the mango one wasn't sweet enough.

We began with the Kobe beef sliders as an appetizer. It took at least 15 minutes to get them. You get three sliders, and they were very good.

My wife ordered a small greenhouse salad with Jalapeno ranch dressing to start, which she liked, and the lobster & shrimp enchiladas for her main dish. She complained that it was too salty and lacked any lobster.

Our daughter had the kids' burger and fries, which was expected.

I ordered the New York Strip with mashed potatoes and it came with green beans as well. My strip was cooked perfectly and tasted decent. However, Longhorn offers a much better quality steak. The green beans were pretty good, but the mashed potatoes were bland and average.

The kitchen felt like a live episode of Hell's Kitchen, with waiters repeatedly asking for orders 5 to 6 times. The kitchen was backed up the entire time we were there.

On a different note, our waitress Susan was excellent. Come here for drinks and bar food, but skip the rest.

text: I go to Rock Bottom pretty often for dinner or their awesome beer, so I was really really excited to have my bridal shower there.  My maid of honor reserved the banquet room which was very nice and separated from the rest of the restaurant.  My MOH and mother said that the person they talked to about reservations and arrangements was wonderful and easy to get in touch with.  My shower was wonderful, the wait staff was great and kept everyone's glasses full.  It started pouring down rain and they let us stay a little longer so my gifts wouldn't get wet when taking them out to the car.  The only reason this place gets 4 stars is because we were not allowed to take our leftover food from the buffet home.  There was salmon, chicken, potatoes, and mac and cheese left over and they told us we couldn't box it up.  Not sure why- they can't re-serve it!  It would have just gone to waste!\n\nAlso, not at all related to the shower, their trivia night is super fun!
paraphrase:
I frequently visit Rock Bottom for dinner or their fantastic beer, so I was thrilled to hold my bridal shower there. My maid of honor booked the banquet room, which was nicely separate from the rest of the restaurant. Both my MOH and mother mentioned that the person assisting with reservations and arrangements was excellent and easy to reach. The shower was wonderful, with attentive wait staff who ensured everyone's glasses were full. When it started pouring rain, they allowed us to stay a bit longer to prevent my gifts from getting wet while being taken to the car. However, the reason for a 4-star rating is that we weren't allowed to take home the leftover buffet food, including salmon, chicken, potatoes, and mac and cheese. I'm not sure why, as they couldn't reuse it, and it would have just been wasted! 

Additionally, unrelated to the shower, their trivia night is incredibly fun!

text: I rarely give a business a superior rating, but I have to say my wife and I had one of the best dining experiences at the Rock Bottom Brewery.   The food was delicious,  the beer, every recipe sampled,  was amazing and the service was wonderful.  The staff made us feel like we were the most important guests in the restaurant. \n\nPlease support this business.  I guarantee you will not be disappointed.
paraphrase: I don't often award top ratings to businesses, yet I must admit that my wife and I had an exceptional dining experience at the Rock Bottom Brewery. The food was delectable, and each beer we tried was outstanding. The service was excellent, with the staff making us feel like the restaurant's most valued guests. I encourage you to support this business—I assure you, you won't be let down.

text: I'm not much of a beer drinker, but my fiancee is a major enthusiast, so I agreed to tag along.  I'm glad I did!  Our waitress was very knowledgeable about the extensive house-brewed beer selection, and helped me to pick one I would love (the kolsch was very crisp, light, and drinkable.)  The food was standard pub fare with a twist, well-executed.  My burger was topped with onion petals and gorgonzola, and cooked to perfection.  The waitress recommended their macaroni and cheese for a side, which was definitely a good choice.  My only complaint is that most of the food was rather heavy, and I spent the rest of the night wallowing on the couch.  Not sure I would make the hour+ trip to Pittsburgh again, but if I found myself in the city, I may just stop by.
paraphrase: I'm not really into beer, but my fiancee is a big fan, so I decided to join her. I'm happy I did! Our waitress was very knowledgeable about the wide variety of house-brewed beers and helped me choose one I'd like (the kolsch was very crisp, light, and easy to drink). The food was typical pub fare with a twist and was well-prepared. My burger was perfectly cooked and topped with onion petals and gorgonzola. The waitress suggested the macaroni and cheese as a side, which turned out to be a great choice. My only gripe is that most of the food was quite heavy, leaving me sprawled out on the couch for the rest of the night. I'm not sure I'd make the hour-plus journey to Pittsburgh again, but if I were in town, I might consider stopping by.

text: Came with my BF we started with the crab artichoke dip. It was very greasy and had virtually no crab. The wedge salad was good and crisp. I had the hazelnut crusted chicken. This has to be one of the worst dishes I've ever had it was like pancake syrup over breaded chicken. To finish the meal we had the homemade chocolate chip cookies served with hot fudge. Well there was no hot fudge it was chocolate syrup the ice cream was so crystallized from melting and re-freezing all around disappointing.
paraphrase: I visited with my boyfriend and we began with the crab artichoke dip. It was excessively oily and lacked any real crab. The wedge salad, however, was fresh and enjoyable. I ordered the hazelnut crusted chicken, which turned out to be one of the worst meals I've ever eaten; it tasted like pancake syrup on breaded chicken. We ended the meal with homemade chocolate chip cookies and what was supposed to be hot fudge. Instead, it was chocolate syrup, and the ice cream was filled with ice crystals from melting and refreezing. Overall, it was a letdown.

text:
Tom and I decided to try this place out before catching a late movie on a Saturday night. He is a beer buff, and neither of us had ever been there, so we gave it a go.

We walked in around 8pm and we were told that there would be an hour long wait. We put our names on the list and went shopping around the area. About 25 minutes later, our table was ready. Parking was a cluster since the lot is shared with Dave & Busters and a karaoke bar called Sing Sing. Tom dropped me off at the door so I could secure our table.

Our server, Jackie, approached me almost immediately and brought two waters to the table to start. She was very attentive and funny, and probably the best part of our experience at the restaurant. Tom ordered a Red Ale and to his surprise, didn't care for it. Jackie gave Tom three free samples of beers she thought he would like after talking to him about his beer drinking habits. He loved all three, and chose the most bizarre (and expensive) beer. Jackie switched the Red Ale to the beer of Tom's choice, and charged us for the less expensive beer. Very nice of her!

We started out with the Ball Park Pretzels with Jalapeno dip. The pretzels came out quickly and they were very fresh, not to mention huge! We only ate one and took the other to go. Very good app though! 

Tom ordered some sort of bacon cheeseburger, and I got the Chicken Fried Chicken, which was a pounded chicken breast, breaded and fried, over white cheddar mashed potatoes with a side of gravy and some apple coleslaw.  Tom's burger came with a small handful of fries, and my meal was very spread out on my plate, like something was missing. Not very good presentation at all. The food itself was okay, nothing to write home about. The potatoes were my favorite part. Tom said that his burger was mediocre.

Another note: The bathrooms are shared with the Sing Sing karaoke bar that is in the same building, so expect drunk singing women in the bathrooms and brides-to-be puking in the stall next to you after enjoying too much alcohol at their bachelorette party... Yuck.

Overall, we might come back for the beer and service, but not for the food. And DEFINITELY not to use the bathroom...
paraphrase:
Tom and I opted to check out this spot before heading to a late movie on a Saturday evening. He's really into beer, and since neither of us had been there before, we thought we'd give it a shot.

We arrived around 8pm and were informed there would be about an hour's wait. We added our names to the list and spent some time shopping nearby. After roughly 25 minutes, our table was ready. Parking was chaotic because the lot is shared with Dave & Busters and a karaoke bar called Sing Sing. Tom dropped me off at the entrance so I could secure our table.

Our server, Jackie, came over almost immediately and offered us two waters to start. She was very attentive and humorous, probably the highlight of our visit to the restaurant. Tom ordered a Red Ale, but to his dismay, he didn't like it. Jackie, after discussing his beer preferences, brought him three complimentary samples of beers she thought he'd enjoy. He liked all three and chose the most unusual (and priciest) one. Jackie replaced the Red Ale with Tom's chosen beer and charged us for the cheaper one, which was very kind of her!

We began with the Ball Park Pretzels with Jalapeno dip. The pretzels arrived quickly, were extremely fresh, and quite large! We only ate one and took the other to go. A very good appetizer!

Tom ordered a bacon cheeseburger, while I chose the Chicken Fried Chicken, which consisted of a pounded, breaded, and fried chicken breast over white cheddar mashed potatoes, accompanied by gravy and apple coleslaw. Tom's burger came with a small serving of fries, and my dish was spread out on the plate, making it seem like something was missing. The presentation wasn't great. The food was okay, nothing remarkable. The potatoes were my favorite part. Tom described his burger as mediocre.

Additionally, the bathrooms are shared with the Sing Sing karaoke bar in the same building, so be prepared for drunk women singing in the restrooms and brides-to-be getting sick in the stall next to you after having too much at their bachelorette party... Gross.

In summary, we might return for the beer and service, but not for the food. And DEFINITELY not to use the restroom...

text: I really miss the Northstar Amber but the specialty beers are always good.  Food is excellent and the service staff is friendly.
paraphrase: I truly long for the Northstar Amber, yet the specialty beers never disappoint. The food is outstanding, and the service team is welcoming.

text:
So after an evening of enjoying a comedy show at the Waterfront, we headed to Rock Bottom for some late night grub.  The place was pretty busy, we had to walk around the bar area for several minutes until a nice couple told us they were about to leave and we could have their bar table.  As nice as this was of them it was awkward standing over them as they finished their drinks paid their bill and left.  

So finally seated I look over the drinks and being the huge IPA lover that I am I went with that and it was indeed a good choice.  The hubby had the Red Ale which was also rather good.

The service was OK, not the best not the worst.  But moving on to more important thing, the food.  Talk about missing the mark hugely!  I ordered the mini street fish tacos.  You get four mini tacos with a marinated in lime piece of fish, and tomatillo salsa.  They were just strokes away from inedible.  I love fish tacos, I mean it is such a simple concept so why do people have such a hard time executing this dish? The shells were hard and literally split at the bottom when I picked them up.  The sour cream sprayed all over the plate only made them messy and the poor fish was just so overcooked.  If I wasn't so hungry I wouldn't have eaten them at all! Lesson learn on that.

The husband had the BBQ Chicken Pizza.  It had beans and corn with roasted red pepper on it along with some sour cream.  My husband is usually the type that will order these lovely specialty pizza's and ask for them to remove all the wonderful unique toppings!  For instance, he would usually just say, Chicken BBQ and cheese, nothing else!  I refused to let him do that this time, because I was really intrigued in the combination of ingredients on this pie.  And boy was I jealous when it came out.  It blew my crappy tacos out of the water!  Should have stuck with something simple.  

Furthermore I could care less if I come back to this location.  The beers were good but hell you can get a decent IPA just about anywhere anymore.  And the underwhelming food sealed the deal.  I know this is a chain, but it still doesn't have to stink...
paraphrase:
After spending the evening enjoying a comedy show at the Waterfront, we made our way to Rock Bottom for some late-night food. The place was fairly crowded, and we wandered around the bar area for several minutes until a friendly couple informed us they were about to leave and offered us their bar table. While this gesture was kind, it felt awkward to stand over them as they finished their drinks, paid their bill, and left.

Once we were finally seated, I browsed the drink menu and, being an avid IPA fan, opted for that, which turned out to be a great choice. My husband tried the Red Ale, and it was quite good as well.

The service was average, not exceptional but not terrible. Moving on to the more pressing matter, the food. It was a major disappointment! I ordered the mini street fish tacos, which consisted of four small tacos with lime-marinated fish and tomatillo salsa. They were just barely edible. I love fish tacos, and it's such a straightforward dish, so why is it so difficult for some to prepare it properly? The shells were hard and split at the bottom when I picked them up. The sour cream smeared all over the plate only added to the mess, and the fish was severely overcooked. If I hadn't been so hungry, I wouldn't have eaten them at all! Lesson learned on that front.

My husband ordered the BBQ Chicken Pizza. It came with beans, corn, roasted red pepper, and a dollop of sour cream. My husband typically orders these delightful specialty pizzas and requests the removal of all the unique toppings! For example, he'd usually just say, BBQ Chicken and cheese, nothing else! This time, I insisted he try it as is, as I was curious about the ingredient combination. I was envious when it arrived. It was far superior to my disappointing tacos! I should have stuck with something simple.

Moreover, I am indifferent about returning to this location. The beers were good, but you can find a decent IPA almost anywhere these days. The lackluster food was the final straw. I understand this is a chain, but that doesn't mean it has to be subpar...

text: Another good meal ... this time the Housemade Meatloaf. It's a blend of turkey and sausage instead of the standard ground beef. A refreshing and delicious twist on a comfort food staple. Served over cheddar mashed and mixed veggies. The server could have behaved like she wanted to be there but everyone has a bad day once in awhile. Service was prompt just the same. Overall good experience.
paraphrase: Once again, a satisfying meal ... this time featuring the Housemade Meatloaf. It's crafted from a mix of turkey and sausage rather than the usual ground beef. A delightful and refreshing take on a classic comfort dish. It comes with cheddar mashed potatoes and mixed vegetables. The server could have shown more enthusiasm, but everyone has an off day now and then. Nonetheless, the service was timely. All in all, a positive experience.

text: Planned a surprise birthday party here for my wife and they did an amazing job helping to pull it off! Service was extremely helpful, professional, and on point all night. Lauren (one of the managers) was reliable and pleasant throughout the entire planning process. If anyone is looking to plan an event I would highly recommend Rock Bottom. Couldn't be happier!
paraphrase: I organized a surprise birthday party for my wife at this venue, and they did a fantastic job assisting with the execution! The service was incredibly helpful, professional, and precise the entire night. Lauren, one of the managers, was dependable and enjoyable to work with during the whole planning stage. If you're considering hosting an event, I would strongly suggest Rock Bottom. I couldn't be more satisfied!

text: The staff here is very nice and the set up has TVs all around the family so enjoy mostly all sports, the music is nice and pretty mellow and the place is very spacey and the prices are pretty reasonable. And most importantly is the place seems to be kept clean at all times.
paraphrase: The employees are quite friendly, and the arrangement includes TVs everywhere, allowing families to watch nearly all sports. The music is pleasant and fairly laid-back, the venue is spacious, and the prices are fairly reasonable. Most importantly, the place appears to be consistently clean.

text: Good food and large portions. I ordered the Veggie Burger for $8.50 and I loved it! It's one of the most unique veggie burgers I have ever had: a homemade patty of beans, rolled oats and veggies. The chipotle mayo really gave it that oomph. My burger was more than enough for me to handle and everything was worth the price. As for drinks, I'm a complete noob when it comes to beer but my friend ordered a tap and even from a small taste, I could tell it was very good quality beer. Bitter and sweet, with a slight tang. I rarely drink beer but i I'm going to, then might as well drink the best kind there is - fresh and microbrewed.\n\nNOTE: There aren't too many options for vegetarians. The veggie burger was pretty good and I'd recommend it, but other than that, there's also the veggie tacos, a few select appetizers, and a few salads from which to choose.
paraphrase:
The food is great with generous servings. I tried the Veggie Burger, priced at $8.50, and absolutely enjoyed it! It's one of the most distinctive veggie burgers I've ever tasted, featuring a homemade patty made from beans, rolled oats, and vegetables. The chipotle mayo added a perfect kick. The burger was more than filling, and everything was well worth the cost. As for drinks, I'm clueless about beer, but my friend ordered one on tap, and even from a small sip, I could tell it was high-quality beer—bitter, sweet, with a slight tang. I rarely indulge in beer, but if I do, it might as well be the best—fresh and microbrewed.

NOTE: There aren’t many vegetarian choices. The veggie burger was really good and I'd suggest it, but beyond that, there are veggie tacos, a few select appetizers, and some salads to choose from.

text: The food is standard American fare--a bit overpriced in my mind--and the beers range from mediocre to very bad.  The K\u00f6lsch is the only decent beer of the bunch.  The service is good, but the wait for food or a table can be quite long (generally 20-30 minutes even for just beer and apps).  I've been to the one at the Denver airport as well, but I was stranded and had free meal tickets, so okay-but-nothing-special pizza from Rock Bottom it was.  Since it's a recognizable national chain, I think of places like this as more of a tourist brewery rather than a true Pittsburgh craft-brew experience.
paraphrase: The menu offers typical American dishes, which I find somewhat overpriced, and the beer selection ranges from subpar to quite poor, with the Kölsch being the only passable option. The service is commendable, but expect to wait a considerable time for a table or food, often 20-30 minutes even for just drinks and appetizers. I also visited the location at the Denver airport, but since I was stuck there with free meal vouchers, I settled for a mediocre pizza from Rock Bottom. As a well-known national chain, I perceive these establishments more as tourist-focused breweries rather than authentic Pittsburgh craft-brewing experiences.

text: The beer is good but the food is nothing to brag about at ALL. I've tried many things on the menu, and if you have developed a \"foodie\" palate, don't even bother, because you will be very disappointed. If you feel comfortable with chain restaurants and their lack of innovative touch (which, nothing wrong with that!) you will most likely enjoy Rockbottom. The only entree I did enjoy were the lobster and shrimp enchiladas (minus the repulsive beans and rice) and the asiago crab dip as an appetizer. Otherwise, you are better off skipping it.
paraphrase: The beer is decent, but the food leaves much to be desired. I've sampled numerous items from their menu, and if you've cultivated a taste for gourmet experiences, it's best to steer clear—you're likely to be let down. However, if you're content with the conventional offerings of chain eateries, Rockbottom might suit your tastes. The only dishes I found enjoyable were the lobster and shrimp enchiladas (excluding the unpleasant beans and rice) and the asiago crab dip as a starter. Otherwise, it's probably best to pass.

text:
I'm HUGE SNL fan, so naturally if someone who's been in the cast is touring, I'll come here to see them. Some have been really great...Jim Breuer. Some have been (surprisingly) very bad....Tracy Morgan. But it's always been a pleasant experience at the venue.  There's not a bad seat in the house and it's small enough that you get to see and hear everything from the stage.  Beware if you sit in the very front, you KNOW what's going to happen!

A couple downsides.... 

Waiting outside in a line in the dead of winter.  The lobby is so small you're forced to wait outside until the first show starts or the first show let's out.  And if you want a good seat, you may be waiting in line for a while.  BURR!

The two drink minimum. I kinda get why they do it, to make money, of course. But this means they jack up the prices of watered down drinks. Better to stick with beer and wine at these places. No frou frou drinks for me! 

Also, as to be expected, the food's not that great. In a perfect foodie world, places that served food would make it fantastic! Since that's not going to happen, you just go into those places hoping for the best. I've never had anything great here. Nacho platter too many re fried beans, to little nacho's....annnnnd I'm out! 

So what did we learn here? Good venue, stick to beer and wine and eat at a better restaurant beforehand. The talent is really what you're there for so, enjoy the show!
paraphrase:
I'm a big fan of SNL, so it's only natural that if a former cast member is performing nearby, I'll be there to watch.
