Some performances have been outstanding, like Jim Breuer's, while others, such as Tracy Morgan's, were unexpectedly disappointing. Nonetheless, the venue offers a consistently enjoyable experience. Every seat is good, and the space is intimate enough to catch all the stage action. Just be cautious if you sit right up front—you know what to expect!

A few drawbacks...

Standing in line outside during the freezing winter months. The lobby is so tiny that you're forced to wait outside until either the first show begins or the previous one ends. Plus, if you're aiming for a prime seat, expect to be in line for a while. Brr!

The two-drink minimum is another issue. I understand it's a revenue thing, but it results in inflated prices for watered-down drinks. It's wiser to stick with beer and wine at these venues. No fancy cocktails for me!

Additionally, as anticipated, the food isn't impressive. In an ideal world for food lovers, places serving meals would offer exceptional quality. Since that's unlikely, you just hope for decent fare. I've never had anything remarkable here. The nacho platter, for example, has too many refried beans and too few nachos... and I'm done!

So, what's the takeaway? It's a great venue, opt for beer and wine, and dine at a better restaurant beforehand. After all, you're really there for the talent, so enjoy the show!

text: I've been here twice now, and like the others you really only come to Improve Comedy Club for the show. Very reasonably priced for shows  though they have the two item minimum so would suggest they all be drinks.\n\nThe food leaves little to be desired. We decided for the sake of time to grab dinner at the venue since the show started at 7. Having a free appetizer coupon we ordered the spinach and artichoke dip along with the sauteed grilled chicken. Spinach and artichoke dip was good though cheesy rich along with cheese sprinkled on top of the chips. Sauteed grilled chicken - hmmm.... I think the chicken was frozen - definitely not fresh - and the guacamole was artificial with a glop of cheese. It looked like a mess with big ole pile of fries. A lot of food for $10. Service has always been pleasant though they do automatically tack on the tip for you at 18%.
paraphrase: I've visited this place twice, and like many others, the main reason to visit the Improve Comedy Club is the show itself. The ticket prices are quite reasonable, although there's a requirement to purchase two items, so it's best to make them drinks.\n\nThe food, unfortunately, is not impressive. We opted to have dinner at the venue before the 7 PM show. Using a free appetizer coupon, we got the spinach and artichoke dip and the sautéed grilled chicken. The dip was tasty but very cheesy, with cheese even on the chips. As for the chicken, it seemed frozen rather than fresh, and the guacamole was artificial with a dollop of cheese. The dish was a bit of a mess with a large helping of fries. It's a lot of food for $10. The service has always been friendly, though they automatically add an 18% tip.

text:
There's nothing like a Saturday night filled with some good laughs, a few chuckles and a couple of good guffaws thrown in for good measure.  That's exactly what we enjoyed on Saturday night!

We hit the comedy club for an evening of Tammy Pescatelli - the hubby and I have enjoyed her routine on TV and on the occasional DVE morning show..  She didn't fail to give us a good evening of laughs!  The big surprise, was Gene Collier - yep, of the Post-Gazette sports writer fame - as the opener.  What a great opening act!  He cracked wise about the perils of being 58 and a baby boomer.  We could relate!

The sightlines were lacking.  There was a rather large woman at the table in front of us with massive Pittsburgh BIG HAIR.  All night long my own head bobbed from side to side as I attempted to actually watch the show, and not just listen to it, as she reached repeatedly and unceasingly for her tableful of snacks.  

With a 2 drink minimum, my first drink was weak and the 2nd was quite strong!  All in all, the two balanced each other out.  But then, you don't go for the drinks, do you?  An enjoyable evening!
paraphrase:
There's nothing quite like a Saturday night brimming with hearty laughs, some light chuckles, and a few hearty guffaws for good measure. That's exactly what we experienced on Saturday night!

We headed to the comedy club to see Tammy Pescatelli—my spouse and I have enjoyed her performances on TV and occasionally on the DVE morning show. She certainly delivered a night full of laughter! The big surprise was Gene Collier, known for his work with the Post-Gazette as a sports writer, who opened the show. What an excellent opening act! He joked about the challenges of being 58 and a baby boomer, and we could definitely relate!

The sightlines were less than ideal. There was a rather large woman at the table in front of us with enormous Pittsburgh-style BIG HAIR. Throughout the night, I bobbed my head from side to side, trying to watch the show instead of just listening, as she constantly reached for her table full of snacks.

With a two-drink minimum, my first drink was weak, but the second was quite strong! Together, they balanced each other out. But then again, you don't go for the drinks, do you? A delightful evening!

text:
My friends & I all purchased Groupons  for the Pittsburgh Improv on or around March 22nd, 2012. At that time, only a few big name comedians were listed on the Improv's website as Special Events. You can use the Groupon on anything that isn't a Special Event. That seemd reasonable. The day the Groupons were purchased, my friend Katrina called & reserved two tickets to Jim Jeffries for 3/30 at 10pm. They allowed her to use her Groupon & gave her a confirmation code & everything! Shortly after, they changed many of the comedians on their website, including Jim Jeffries, to Special Events. When I called I was told I could not use my Groupon. The same thing happened to many of my friends. It's a classic bait & switch. These comedians were NOT Special Events until after the Groupon came out. In addition, other restrictions, such not being able to use the Groupons on Saturdays & having to use the additional four vouchers for one show, were NOT listed under the restrictions when the Groupon was for sale. 

So tonight, my friends & I had pretty much resolved ourselves to the fact that we were not going to the Improv because they wouldn't allow us to use our Groupons. But I walked over to the Will Call window with my friend Katrina so she could at least get her tickets. Big surprise - she was told they didn't have her reservation or her name in their computers! They wouldn't give her the tickets. It's an absolute joke of a deal. How is everyone going to use their Groupons by Sept. if all of the shows are Special Events now? 

In addition, no one at the Improv answers the phones or responds to emails. 

My friends & I (about 10 of us total) are all getting refunds through Groupon and/or our credit card companies & we are all reporting the Improv to the Better Business Bureau, etc. 

The Pittsburgh Improv is extremely shady & does not care about their customers.
paraphrase:
On or around March 22nd, 2012, my friends and I bought Groupons for the Pittsburgh Improv. At that time, only a handful of well-known comedians were listed as Special Events on the Improv's website. The Groupon could be used for anything that wasn't labeled as a Special Event, which seemed fair. The day we bought the Groupons, my friend Katrina called and reserved two tickets for Jim Jeffries on March 30th at 10 pm. They let her use her Groupon and even provided a confirmation code. Soon after, they reclassified many comedians, including Jim Jeffries, as Special Events on their website. When I called, I was informed that I couldn't use my Groupon. This same issue affected many of my friends. It's a clear bait and switch. These comedians weren't marked as Special Events until after the Groupon was released. Moreover, other restrictions, like not using Groupons on Saturdays and needing to use all four vouchers for one show, were not mentioned when the Groupon was available for purchase.

Tonight, my friends and I had mostly accepted that we wouldn't be attending the Improv because they refused to let us use our Groupons. However, I accompanied my friend Katrina to the Will Call window so she could at least pick up her tickets. To our surprise, they claimed they didn't have her reservation or her name in their system! They refused to give her the tickets. This deal is an absolute farce. How can everyone use their Groupons by September if all shows are now Special Events?

Additionally, no one at the Improv answers phone calls or replies to emails.

All ten of us are getting refunds through Groupon and/or our credit card companies, and we are all reporting the Improv to the Better Business Bureau and other relevant bodies.

The Pittsburgh Improv is incredibly untrustworthy and disregards its customers.

text:
The place does what it does best - Comedy.  The food, however.  Blah.

First off, If you go to a show, please note there is a two item per person minimum.  This means that from their extensive menu of overpriced bar food and drinks, you are REQUIRED to choose two items each.    

Second, the sodas are NOT FREE REFILLS!  I learned that one the hard way.  Get water. 

Third, before tipping your server, please make sure she hasn't already taken her cut out.  Even after paying for two very bland, overpriced meals and way too many Diet Cokes, I thought that my bill was rather high.  Upon further inspection, I noticed that my server had taken an 18% gratuity.  The menu explicitly says that an automatic gratuity will be taken out for parties of six or more, but there was only two of us and we weren't even drunk.  I found this tactic awfully presumptuous and left her tip at the 18% even though I typically tip well over 20%.    

Overall, I don't like the idea of being required to purchase food just to see a comedy show.  I can see why they do it, but if I were required to buy a crappy popcorn and overpriced pop every time I went to the movies, I would be far less inclined to go as I am not inclined to go to the Improv again.
paraphrase:
The venue excels at what it does best – Comedy. The food, on the other hand, is unremarkable.

To start, if you're attending a show, be aware there's a minimum requirement of two items per person. This means you must select two items each from their vast menu of expensive bar food and drinks.

Additionally, be advised that sodas do not come with free refills! I found this out the hard way. Opt for water instead.

Furthermore, before tipping your server, ensure she hasn't already included her gratuity. Despite paying for two tasteless, overpriced meals and too many Diet Cokes, my bill seemed unusually high. Upon closer examination, I realized my server had added an 18% gratuity. The menu clearly states this charge is for parties of six or more, yet there were only two of us, and we were sober. I found this assumption quite audacious and left her tip at 18%, even though I usually tip over 20%.

In conclusion, I dislike the obligation to purchase food just to enjoy a comedy show. I understand their reasoning, but if I had to buy mediocre popcorn and overpriced soda every time I went to the movies, I'd be much less inclined to go, just as I'm now reluctant to return to the Improv.

text:
The Pittsburgh Improv is unfortunately another great idea hamstrung by the details of mediocre execution (some specific examples at the end).

The setup is nice, a delightful--if a bit sterile--reproduction of what you'd think a comedy club should like if you watch enough TV.  The interior is clean; the seats are comfortable; and it's an OK atmosphere to see a show and laugh.

It's too small, however, to attract many of the best acts, which limits its ability to ever become the central hub for Pittsburgh comedy.  Most of the big names that have recently come through Pittsburgh--Jim Gaffigan, Louis CK, Second City--all perform elsewhere (though they managed to get Bob Saget, to their credit).

But the biggest problem is the food and drink.  They're very up front about their mandatory two item minimum, but the forced service requires that you spend extra money and get interrupted by waitstaff at least several times during the show.  The prices are well above what I'd consider reasonable given the quality, and I'd begrudgingly--or even happily--just shrug off the cost the same way I would a stadium concession stand markup...but the food is bad.  You're forced to either (a) buy extremely overpriced drinks (3.50 for a soda, for example, with no refills--or ten dollars for one with refills), or (b) buy moderately overpriced food which you wouldn't want in the first place.  I thought the ten dollar chicken sandwich looked reasonable, until a few bites in I decided I'd rather just throw away the money than keep eating it.

Still, I think this place has a lot of potential; the core idea is very workable; and they do attract some really good comedians.  Two stars for now, with high hopes for improvements.

A few specific drawbacks which could be VERY easily fixed:
1 - A lot of the drinks didn't have prices on them.  I know this isn't uncommon, but at a venue where every question you ask interrupts not only your own show-viewing experience as well as the experiences of your neighbors, it's an incredibly poor decision here.  Make everything on the menu exceptionally clear.  It's not that hard.
2 - Put the drink prices on the website too.  An Improv representative has personally responded to many of these Yelp reviews, and she once said "Our menus and pricing are listed on our website, which makes for easy planning!"  I'm calling shenanigans.  Not all the prices are on the website, which makes for difficult planning.
3 - Reminding me to tip my server during the show AND adding an automatic gratuity to the check makes me feel like you're trying to trick me.  It's kind of an unprofessional way to say goodbye to one's customers.
4 - Since the $3.50 soft drinks don't come with free refills, the servers shouldn't ask "would you like a refill on that?" or anything of the sort.  They should ask "would you like another one?" instead.  They should also respond to requests for refills by saying "we don't offer refills, but I'd be happy to get you another one."  Just because there's a 10 dollar refillable soda option on the menu doesn't mean people all figure it out.  Most patrons don't even bother reading the beverage section of the menu if all they want is a standard beverage offered almost everywhere (like a cola or diet cola), and the reading difficulties imposed by the darkness make them even less likely to browse the offerings.
paraphrase:
The Pittsburgh Improv, regrettably, is yet another promising concept hampered by the mediocre execution of its details (see specific examples at the end). The setup is appealing, resembling a charming—though slightly sterile—version of a comedy club as seen on TV. The interior is tidy, the seating is comfortable, and it provides a decent atmosphere for enjoying a show and some laughs.

However, it's too small to attract many top-tier performers, hindering its potential to become the main hub for comedy in Pittsburgh. Most big-name comedians who have recently visited Pittsburgh—like Jim Gaffigan, Louis CK, and Second City—perform at other venues (though they deserve credit for securing Bob Saget).

The most significant issue, though, is with the food and beverages. They clearly state their mandatory two-item minimum, but this enforced service means spending extra money and being interrupted by waitstaff multiple times during the show. The prices exceed what I consider reasonable for the quality, and while I might reluctantly—or even willingly—accept the cost, similar to a stadium's concession markup, the food itself is poor. You're forced to either (a) buy highly overpriced drinks ($3.50 for a soda, for instance, with no refills—or ten dollars for one with refills), or (b) purchase moderately overpriced food that is unappealing. The ten-dollar chicken sandwich seemed reasonable until a few bites made me prefer to simply waste the money rather than eat it.

Nonetheless, I believe this place has significant potential; the core idea is sound, and they do attract some excellent comedians. For now, it's two stars, but with great hopes for future improvements.

A few specific, easily fixable issues:
1. Many drinks lack pricing information. While not uncommon, at a venue where every question disrupts your own and your neighbors' experience, it's a particularly poor choice. Make everything on the menu abundantly clear. It's not difficult.
2. Include drink prices on the website as well. An Improv representative has responded to many Yelp reviews, claiming, "Our menus and pricing are listed on our website, which makes for easy planning!" I dispute that claim. Not all prices are on the website, making planning difficult.
3. Reminding me to tip during the show and adding an automatic gratuity makes it feel like a trick. It's an unprofessional way to bid farewell to customers.
4. Since $3.50 soft drinks don't include free refills, servers shouldn't ask, "Would you like a refill on that?" Instead, they should ask, "Would you like another one?" They should also respond to refill requests by saying, "We don't offer refills, but I'd be happy to get you another one." Just because there's a ten-dollar refillable soda on the menu doesn't mean everyone figures it out. Most patrons don't read the beverage section if they just want a standard drink available everywhere (like cola or diet cola), and the darkness makes it even less likely they'll browse the options.

text:
We came to the Improv for the first time on Friday night for my wife's work holiday party. We arrived early for dinner and drinks from a predetermined menu. Dinner started with a salad of clearly bagged, almost brown iceberg lettuce, with some croutons and frozen-then-thawed looking tomatoes that were pinkish at best. I took a few bites of this unappetizing poor excuse for a salad, and I can tell you it was downright tragic. Dinner arrived and since there wasn't any vegetarian options on this predetermined menu, we received a dish that was supposed to contain meat with the meat omitted. It was a huge pile of spaghetti in a bowl. It looked sloppy and tasted worse than it looked. The pasta was overcooked, and the tomato sauce was just watered down puréed canned tomatoes. I saw traces of onion in my dish but didn't taste them at all. I was so hungry that I salted the heck out of this abomination and took a few bites. Yuck. I didn't make a dent in this crapfest. For dessert we were given a choice of chocolate cake, which my wife ordered, and cheesecake, which I ordered. The chocolate cake had freezer burn. Inexcusable! It wasn't edible. The cheesecake was only a hair better, but it had obviously come out of the freezer as well. I know people aren't coming here for the food, but if you are FORCING them to order things off of your menu to fulfill some outdated requirements (two drink/two menu item minimum) then at least make some halfway decent food. It doesn't even have to be memorable...just not OFFENSIVE. Which it was!! Had I paid for any of this I would have made a huge stink, but I tried to be polite and push my food around and make small talk. 

The comedians were good. The venue was nice-ish and we got to sit in the front because we arrived early for our "event". Not always the best thing in a comedy club (we only got picked on a little bit). But damn, I was hungry! I found myself fantasizing about all of the crappy Waterfront restaurants and how I'd rather be there...how I'd rather be heating up a discount microwave dinner...how I'd rather have a giant bowl of Captain Crunch...how I'd rather go back in time and eat spaghetti from my high school cafeteria...what a dreadful dining experience.

My car was also violated in the parking lot. I had an adorable German license plate on the front of my adorable German car which was stolen while I was eating hideous food, which was just the slap in the face I wanted when finally retreating to my car. I feel sick in so many different ways about this experience as a whole.

The Improv should consider outsourcing the food prep to a catering company. I truly believe that's the only thing that can save this place. Do yourself a favor and eat beforehand, and just order a beer. Well, two I guess. Other diners at our table complained about the quality of the cocktails so I'd steer clear of those. Unless there is a can't miss type of comedian here, I can't ever picture going back to the Improv. I'd give the comedians 4 stars, and the food ZERO stars.
paraphrase:
We attended the Improv for the first time on Friday night for my wife's work holiday gathering. We got there early to have dinner and drinks from a fixed menu. The meal began with a salad made of clearly packaged, nearly brown iceberg lettuce, topped with some croutons and tomatoes that appeared frozen and thawed, with a pinkish hue at best. I managed a few bites of this unappealing excuse for a salad, and it was absolutely dismal. When the main course arrived, there were no vegetarian options on this set menu, so we were served a dish that was meant to include meat, with the meat simply left out. It was a large bowl of spaghetti. It looked messy and tasted even worse. The pasta was overly cooked, and the tomato sauce was nothing more than diluted puréed canned tomatoes. I spotted onion bits in my dish but couldn't taste them. I was so hungry that I heavily salted this disaster and tried a few bites. Disgusting. I barely touched this fiasco. For dessert, we had the option of chocolate cake, which my wife picked, and cheesecake, which I chose. The chocolate cake was freezer burnt. Unacceptable! It was inedible. The cheesecake was slightly better, but it was clearly from the freezer too. I understand people don't come here for the food, but if you're forcing them to order from your menu due to some outdated rule (two drink/two menu item minimum), at least offer some reasonably decent food. It doesn't need to be unforgettable...just not OFFENSIVE. Yet it was!! If I had paid for this, I would have made a huge fuss, but I tried to be courteous, pushing my food around and making small talk.

The comedians were entertaining. The venue was somewhat nice, and we got front-row seats because we arrived early for our "event." Not always ideal in a comedy club (we got teased a bit). But honestly, I was starving! I found myself daydreaming about all the subpar Waterfront restaurants and how I’d prefer to be there...or heating up a cheap microwave dinner...or having a big bowl of Captain Crunch...or going back in time to eat spaghetti from my high school cafeteria...what a dreadful dining experience.

Moreover, my car was tampered with in the parking lot. My charming German car’s cute German license plate was stolen while I was having a terrible meal, which was just the insult I needed when finally heading back to my car. I feel sick in numerous ways about this entire experience.

The Improv should consider hiring a catering company to handle the food. I genuinely think that's the only way to improve this place. Do yourself a favor, eat before you go, and just order a beer. Well, two, apparently. Others at our table complained about the cocktail quality, so I'd avoid those. Unless there’s an unmissable comedian performing, I can't imagine returning to the Improv. I’d rate the comedians 4 stars, and the food ZERO stars.

text:
This was my second visit to the club and it was still a blast. My girlfriends bought tickets for John Witherspoon for my bachelorette. We were all looking forward to it and it was hilarious! He was wonderful. 

The servers were fantastic. They always came back to check in on us and make sure we had plenty to eat and drink. I suggest the Pink Cadillac for a drink as well as the cheese cake and chocolate cake for dessert. 

The only reason it isn't 5 stars is because they had us wait an hour past the time we were to enter the building. We were to see the 9pm showing so the doors were to open at 8:45pm. They came out and explained that the show had run over and we wouldn't be able to even enter the building until 9:20pm. It was 28 degrees outside. We finally were able to enter the building, order drinks then the show finally started at 9:45pm.  The host did offer a blanket apology which was nice but hardly made up for the fact the show started almost an hour late.
paraphrase:
This was my second time at the club, and it was just as enjoyable as before. My friends purchased tickets to see John Witherspoon for my bachelorette party. We were all eagerly anticipating his performance, and it turned out to be hilarious! He was excellent.

The service staff were great. They frequently checked on us to ensure we had enough food and drinks. I recommend trying the Pink Cadillac cocktail, as well as the cheesecake and chocolate cake for dessert.

The only reason I'm not giving a full 5-star rating is due to the hour-long wait past our scheduled entry time. We were supposed to attend the 9pm show, with doors opening at 8:45pm. However, they informed us that the previous show had run late, and we couldn’t enter until 9:20pm. It was 28 degrees outside. Once we got inside and ordered drinks, the show eventually began at 9:45pm. Although the host did issue a general apology, it didn't quite compensate for the nearly hour-long delay.

text: Good! I can't comment on the food or the bathrooms, but from what experienced it was great! The drinks were decent, and I  believe you must order two items at minimum. The staff is quick, but they should have more people working on a Saturday night. Before you can ask for anything else they bolt to the next table or to the kitchen.\n\nThe comics we saw were funny as well. Will definitely go back!
paraphrase: Great! I can't speak about the food or the restrooms, but my experience was fantastic! The drinks were okay, and it seems like there's a requirement to order at least two items. The staff is fast, but they could use more team members on a Saturday night. They rush off to the next table or the kitchen before you can make another request. The comedians we watched were hilarious too. I will certainly return!

text: I love that we have a great comedy club in Pittsburgh with a great location at the Waterfront.  Perfect for a date night and I recommend going to a later show and grabbing dinner beforehand.  I received an online coupon code for $5 tickets for John Heffron. What a deal-def. check for deals before you pay full price for any shows here.  We went early to pick up the tickets at the ticket office.  People were lined up outside about an hour before the show and it was freezing out.  I had no interest in doing this so we grabbed a beer across the street at Rock Bottom. Once they opened the doors (15 minutes before show time) the line went quickly and even though we were at the end we still got seated at a table in the back.  It is not huge inside so there aren't really any bad seats.  They usually have an MC for the night who starts  warming up the crowd. This guy was funny and so was the opening act before John Heffron.  My only complaint about this place is that they have a two drink minimum and the prices are outrageous.  $9.50 for a mixed well drink and $7 for draft beers.  The soda was $3.50! Yikes!  At least we didn't pay full price for the tickets.
paraphrase: I'm thrilled that Pittsburgh boasts an excellent comedy club situated perfectly at the Waterfront. It's ideal for a date night, and I suggest catching a later show after dinner. I found an online coupon code for $5 tickets to see John Heffron, which is a fantastic deal—definitely look for discounts before paying full price for shows here. We arrived early to collect our tickets at the box office. People started lining up about an hour before the show, and it was freezing outside. I wasn't interested in waiting in the cold, so we grabbed a beer at Rock Bottom across the street. Once the doors opened (15 minutes before the show), the line moved quickly, and even though we were at the end, we still got a table in the back. The venue isn't large, so there aren't really any bad seats. They typically have an MC who warms up the crowd, and this guy was hilarious, as was the opening act before John Heffron. My only gripe with this place is the two-drink minimum, and the prices are steep—$9.50 for a mixed well drink and $7 for draft beers, with soda at $3.50. At least we didn't pay full price for the tickets.

text: I went here for the first time the other night and had a great time!  The comedian was hilarious and it was a great intimate setting for a fun night out with friends!  My only complaint was that it was a little pricey for the overall experience because not only do you need to buy a ticket, but every person needs to buy 2 items of food/drink and the drinks aren't cheap (around $10-12).  However, I would definitely go back sometime!
paraphrase: I visited this place for the first time recently and had an enjoyable experience! The comedian was very funny, and the venue provided a nice, cozy atmosphere for a fun night out with friends. My only issue was the cost, as in addition to purchasing a ticket, each person must buy two food or drink items, with drinks priced at about $10-12. Despite this, I would certainly consider returning in the future!

text: So...we got here and seated by 635pm. Orders placed. Drinks served. Now it is 752pm. One of my party has still not received his chicken sandwich. I had\nMy shrimp diablo. It was as described. Spicy and good!!! But hour plus for a chicken sandwich is unacceptable. We also sent an order of chicken fingers back...dryer than sand. And my fianc\u00e9s chicken Caesar salad chicken was as dry as the chicken fingers. Barely edible, in fact, she didn't eat more than 3 pieces of it. Upgrade the food service and this place would be somewhat a lil better.
paraphrase: We arrived and were seated by 6:35 PM. We placed our orders, and drinks were served. It is now 7:52 PM, and one of our group still hasn't received his chicken sandwich. I had the shrimp diablo, which was exactly as described—spicy and delicious! However, waiting over an hour for a chicken sandwich is just not acceptable. We also returned an order of chicken fingers because they were as dry as sand. Additionally, the chicken on my fiancé's chicken Caesar salad was as dry as the chicken fingers. It was barely edible; she only managed to eat three pieces. If they improved the food service, this place could be a little better.

text:
This is the best Giant Eagle in the area and is the most like grocery stores in Bay Area. Here's why:

1. They sell beer!
2. Huuuuuge produce selection
3. Natural/organic foods section: cereal, pasta, soups, etc and fancy chocolate bars
4. Long isle of ethnic foods includes mexican, indian, italian, asian and , of course, jewish
5. Functioning bakery with fresh, appetizing breads and desserts (I am still loyal to Allegro Hearth though)

The store is huge and I haven't been to all of it yet but they also have nicer flowers, balloons, home/seasonal section, DVD rentals and a photo center as well as a drive-through pharmacy. 

It seems as this GE just might be the answer to my grocery stores woes.
paraphrase:
This is the top-notch Giant Eagle in the vicinity, closely resembling grocery stores in the Bay Area. Here's why:

1. They offer beer!
2. Enormous selection of produce
3. Natural/organic foods section includes cereal, pasta, soups, and gourmet chocolate bars
4. Extensive aisle of ethnic foods features Mexican, Indian, Italian, Asian, and, naturally, Jewish options
5. A fully operational bakery with fresh, enticing breads and desserts (though I'm still loyal to Allegro Hearth)

The store is enormous, and I haven't explored all of it yet, but they also have better flowers, balloons, a home/seasonal section, DVD rentals, a photo center, and a drive-through pharmacy.

It seems this GE might just be the solution to my grocery store dilemmas.

text: Probably the most comprehensive supermarket in the county, if not all of southwestern PA. Its selection is considerably larger than what you can find in most Giant Eagles, and it has more upscale items. I live an easy walk from the Swissvale Giant Eagle, but I often drive to this location for the superior selection.
paraphrase: This might be the most all-encompassing supermarket in the county, possibly even in all of southwestern PA. It offers a significantly broader range of products than most Giant Eagles and includes more high-end items. Although I can easily walk to the Swissvale Giant Eagle, I frequently choose to drive to this store for its better selection.

text: Nice Giant Eagle, not as nice as their new Market Districts, but you can look at this Giant Eagle and see how they came up with the Market District Idea and rolled with it. Fresh made sushi, good cheese selection, awesome bakery.
paraphrase: This Giant Eagle is pleasant, although it doesn't quite match the appeal of their new Market District stores. However, you can see how this location inspired the Market District concept. They offer freshly made sushi, a good variety of cheese, and a fantastic bakery.

text:
Wahhhhh!!!! They're remodeling this one and it's frustrating! I swear I don't mind change. I really don't~  I should have seen this coming.  There were telltale signs of this happening. First, they switched the pharmacy to the other end of the store where the now defunct Iggle video was. That's A-ok with me, I didn't use the pharmacy anyway. 

But THEN, THEN they moved the whole toiletries section over there. They put my spaghetti and "ethnic food" section (by the way, I love that ethnic food includes spaghetti) to where my Tylenol was. My kitty food is now where my spaghetti once was. Can you tell how exasperated I'm getting?? 

Did I mention I don't mind change? But here's the thing, I really hate grocery shopping. It's kinda sorta my own fault because I must read every label to make sure I'm not eating something with 25 grams of fat and 5000 calories (even though ice cream may go into my cart from time to time.) So going to this Giant Eagle frequently was nice because I KNEW WERE EVERYTHING WAS. Now I don't....wahhh! 

Ok, I'll stop crying. I know I'll eventually be back. I'm sure (??) it will be an even better more magical grocery shopping experience once they're done. But I can't take it for right now. I'll be forced to shop at the Ghetto Bird down in the Southside until my former #1 store is all shiny and new again.
paraphrase:
Oh noooo!!!! They're renovating this place and it's driving me nuts! Honestly, I don't mind change—I really don't. I should have anticipated this. There were clear signs this was going to happen. First, they moved the pharmacy to the other end of the store where the now-closed Iggle video used to be. That's fine by me since I never used the pharmacy anyway.

But THEN, THEN they relocated the entire toiletries section over there. They've put my spaghetti and "ethnic food" section (it's funny that spaghetti falls under ethnic food) where the Tylenol used to be. My cat food is now where my spaghetti was. Can you tell how frustrated I'm getting??

Did I mention change doesn't bother me? But here's the thing: I really dislike grocery shopping. It's partly my fault because I have to read every label to ensure I'm not consuming something with 25 grams of fat and 5000 calories (even though I might occasionally add ice cream to my cart). So, visiting this Giant Eagle regularly was great because I KNEW WHERE EVERYTHING WAS. Now I don't....oh no!

Alright, I'll stop whining. I know I'll eventually go back. I'm sure (??) it will be an even more amazing grocery shopping experience once it's completed. But right now, I can't handle it. I'll have to shop at the Ghetto Bird down in the Southside until my former favorite store is all revamped and new again.

text:
You're getting an extra star for two solid reasons.  

One is because the remodel is over and the store is pretty again (and a bit fancier).  Two, and thee most important part, this iggle has the best quality and well made sushi out of any Iggle's out there!  Believe me, I've eaten many a rainbow roll from many different locations around the Burgh and nothing has compared.  Dare I say, it's as close as you get to going to a good sushi restaurant.  Bold statement, right? 

I  just love me some sushi and want it often, quick and convenient and this serves all those purposes with upholding the quality.  Need a quick sushi fix?  This place is it!
paraphrase: You're earning an additional star for two main reasons. First, the renovation is complete, and the store looks nice again, even a bit more upscale. Second, and most importantly, this Iggle offers the highest quality and best-made sushi compared to any other Iggle location! Trust me, I've tried numerous rainbow rolls from various places in Pittsburgh, and nothing compares. I might even say it's as close as you can get to dining at a good sushi restaurant. Quite a bold claim, right? I just love sushi and want it often, quickly, and conveniently, and this place meets all those needs while maintaining quality. Looking for a quick sushi fix? This is the spot!

text: This is easily one of the best Giant Eagles I've ever been to. Huge. Very nice selection. Easily comparable to Whole Foods. Went to their cheese market, and was pleasantly surprised with the selection. I got Beemster Vlaskas, an amazing Gouda. Very large parking lot.
paraphrase: This is definitely among the top Giant Eagles I've visited. It's enormous and has a very impressive selection, quite similar to Whole Foods. I visited their cheese section and was delighted by the variety. I picked up some Beemster Vlaskas, an excellent Gouda. The parking lot is also very spacious.

text: This place has amazing selection when it comes to some things and zero selection when it comes to others.  We popped in here a couple times over the weekend I spent in PIT and both times they didn't really have what we were looking for.  Visit #1: Liquid smoke.  Visit #2: Hawaiian rolls.  I know these items must exist in PA just not @G-Eagle.  \n\nThey did however have crazy selection when it came to things like....corned beef brisket or anything that has potato in it.  My californian mind was boggled by the variety of brands and cuts of corned beef that were available.  Too bad that wasn't what I was there for.
paraphrase: This place offers an incredible variety for certain items but lacks options for others. During the weekend I spent in PIT, we stopped by twice, and neither time did they have what we needed. On our first visit, we were looking for liquid smoke, and on the second, for Hawaiian rolls. I’m sure these products exist in PA, but not at G-Eagle. However, they did have an impressive array of items like corned beef brisket and anything potato-related. As a Californian, I was amazed by the assortment of brands and cuts of corned beef available, though unfortunately, that wasn't what I was seeking.

text: oddly enough, of all the giant eagles i have visited in pittsburgh, this is the only location that carries the toothpaste that i've used for most of my life (outside of pittsburgh). the location is clean and the staff are friendly.  i sometimes resent having a market district being the closest giant eagle to my apartment, as many of the items that i buy there are severely overpriced. but whenever i run out of groceries and toothpaste at the same time, i take the hike down to waterfront to shop here, although they may not carry all the groceries that i'd like.
paraphrase: Strangely, out of all the Giant Eagle stores I've been to in Pittsburgh, this is the sole location that stocks the toothpaste I've used for the majority of my life (outside of Pittsburgh). The store is tidy, and the employees are pleasant. I occasionally feel frustrated that the nearest Giant Eagle to my apartment is a Market District, as many of the products I purchase there are extremely overpriced. However, whenever I need both groceries and toothpaste, I trek down to the Waterfront to shop here, even though they might not have all the groceries I want.

text: I'm with Rachel C. on this one. You get an extra star for being a pretty Giant Eagle and a Cafe with a very decent beer selection of 6packs and individual bottles. Even though the Giant Eagle in Robinson is better in my opinion you, Waterfront Giant Eagle, are not bad and pretty close to where I live.
paraphrase: I agree with Rachel C. on this. You deserve an extra star for being a visually appealing Giant Eagle and having a respectable cafe with a decent variety of 6-pack and single bottle beers. Although I personally find the Giant Eagle in Robinson superior, you, Waterfront Giant Eagle, are not bad and conveniently located near me.

text: This review is specifically for the DFS cleaners at this Giant Eagle location.  I dropped clothing off for the sake of convenience once (I really should have known better).  Never again.  My items came back completely covered in lint.  I could let this slide, however my pencil skirt was safety pinned to the hanger.  The weight of the skirt caused even larger holes than what were placed there by the pins.  Being that it's a pencil skirt designed to have a blouse tucked in, there's no way that I can wear it again.  This was an expensive skirt, completely ruined.  Very disappointed.  Again, this review is just for the dry cleaners.  The rest of the store is actually better than a lot of other GE's I've been to in Pittsburgh.  Just don't take your dry cleaning here.
paraphrase: This feedback concerns the DFS cleaners at this Giant Eagle branch. I opted for the convenience of dropping off my clothes once (I really should have known better). I won't make that mistake again. My clothes were returned entirely covered in lint. I could overlook that, but my pencil skirt was secured to the hanger with safety pins. The skirt's weight caused the holes from the pins to become even more pronounced. Since the skirt is meant to be worn with a tucked-in blouse, it's now unwearable. This was an expensive skirt, now completely ruined. I'm very disappointed. To reiterate, this review pertains solely to the dry cleaners. The rest of the store is actually superior to many other Giant Eagles I've visited in Pittsburgh. Just avoid bringing your dry cleaning here.

text: One comfy seat in the entire place, but they have a cafe, they have good books to look at, and they are open until 11pm.  What more could you want?  Why an APPLE PURSE of course!  And a nice Earl Gray tea thrown in for good measure.  Wow...I love this place.  I am such a nerd...only a nerd would enjoy going to a book store and reading as much as I do.  The extra amenities just make it that much more enjoyable.
paraphrase: There's just one comfortable chair in the whole venue, yet they offer a cafe, have a great selection of books to browse, and stay open until 11pm. What else could you ask for? Naturally, an APPLE PURSE! Plus, a delightful Earl Gray tea for good measure. I absolutely adore this place. I'm such a nerd... only a nerd would relish visiting a bookstore and reading as much as I do. The additional perks make it even more pleasurable.

text:
Such nice staff.  

I really lament the decline of the modern bookstore.  I really enjoy browsing and picking up a new book.  

The store has a great selection and the staff are always helpful and kind.  

I really hope this store stays here for some.  The prices are also fair and often have great discounts.
paraphrase: The staff here are wonderful. I genuinely regret the downfall of contemporary bookstores, as I love exploring and discovering new books. This store offers an excellent variety, and the team is consistently supportive and friendly. I truly wish this place remains open for a while. The prices are reasonable, with frequent good deals.

text:
I have to agree with Matthew U on this one. I do enjoy Barnes & Nobles but sometimes i get lost just trying to find a darn calendar. I went here after a dinner date with some neighbors and couldn't believe how packed it was. We had to wait 10 minutes just to find enough chairs to seat 4 people. We ended up stealing them from other tables. Not to mention my hubby stood in line for 15 minutes just to get an Ice tea lemonade.

So amidst all the books, newspapers and magazines they have an audio and video section. The prices aren't anything to rave about. I can buy the same movies online for a lot cheaper there's just the waiting period. I do shop here sometimes for there discount books. I found a lot of great recipe and mystery books for really cheap, like under $5. That's a pretty good deal but considering there is a half priced book store who has a great selection of books for literally half the price of retailers, how can you beat those prices. 

B&N is more of a hangout spot with books in case you want to buy one right there and then. But everything is kind of confusing you always need to ask where something is. I'd rather shop online for anything i really want. It's just not worth it to buy it wholesale.
paraphrase:
I have to agree with Matthew U on this topic. While I do enjoy Barnes & Noble, sometimes it’s a challenge just to locate a simple calendar. After a dinner date with some neighbors, I was surprised by how crowded it was. We had to wait 10 minutes just to find enough chairs for four people, eventually borrowing them from other tables. Plus, my husband waited 15 minutes in line just to get an iced tea lemonade.

Among all the books, newspapers, and magazines, they also have an audio and video section. The prices aren't impressive; I can find the same movies online for much cheaper, though there is a waiting period. I do occasionally shop here for their discounted books, having found many excellent recipe and mystery books for under $5, which is a great deal. However, considering there’s a half-price bookstore with a great selection for literally half the retail price, it’s hard to compete with those prices.

Barnes & Noble feels more like a hangout spot that happens to have books if you decide to buy one on the spot. However, everything is somewhat disorganized, requiring frequent assistance to find items. I prefer shopping online for things I truly want, as buying in-store just doesn’t seem worthwhile.

text: I stopped here yesterday with Rob and Matt after golf to have a few drinks prior to seeing \"The Hangover\" at Loews across the street.  Incidentally, \"The Hangover\" is one of the funniest movies I've seen in years and worth the $10 to see it in the theater.  Okay, back to the restaurant the space itself is very nice.  There are plenty of hightops, plasmas and a few booths.  It's spacious and they have outdoor seating and open windows.  We sat at the bar and were greeted relatively quickly by the bartender who was tending a pretty much empty bar.  I ordered a guinness as usual, Rob ordered a long island iced tea and Matt had a miller light.  Make sure and tell them if you want a top shelf tea else you'll be getting the stuff in the plastic bottles.  I sampled Rob's long island tea and it was not that good way too much sours.  The next one he ordered was much better after a little guidance to the bartender.  The bartender herself was okay, not too friendly but quick enough with the pours.  I don't remember her name which is usually not a good sign.  We ordered a few things from the appetizer menu.  We started with the chips and salsa.  The chips were crisp enough, but not served warm and the salsa had some spice but the colors were a little off kind of looked like the color of pea soup.  Rob then ordered the philly cheese steak sliders.  I tried one it was okay I guess  the bun was warm and it was pretty loaded with cheese and onions.  He asked for a side of mayo and they said they were out, which is bizarre because there is a giant eagle a couple blocks away.  This pretty much sums up my experiences at the Bar Louie's I've been too.  They kind of do just enough for me to come back but rarely make any real effort with service.  Maybe I'm being too critical... I tried to order the baked goat cheese, but they were out... So, I settled on the hummus appetizer.  This was surprisingly pretty good.  The pita was warm and toasted the hummus had lots of flavor and the tabouleh was edible.  It's probably best to stick to simple stuff when dining here.  Matt had the hot wings which I tried one and it was okay, your pretty standard mass produced chicken wing.  All in all this place isn't that bad.  If you want to grab a drink before or after the movie and you're more concerned with convenience than necessarily friendly service and really good food than you could do worse.
paraphrase: Yesterday, after a round of golf, Rob, Matt, and I stopped by this place for a few drinks before heading to see "The Hangover" at Loews across the street. By the way, "The Hangover" is one of the funniest films I've watched in years and definitely worth the $10 theater ticket. Now, back to the restaurant: the venue is quite nice, featuring plenty of high tables, plasma screens, and a few booths. It's roomy, with outdoor seating and open windows. We sat at the bar and were served fairly quickly by the bartender, who was attending to an almost empty bar. I ordered my usual Guinness, while Rob got a Long Island iced tea, and Matt chose a Miller Lite. If you prefer a top-shelf tea, make sure to specify, or you'll end up with the stuff from plastic bottles. I tasted Rob's Long Island iced tea, and it wasn't very good—too much sour mix. However, the next one was better after some guidance to the bartender. The bartender was alright, not overly friendly but efficient enough with the drinks. I can't recall her name, which usually isn't a positive sign. We decided to try some appetizers, starting with chips and salsa. The chips were crunchy but not warm, and the salsa was spicy but had an odd color reminiscent of pea soup. Rob ordered the Philly cheesesteak sliders, and I tried one; it was decent, with a warm bun and plenty of cheese and onions. He requested a side of mayo, but they were out, which was strange given there's a Giant Eagle a few blocks away. This experience sums up my visits to Bar Louie locations—they do just enough to bring me back but rarely excel in service. Perhaps I'm being too harsh... I wanted the baked goat cheese but it wasn't available, so I opted for the hummus appetizer. It was surprisingly good, with warm, toasted pita and flavorful hummus, and the tabbouleh was edible. It's probably best to stick to simple dishes here. Matt had the hot wings, and I tried one; it was okay, your typical mass-produced chicken wing. Overall, this place isn't bad. If you're looking for a drink before or after a movie and prioritize convenience over friendly service and exceptional food, you could do worse.

text:
The Pros: Great view of the city and river in good weather...  
Lovely for loud large groups
Varied menu with some original items
Great place to watch a game inside with a huge bar
Fun location
Good place to take out of towners.

The Cons: The food when I went yesterday was not great.  We got the wrong sandwich, our order wasn't as described because the kitchen was out of "lots of things"  (Tell us when we order, not when you deliver the meal without elements on the plate.)  
Can be loud--not a place for a romantic date...
The bathroom condition was really bad.

The server did comp up an appetizer, and they were friendly enough.. they just failed to wow. 

The view was great, and we did enjoy walking around seeing the fountain dance to "Pour some sugar on me"  seriously?!?!?  Yes!   Classy eh?
paraphrase:
Pros: The city and river view is fantastic in nice weather...  
Perfect for large, boisterous gatherings
The menu is diverse with some unique options
Ideal spot for watching a game indoors with a massive bar
Entertaining location
Great for hosting out-of-town guests.

Cons: The food was disappointing when I visited yesterday. We received the wrong sandwich, and our order didn't match the description because the kitchen was out of "many things" (Inform us while ordering, not upon delivery without certain items.)  
It can get loud—not suitable for a romantic evening...
The bathroom was in poor condition.

The server did offer a free appetizer, and they were quite friendly... just not remarkable.

The view was excellent, and we enjoyed strolling around watching the fountain dance to "Pour Some Sugar on Me"—seriously?!?!? Yes! Quite classy, right?

text:
I had the EXACT same experience as John R. Went here to get a quick bite to eat before seeing a movie across the street. The place was near empty and there were three waitresses. Got seated and waited on immediately. Ordered drinks, and it took them 15 minutes to come...no, not alcoholic drinks, two waters and an iced tea. This should have been a bad sign, but at this point we ordered food.

20 minutes go by...we flag down our waitress, to let her know that we need to get to a movie in 45 minutes. She said our food would be out in a few minutes. Another 20 minutes, still no food, and our waitress is nowhere to be seen. We flag down another waitress, she says she will find our waitress, who we then notice is chatting with one of the other waitresses. She finishes her conversation and finally makes her way over to our table about five minutes later. She says she didn't even realize our food hadn't arrived (well, you never even checked on us, so of course you didn't). After speaking with the kitchen staff, she informs us that she "definitely" put our order in so it "must have been given to someone else by mistake." Because they wouldn't have noticed that that WASN'T what they ordered? Seriously?

The waitress told us she could give us our food for take out and, since we were starving, we reluctantly agreed. 10 minutes later, our food and the bill arrived. All that was comped was the iced tea, and we got some free utensils and wetnaps. How generous! We shovelled what we could into our mouths, did NOT leave a tip (thankfully, we didn't have the added gratuity that John had), and sprinted to our movie where we covertly finished our smuggled food. Worst part? I opened the wetnap, and it was totally dry. Of course.

However, I gave this place two stars instead of one. That's because my food, when I finally got it, was perfect. I had the Greek burger and it was huge, cooked exactly how I like it, with delicious toppings. I really wish I could come back here for the food but...hell no.
paraphrase:
I had the exact same experience as John R. I went to this place for a quick meal before catching a movie across the street. The restaurant was almost empty, and there were three waitresses. We were seated and served right away. We ordered drinks, which took 15 minutes to arrive—not alcoholic drinks, just two waters and an iced tea. This should have been a warning, but we went ahead and ordered food.

After 20 minutes, we stopped our waitress to mention we needed to make a movie in 45 minutes. She assured us our food would be out shortly. Another 20 minutes passed with no food, and our waitress was nowhere in sight. We approached another waitress who promised to find her. We then noticed our waitress chatting with a colleague. She finished her conversation and finally came to our table about five minutes later. She admitted she hadn't realized our food hadn't arrived (well, she never checked on us, so obviously she didn’t know). After talking to the kitchen staff, she claimed she definitely placed our order, so it "must have been given to someone else by mistake." Because they wouldn’t notice that wasn’t what they ordered? Seriously?

The waitress offered to pack our food to go, and since we were hungry, we reluctantly agreed. Ten minutes later, our food and the bill arrived. All they comped was the iced tea, plus we got some free utensils and wetnaps. How generous! We quickly ate what we could, left no tip (luckily, we didn’t have the added gratuity that John did), and rushed to our movie, where we discreetly finished our food. The worst part? I opened the wetnap, and it was completely dry. Of course.

Despite everything, I rated this place two stars instead of one. That’s because when I finally got my food, it was perfect. I had the Greek burger, which was huge, cooked exactly as I like it, with tasty toppings. I really wish I could return for the food, but... absolutely not.

text:
Let me just say that this used to be a place I loved.

Remember when you'd go somewhere and have good food, great service and a general pleasant experience and then a few months you'd go back and have it all again? Well one day you decide to go back and you realized everything has changed in a way that you can't describe and the place is no longer familiar. To me, that is Bar Louie

I can't say the food is bad. I had an order of the pretzel sticks for an appetizer and the cinnamon sauce tasted like water and the pretzels were stale. I will say I still ate them so shame on me. My  sandwich (the Mario) as always was very good.

Now here comes the bad:

1. There were fruit flies EVERYWHERE. I felt like we were eating on top of a garbage dump. At one point I had to get a new drink because one flew in my beer. I understand that the establishment is an indoor/outdoor place but there is no reason for 15 fruit flies to hover over my table.

2. The place is enormous and they had one, very nice, but over worked server. The girl looked like she was about to have a heart attack because she never stopped moving.

3. Because of number two our service in general was not good. I can't totally blame the girl she did the best we could. I mean. I've played Diner Dash on my iPad, I know what happens when a place fills up too quickly when you are the only one working.

It's sad when a place you love turns into a place you never want to go again. I think I'm being very generous by giving it two stars but because I used to love it so much I can't rate it that low. Part of me hopes it gets better, but like an absentee father it'll just let me down again.
paraphrase:
Let me just express that this was once a place I cherished.

Remember when you’d visit a spot and enjoy excellent food, fantastic service, and an overall delightful experience, and then, a few months later, return to find the same charm? Then, one day, you decide to go back and realize everything has changed beyond words, and the place feels unfamiliar. For me, that’s Bar Louie.

I can’t claim the food is terrible. I ordered the pretzel sticks as an appetizer, and while the cinnamon sauce was watery and the pretzels were stale, I still ate them, so that’s on me. However, my sandwich (the Mario) was, as always, very good.

Now for the negatives:

1. There were fruit flies everywhere. It felt as if we were dining on a garbage heap. At one point, I had to get a new drink because one flew into my beer. I understand the place is both indoor and outdoor, but there’s no excuse for having 15 fruit flies hovering over my table.

2. The venue is huge, yet there was only one server, who was very nice but overworked. She seemed on the brink of exhaustion, constantly on the move.

3. Due to the second issue, our service was generally lacking. I can’t entirely blame the server; she did her best. I’ve played Diner Dash on my iPad, so I understand what happens when a place gets too crowded with only one person working.

It’s disappointing when a beloved place becomes one you’d rather avoid. I think I’m being quite generous by giving it two stars, but because I used to love it so much, I can’t rate it any lower. Part of me hopes it improves, but like an absentee father, it’ll just disappoint me again.

text:
We were going to see a movie that started at 9:40pm. The 2 of us got to Bar Louie at 8:30.  We sat outside, and it was nearly 30 minutes before the waiter even stopped to get our order.  I tried stopping a waitress but was told 'this isn't my table'.  Finally the waiter took our order.  1 Sam Adams light bottle, and one Pineapple Juice+Vodka.  After about 15minutes another waiter (maybe the manager I don't know) asked if we had been waited on. We said yes, and told him we had been waiting.  He left, and another (different) waitress came to bring us our drinks.. they were wrong.. My date was given vodka on the rocks. and I was given a Sam Adams.. 
They took these back, and another 10 minutes before the correct order was served.. We told them to bring another round and the check.  It was 20 minutes before they returned.. and they forgot the check.

By this time we had to go to be on time for the movie.. my date asked for the check again so we could leave.. finally they brought it and I gave him my card, when he came back we signed it and left in a hurry to catch the movie.

for 2 bottle beers, and 2 pineapple juice + vodka I was charged $31.03. I didn't have time to complain or ask. I won't be going back.
paraphrase: We planned to catch a movie starting at 9:40pm and arrived at Bar Louie at 8:30. We opted to sit outside and had to wait almost 30 minutes before a waiter came to take our order. I attempted to get the attention of a waitress, but she said, "this isn't my table." Eventually, the waiter took our order for a Sam Adams Light and a Pineapple Juice with Vodka. After about 15 minutes, another staff member, possibly the manager, asked if we had been served. We confirmed we had, but mentioned the delay. He left, and another waitress brought our drinks, but they were incorrect; my date received vodka on the rocks, and I got a Sam Adams. They took these back, and it was another 10 minutes before we received the right drinks. We ordered another round and requested the bill. It took 20 minutes for them to return, and they forgot the check. By then, we had to leave to make it to the movie on time, so my date asked for the check again. It was finally brought to us, and I handed over my card. After signing, we rushed out to catch the movie. I was charged $31.03 for two bottle beers and two pineapple juice with vodka. I didn't have time to question the bill and won't be returning.

text:
Although I wouldn't  travel out of my way to have dinner here, this is a resturant that I enjoy having a meal at, when I am in the Homestead water front. On all of my visits here I have enjoyed the tempura shrimp. The dipping sauce are to die for.  I've found myself craveing the tempura shrimp, and the accompanying sauces. 

The first time I ever had dinner here was as a birthday dinner with me and my big sister. So this resturant has sentimental value to me. I attach eating here to having a special night out in the Waterfront. 

Bar Louie has some very flavorful and fun dishes for bar food, that is.
paraphrase:
While I wouldn't go out of my way to dine at this restaurant, I do enjoy having a meal here whenever I'm at the Homestead Waterfront. Each time I've visited, I've loved the tempura shrimp—the dipping sauces are absolutely amazing. I've even found myself craving the tempura shrimp and those delectable sauces. 

My first experience dining here was a birthday dinner with my big sister, so this place holds sentimental value for me. I associate eating here with a special night out at the Waterfront. 

Bar Louie offers some very tasty and enjoyable dishes, especially for bar food.

text: When the ratio to wait staff to diners is 3:1 you'd think you could get service!!!\n\nNothing erks me more then seeing idol wait staff and hungry patrons. I very rarely walk out of a restaurant with out having eaten first. I came here on Saturday ay 6pm. Almost none of the diners had food on their table and many of them didn't have drinks in the 15 minutes I was there I counted 9 waitress again I counted 9 waitresses and not a one of them came over to our table to get even a drink order. Just from the looks of it I could tell staying any longer would be a total train wreck. So after 15 minutes of waitress counting we left with empty bellies.
paraphrase: When the staff-to-diner ratio is 3:1, you'd expect to receive service! Nothing frustrates me more than watching idle staff and hungry customers. I almost never leave a restaurant without eating. I visited at 6pm on Saturday. Hardly any diners had food on their tables, and many didn't have drinks. During the 15 minutes I was there, I counted nine waitresses, and not one approached our table to take even a drink order. It was clear that staying longer would be a disaster, so after 15 minutes of counting waitresses, we left without eating.

text: I came here last night to get a few appetizers before a movie, and was hoping to be able to write a review on the food. Unfortunately, I cannot because the service was so bad that I left before I had the chance to order. We found seats at the bar behind the taps. After sitting there for 5 minutes and not being acknowledged, we thought that they might not be able to see us, and so we moved to a different pair of seats in the middle of the bar, grabbing a few menus on the way so we would be ready to order. It was fairly busy, but it is not like there were people standing at the bar trying to get drinks - everyone was seated and I'm sure they face much busier times. We sat in our new seats for almost 15 minutes (I timed it - I am not exaggerating), and were not even acknowledged. You would at least think the bartender would acknowledge us and tell us he would be with us when he could, but despite staring him down almost the whole time, we could not even make eye contact. I hope this is a fluke but will probably not be back to find out.
paraphrase: I visited last night to grab a few appetizers before a movie, intending to write a review about the food. Sadly, I can't do that because the service was so poor that I left before placing an order. We initially found seats at the bar behind the taps, but after sitting there for 5 minutes without any acknowledgment, we suspected we were out of sight. So, we moved to another set of seats in the middle of the bar, picking up a few menus on our way to be ready to order. Although it was fairly busy, there weren't people standing at the bar for drinks—everyone was seated, and I'm sure they handle busier times. We stayed in our new seats for almost 15 minutes (I timed it, so I'm not exaggerating) without being acknowledged. You'd expect the bartender to at least acknowledge us and say he'd assist us soon, but despite watching him almost the entire time, we couldn't even make eye contact. I hope this was an anomaly, but I likely won't return to find out.

text: The absolute worst service I have ever had at any bar or restaraunt.  And, in looking at other reviews, I am not the first.  There are many options at the Waterfront, and I would suggest you try any of them; but stay far away from this place!
paraphrase: I've never experienced service as terrible as this at any bar or restaurant. After reading other reviews, it's clear I'm not alone. With plenty of choices at the Waterfront, I recommend exploring any of them—just steer clear of this place!

text: $1 burger night on Tuesday. Add ons more.
paraphrase: Tuesday features $1 burger night. Additional toppings cost extra.

text: It was the first time that I went to this location.  We sat outside and it was a beautiful night.  I had the BBQ Burger with fries.  For the price of the burger I expected something much better.  It was a very thin burger when it came to the meat.  As a whole it looked large but that was only because of what they piled on top of it.  In addition, their drinks are expensive!  Just one cocktail could run between $10-$12 and they aren't very large.  Our waitress spent more time talking to her friends at the next table than actually tending to our needs.  There are enough restaurants at the Waterfront that I don't know that I will come back here.
paraphrase: This was my first visit to this place. We chose to sit outdoors, and the weather was lovely. I ordered the BBQ Burger with fries, but given the price, I anticipated something much better. The burger's meat was quite thin, and its overall size was deceptive because of the toppings. Additionally, their drinks are pricey; a single cocktail costs between $10 and $12 and isn't very big. Our waitress was more focused on chatting with her friends at the nearby table than attending to us. With so many dining options at the Waterfront, I'm uncertain if I'll return here.

text:
I personally enjoyed my recent visit to Bar Louie with a friend but completely understand the poor reviews on help. The restaurant is pricey for what it is, and our waitress was a bit lackluster.

So why does Bar Louie get 3 stars? Happy Hour and a nice patio!

Happy Hour is on Monday-Friday from 4-7pm and offers lots of different options -- $3 Drafts, $4 Wines, $5 Martinis, and 1/2 Price Select Appetizers and Flatbreads. My friend and I ordered 4 Martinis and 3 Appetizers between the 2 of us, and our bill was less than $40 total. Not bad!

I ordered a Strawberry Peach Bellini and a S'Mores Martini, and both were tasty. We shared Bruschetta Pomodoro, Spinach & Artichoke Dip, and Roasted Vegetable Flatbread. The food was also good, and I would order all three items again in the future.

My recommendation is to understand what Bar Louie is good at (Happy Hour) and only frequent the restaurant at those times!
paraphrase:
I recently visited Bar Louie with a friend and had a good time, but I can see why it gets negative feedback regarding service. The place is a bit too expensive for what it offers, and our server didn't stand out.

So, why does Bar Louie earn 3 stars? It's all about Happy Hour and the pleasant patio atmosphere!

Happy Hour runs from Monday to Friday, 4-7pm, with a variety of deals -- $3 Drafts, $4 Wines, $5 Martinis, and 50% off select appetizers and flatbreads. My friend and I ordered 4 Martinis and 3 appetizers, and our total bill was under $40. Not bad!

I tried a Strawberry Peach Bellini and a S'Mores Martini, both of which were delicious. We shared Bruschetta Pomodoro, Spinach & Artichoke Dip, and Roasted Vegetable Flatbread, all of which were tasty and I'd happily order again.

My suggestion is to focus on what Bar Louie excels at (Happy Hour) and visit during those times!

text:
This is one of those places where the presentation is nice, but the taste is too ordinary. Friends and I went here for happy hour (starting at 5pm). They claimed to have $1 draft, $5 mixed drinks, and half off appetizers. When we got there, we realized the happy hour special only applies to certain items, so pretty much the discount was pointless. We ended up spending a fortune on beer and mediocre appetizers. 

I only had appetizers here so I can't really judge them for their entrées, but I will rate according to my eating/drinking experience.  I got the spinach artichoke dip. It was a little runny and the dip to chips ratio was disappointing. For a dip, it had way too many obstacles built in to it. I don't know what they did with the artichoke, but it was super fibrous...I felt like I was chewing grass. 

Overall, I would not recommend this place unless you have money to make it rain. Food was way too average for the high price.
paraphrase: This is one of those spots where the presentation is appealing, but the flavor is quite bland. My friends and I visited during happy hour, which starts at 5pm. They advertised $1 drafts, $5 mixed drinks, and half-priced appetizers. However, when we arrived, we found out that the happy hour deals were limited to specific items, making the discount virtually useless. We ended up spending a lot on beer and unimpressive appetizers. I only tried the appetizers, so I can't comment on their main courses, but I'll share my thoughts based on what I experienced. I ordered the spinach artichoke dip, which was somewhat runny and had a disappointing dip-to-chip ratio. The dip had too many hurdles to enjoy it properly. I'm not sure what they did to the artichoke, but it was incredibly fibrous, like chewing on grass. Overall, I wouldn't recommend this place unless you're willing to spend a lot. The food was too average for the steep price.

text:
Meh, I just don't see the appeal Bar Louis has to Pittsburghers. I guess this particular restaurant benefits from people who work at the Waterfront stopping in after a shift. It's always packed. Being so close to the AMC theater, they get traffic before and after movies. 

It's expensive, the food is so so. Before I get ahead of myself, I had the BBQ ranch chicken salad without chicken. The BBQ ranch dressing was very good, but it's very easy to duplicate at home. It's equal parts ranch dressing and KC Masterpiece hickory BBQ sauce and a drop or two of Worcestershire and a drop of franks hot sauce. 

Tuesday is dollar burgers 5 until midnight, daily drink and happy hour selections, but even that's not enough to get me here. Skip it. One caveat for this location, free parking. At Station Square's location you gotta pay to park the jalopy.

I'm eager to see if this place sees a huge drop in business when Burgatory opens across the street.
paraphrase:
Honestly, I don't really get what Pittsburghers find appealing about Bar Louis. It seems like this restaurant mainly benefits from workers at the Waterfront who drop by after their shifts, which is why it's always so crowded. Its proximity to the AMC theater also means it attracts customers before and after movie showings. 

The place is pricey, and the food is mediocre. To give an example, I had the BBQ ranch chicken salad without chicken. While the BBQ ranch dressing was quite good, it's nothing special and can easily be made at home with equal parts ranch dressing and KC Masterpiece hickory BBQ sauce, plus a couple of drops of Worcestershire and Frank's hot sauce.

On Tuesdays, they offer dollar burgers from 5 p.m. to midnight, along with daily drink and happy hour deals, but even that doesn't entice me to visit. I'd say it's worth skipping. One advantage of this location is the free parking, unlike the Station Square spot where you have to pay to park. 

I'm curious to see if they'll experience a significant drop in business once Burgatory opens right across the street.

text: I am normally a Bar Louie Fan - my husband and I LOVE the one in Tampa.  This location needs a lot of work.  We went on a Friday evening before a show at the Improv.  We were looking to meet up with friends and have drinks and a quick bite to eat.  We never got to the food part because of the level of service that we received.  We got there at about 6:40/6:45 so Happy Hour was wrapping up, but I would say that the bar was not even 3/4 full.  There were also plenty of empty tables.  This should have been our sign to run in the opposite direction.  But we grabbed a table.  I am not a clock watcher, so I am not sure how long it took us to get a server but it was at least 5-7 minutes.  Then we got our drinks at 7:04.  The only reason that I know is because I was looking at my phone because my husband and I wanted to get some apps ordered before the end of Happy Hour.  That went out the window.  When our friends for there; they ordered drinks.  We finished them and left.  The server was nice, that is not the issue.  The issue was how GROSSLY UNDERSTAFFED they were.  They had two bartenders on (I think) and not nearly enough cocktail servers.  The server apologized for the wait on the drinks, but explained it away with \"this happens at the end of happy hour because the bar get swamped\".  That is a sign that you need more staff.  I have never had this happen at any other bar before.  Get more staff.  We ended up passing up on food because we were worried about time, not the fact that we did not get HH pricing.  Even though, in my opinion, they should have honored HH pricing since we were ready to order food at 7:50/7:55.  We ended up going to the Improv and got food there.  Bar Louie lost a great deal of my money because their lack of staffing and they will never get our Pittsburgh business again or our friends who live there.
paraphrase: I usually enjoy Bar Louie and my husband and I absolutely love the Tampa location. However, this particular branch needs significant improvement. We visited on a Friday evening before a show at the Improv, hoping to catch up with friends over drinks and a quick meal. Unfortunately, we never got to the food due to the service we experienced. We arrived around 6:40/6:45, just as Happy Hour was ending, and noticed the bar was less than three-quarters full with many empty tables. This should have been a warning to leave, but we sat down. I don't typically watch the clock, so I'm unsure how long it took for a server to approach us, but it was at least 5-7 minutes. We received our drinks at 7:04, which I know because I checked my phone while trying to order appetizers before Happy Hour ended, but that plan failed. When our friends arrived, they ordered drinks, which we finished before leaving. The server was friendly, which wasn't the issue; the real problem was the severe understaffing. There seemed to be only two bartenders and not enough servers. The server apologized for the wait, attributing it to the end-of-Happy Hour rush, a clear indication that more staff was needed. I've never experienced this at any other bar. They should hire more staff. We skipped ordering food because we were concerned about time, not the lack of Happy Hour pricing. I believe they should have honored Happy Hour prices since we were ready to order around 7:50/7:55. We ended up eating at the Improv instead. Bar Louie missed out on a lot of my business due to their staffing issues, and I won’t be returning to this Pittsburgh location, nor will my local friends.

text: We stopped here to enjoy a drink before our movie. Our server, was friendly. My husband had a margarita and my server suggested a berry mojito to me.\nBoth drinks were overly sweet and barely any alcohol.  After I finished my drink, I used my fork to dig for the blackberries at the bottom only to find (to my disgust), a decent sized piece of clear saran wrap! Ugg! Did they muddle the mint into the saran wrap?!!! She comped the drink but we won't be back.\nCan't speak about the food since we didn't have any but atmosphere was loud and it was difficult to have a conversation without repeating yourself.
paraphrase: We decided to stop here for a drink before our movie. Our server was pleasant. My husband ordered a margarita, and the server recommended a berry mojito for me. Both drinks were excessively sweet and had little alcohol content. After finishing my drink, I used my fork to retrieve the blackberries at the bottom and, to my horror, discovered a sizable piece of clear saran wrap! Ugh! Did they mix the mint with the saran wrap?!!! She didn't charge us for the drink, but we won't return. I can't comment on the food since we didn't try any, but the atmosphere was noisy, making it hard to have a conversation without repeating ourselves.

text: I can't even review this place. We sat ourselves and waited for a waitress to take our order, and we waited and waited until we got tired and just left. And I have to be clear it was a Saturday afternoon, maybe like 10 tables with people at them and I saw 3 waitress walking around. I think it's probably a bad idea to ignore people that come into your restaurant, especially since burgatory just opened across the street.
paraphrase: I can't bring myself to review this establishment. We seated ourselves and waited endlessly for a waitress to take our order until we eventually got fed up and left. To clarify, it was a Saturday afternoon with about 10 occupied tables, and I noticed 3 waitresses roaming around. Ignoring customers who walk into your restaurant seems unwise, especially since Burgatory just opened across the street.
